gmw_00006011.name: SSA22a-C15
gmw_00006011.overview: SSA22a-C15 is a Lyman-break galaxy located near the photometric center of the Lyman-alpha blob LAB 8 [arXiv:2001.03699]. The galaxy's systemic redshift is confirmed via near-infrared detections of its [O III] emission lines, which are spatially offset from the peak Lyman-alpha emission of LAB 8 [arXiv:2001.03699].

sources.summary: The paper locates SSA22a-C15 near LAB 8, gives its literature coordinates, confirms its [O III] redshift, and notes that LAB 8 Ly emission is offset from it.

quotes.quote: Two of the Lyman break galaxies from are within the combined LAB 1 / LAB 8 structure. Adopting the nomenclature of , these are SSA22a-C11 and SSA22a-C15. We list the coordinates of both galaxies in Table tab:sources We note the coordinates for these Lyman break galaxies provided in - RA = 22 ^ h 17 ^ m 25.67 ^ s /Dec = +00 ^ 12 35.2 for SSA22a-C11, and RA = 22 ^ h 17 ^ m 26.127 ^ s /Dec = +00 ^ 12 55.3 for SSA22a-C15 -- appear to be offset by 1 to the north-west with respect to where these galaxies are located in our data.

SSA22a-C11 is located in the south-west of LAB 1, while SSA22a-C15 is found close to the Ly photometric centre of LAB 8. Both galaxies have their redshift confirmed via near-IR detections of their [ O iii ] 4963,5007 lines McLinden2013 . Interestingly, the systemic redshift determined from [ O iii ] does not differ from the Ly emission line.
As a notable feature we find that the peak in the north corresponding to LAB 8 is spatially offset from the in LAB 8 embedded galaxy SSA22a-C15.

gmw_00006012.name: GLASS 10021
gmw_00006012.overview: GLASS 10021 is a gravitationally lensed galaxy located at a redshift of z=7.29 [arXiv:2306.07940]. The galaxy features two unresolved emission components in its [O III] line image, which may indicate a rotating stellar disk rather than a lensing effect [arXiv:2306.07940].

sources.summary: The paper describes GLASS 10021 as a lensed z=7.29 galaxy with two unresolved [O III] emission components, likely reflecting rotation rather than lensing.

quotes.quote: GLASS 10021 is a lensed galaxy at z=7.29 with a lensing factor of =1.72 . From Figure fig:z6o_img , we tentatively observe two components in the emission line image, although these two components are unresolved. The peak positions of the two components are separated by 0.''26 . Because such a multi-component feature is not observed in the stellar continuum, it is unlikely that the multi-component [ Oiii ] emission is due to lensing effect.

One of the objects, GLASS 10021, has a FWHM _ b that is almost identical to FWHM _ n with two components separated by 76 km s ^ 1 . This objects, mentioned previously in Section subsec:offset_multi , also has two components in the [ O iii ] emission line image. It is possible that we are observing the rotation disk of this object.

gmw_00006013.name: NGC 4713
gmw_00006013.overview: NGC 4713 is a face-on, bulgeless Sd spiral galaxy [arXiv:1010.2497, arXiv:1304.1279] located approximately 17.9 Mpc away [arXiv:0801.4382]. It features a weak active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0801.4382] and a large H I envelope with a disturbed, warped outer gas component [arXiv:1010.2497, arXiv:1304.1279].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4713 as a bulgeless disk galaxy with separate main and outer H I kinematic components, whose misaligned outer component may reflect a warp or recent interaction.

quotes.quote: Three of our objects have two kinematic components: NGC 4561, NGC 4713, and NGC 6509. The main component covers most of or more than the optical disk and the outer component is characterized by low surface brightness emission in the outer disk. We carried out the velocity field modeling separately for the two components. Table tab:final_params quotes only the kinematic parameters determined for the main component, as it corresponds to the optical disk.

The left panel of the top, middle, and bottom rows in Figure fig:outer_disk shows the main and outer components in the first-moment map of NGC 4561, NGC 4713, and NGC 6509, respectively. The position angle of the major axis of the outer component differs by between about 30 and 90 from that of the main component.

Although much of the emission in the first-moment maps is of low significance, the position-velocity diagrams show that there is significant emission in the outer-disk components. These outer components are likely due to a warp, which may indicate that these galaxies have undergone a recent interaction.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4713 as an Sd transition-object galaxy with a very soft nuclear X-ray source, resolved nuclear cluster, and data compatible with a weak AGN.

quotes.quote: NGC 4713 This galaxy is of type Sd, at a distance of 17.9 Mpc t88 . Its nucleus was classified as T2 (transition object with type 2 spectrum) by hfs97-3 . The nucleus is clearly detected by , but with only ten counts. It is a very soft source, with nine of the ten counts below 2.5 keV.

The data are consistent with the AGN hypothesis: the ``transition object'' classification by hfs97-3 implies there may be an AGN component in the optical spectrum; the nucleus is without doubt an x-ray source, though it is not possible to distinguish between emission from circumnuclear gas and the nucleus proper in the current \ imaging. The hardness ratio (with large uncertainty) is consistent with a = 2 power-law. NGC 4713 may be similar to NGC 3184 in having a low-luminosity AGN inside a nuclear star cluster.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4713 as a face-on spiral with a large H I envelope, disturbed warped gas, symmetric radio disk, and symmetric 6 cm polarized spiral magnetic field.

quotes.quote: NGC 4713 As NGC 4532, the face-on spiral galaxy NGC 4713 hosts a large H i envelope (Chung et al. 2009). It also shares many H i properties with NGC 4808, one of its nearest large neighbors in the southern outskirts of the cluster. The H i velocity field suggests a disturbed warp for the gas beyond the stellar disk (Chung et al. 2009). Its radio continuum disk is symmetric at 20 and 6 cm with a normal spectral index. Polarized emission at 20 cm is only detected in the southern part of the galactic disk.

gmw_00006014.name: G1
gmw_00006014.overview: The galaxy G1 is a primary lensing galaxy located close to image B of the lensed system SDSS J1001+5027 [arXiv:1306.5105]. Its light distribution can be modeled using either a regularized pixel grid or an elliptical Sersic profile [arXiv:1306.5105].

sources.summary: The paper identifies G1 as the primary lensing galaxy near image B and models its light to control contamination in the SDSS J1001 + 5027 time-delay photometry.

quotes.quote: However, image B lies close to the primary lensing galaxy G1. Minimizing the additive contamination by G1 to the flux measurements of B therefore requires a model for the light distribution of G1. In Fig. figure:dec , we show two different ways of modeling these galaxies. Our standard approach, shown in the bottom panels, consists in representing all extended objects, such as the lens galaxies, by a regularized pixel grid. The values of these pixels get iteratively updated during the deconvolution photometry procedure.

To explore the sensitivity of our results to a possible bias of this kind, we have adopted an alternative approach of representing G1 and G2 by two simply parametrized elliptical Sersic profiles, as shown in the top row of Fig. figure:dec .

gmw_00006015.name: 2MASS J10261389+5237510
gmw_00006015.overview: 2MASS J10261389+5237510 is an active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z=0.259 [arXiv:1910.02412]. The system hosts a supermassive black hole with a measured mass of approximately 3.65 × 10^7 solar masses [arXiv:1910.02412].

sources.summary: The paper selects 2MASS J10261389+5237510 as a monitored AGN, reports prior luminosity, line width, black-hole mass and Eddington ratio, then measures its H-beta lag and mass.

quotes.quote: We summarize the main properties of the two AGNs. (I) 2MASS J10261389+5237510 at z=0.259 with the B = 17.96 2010A&A...518A..10V . Using the SDSS DR7 spectra, 2011ApJS..194...45S reported the monochromatic luminosity at 5100 \ L_ 5100 = 44.35 0.01 erg s^ 1 , H emission line FWHM = 3175 91 km s^ 1 , weak Fe II emission (with a Fe II/H ratio R_ Fe II 0.03 ), M_ BH = 8.09 0.02 M_ and Eddington ratio ( _ EDD ) = 0.86 for the source.

Based on cross-correlation analysis using B band and H emission line light curves, we measured the rest-frame lag of 33.2^ +3.9 _ 4.7 and 42.6^ +14.3 _ 11.9 days, respectively for 2MASS J1026 and SDSS J1619. Using the _ line of the total H line profile in the rms spectrum and a virial factor of f=4.47 , we determined black hole mass as 3.65^ +0.49 _ 0.57 10^7 M_ ( 23.02^ +7.81 _ 6.56 10^7 M_ ) for 2MASS J1026 (SDSS J1619).

gmw_00006016.name: J0336+3218
gmw_00006016.overview: J0336+3218 is an active galactic nucleus radio source featuring an optically thick core and an optically thin jet extending southwestward [arXiv:1006.5151]. This jet exhibits a monotonic transverse rotation-measure gradient, which is consistent with a helical magnetic field [arXiv:1410.6653].

sources.summary: The paper confirms 0333+321 has jet EVPAs and monotonic transverse rotation-measure gradients consistent with a helical magnetic field across the jet and core.

quotes.quote: 0333+321 VLBA observations of 0333+321 at 8 frequencies near 4 and 8 GHz obtained in 2003 are presented by Asada et al. (2008), who found the jet EVPAs to be almost perpendicular to the jet direction and to follow the bending of the jet. Thus, they inferred the magnetic field to run roughly parallel to the jet direction. Asada et al. (2008) also reported a clear gradient in the RM distribution across the jet, which they interpreted as evidence for a helical jet magnetic field, particularly since the gradient encompassed both positive and negative values.

Our RM map confirms the presence of a clear RM gradient transverse to the jet direction along the entire jet and core region, as was reported by Asada et al. (2008). Figure fig:0333RM also shows two slices taken roughly perpendicular to the jet direction plotted together with their errors. The transverse RM gradients are all in the same direction and are monotonic. The significance of the gradients is approximately 4 .

sources.summary: The paper describes J0336+3218 as an AGN radio source whose southwest jet and core are mapped with corrected two-frequency synthesis and a physically consistent spectral-index map.

quotes.quote: (2) J0336+3218. Figure 5 presents both the single-frequency images of the radio source (upper panel) obtained separately at 2.3 and 8.6GHz and the maps of the intensity and spectral-index distributions over the source obtained through two-frequency synthesis at a reference frequency of 5.5 GHz (lower panel). The source consists of an optically thick core and an optically thin jet extending roughly southwestward of the core. The jet extent reaches about 27 and 10 mas at the low and high frequencies, respectively.

As a result of the two-frequency synthesis with allowance made for the shift found, we managed to obtain the proper maps of the intensity and spectral-index distributions over the source. As we see from Fig. 5c, we managed to reconstruct a more extended jet structure (up to 15 mas) than in the case of using only the high-frequency data, with the synthesized map having the angular resolution corresponding to the high-frequency data.

gmw_00006017.name: J0724+4159
gmw_00006017.overview: J0724+4159 is a low-ionization broad absorption line quasar [arXiv:0808.2105]. The radio source associated with this quasar is unresolved and exhibits a flat radio spectrum [arXiv:0808.2105].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0724+4159 as a LoBAL quasar with unresolved compact radio emission, flat radio spectrum, matched EVN/MERLIN fluxes, and high core polarization.

quotes.quote: J0724+4159 is a LoBAL quasar. The radio source associated with this quasar is unresolved in the VLA observation at 20 cm. Its flux density is 7.9 mJy in FIRST. J0724+4159 has a spectral index between 20 cm and 3.6 cm of = 0.0 . The radio loudness is logR=1.6 (Becker et al. 2000). The naturally weighted image with the EVN (Fig.1(a)) still shows an unresolved structure at 18 cm, with a total flux density of 7.2 mJy.

gmw_00006018.name: J0728+4026
gmw_00006018.overview: J0728+4026 is a radio-loud, low-ionization broad absorption line quasar [arXiv:0808.2105] that exhibits a steep radio spectrum and a core-jet structure [arXiv:0808.2105]. Over a rest-frame span of several years, its strong magnesium and iron absorption troughs weakened and eventually disappeared completely [arXiv:1501.00091].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J0728+4026 as a LoBAL quasar with a steep radio spectrum and a polarized EVN core-jet structure containing most of the MERLIN flux.

quotes.quote: J0728+4026 was discovered as a LoBAL quasar. It is a point source with 18 mJy with the VLA at 18 cm (A-configuration). These non-simultaneous data suggest a steep spectral index = -1.1 . Its radio loudness is logR=0.37 (Becker et al. 2000). The naturally weighted image with the EVN (Fig.1(b)) shows a core-jet structure.

sources.summary: The paper describes FBQS J072831.6+402615 as an OFeLoBAL quasar whose Mg and Fe absorption weakened dramatically and then disappeared, with modeling placing its outflow within an uncertain 447 pc upper limit.

quotes.quote: For FBQS J072831.6+402615, its spectrum from the FBQS epoch contains strong absorption lines from Mg 2 and Fe 2 multiplets, with a blueshifted velocity of 16,000 . In the OMR spectrum acquired 6.85 rest-frame years later, the absorption troughs of Mg 2 and Fe 2 UV1 and UV2+3 had decreased in absorption depth by a factor of four. After an additional 2.88 rest-frame years, the residual overlapping troughs had completely disappeared in the YFOS spectrum.

Based on the absorption depths of Fe 2 UV1 and UV2+3 and possibly Fe 2 UV62, we obtain a model spectrum using U = 10^ 3 , n _e = 10^6  cm^ 3 and N _H = 10^ 22   cm^ 2 to match the difference spectrum of FBQS J072831.6+402615; the distance of the outflow winds is then found to be 447 pc.

gmw_00006019.name: J1122+3124
gmw_00006019.overview: J1122+3124 is a radio-loud, low-ionization broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:0808.2105]. The source features a dominant central radio core with a two-sided, kiloparsec-scale structure [arXiv:0808.2105] that exhibits a faint elongation [arXiv:2106.02646].

sources.summary: The paper highlights J1122+3124 as the only observed steep-spectrum BAL quasar with a two-sided kiloparsec-scale radio structure and a dominant central component.

quotes.quote: J1122+3124 is a LoBAL quasar. The spectral index between 20 cm and 3.6 cm obtained by the VLA in D-configuration is = -0.6 . The estimated radio loudness is logR=1.52 (Becker et al. 2000). The naturally weighted image of the source with the EVN (Fig.1(d)) shows a central component with two-sided structure at 18 cm, which is nearly aligned with the central component. The southern component is located 150 mas away from the central component, while a weak northern component lies 87 mas away.

sources.summary: 1122+3124 is one of the visually extended rQSOs; its dominant radio core has faint elongation and fit residuals, requiring two Gaussian components.

quotes.quote: Five of our targets exhibit visually extended emission, both from an examination of the central images (Figure images ) and based on their single-component Gaussian fits: 0946+2548, 1007+2853, 1122+3124, 1153+5651, and 1535+2434. All five are rQSOs.

1122+3124 The source consists of a single dominant core component that shows a faint elongation along PA =205^ . A single component fit leaves a definite visible residual along the direction of the elongation. Therefore, two components are required to fit the source adequately.

gmw_00006020.name: J1150+2819
gmw_00006020.overview: J1150+2819 is a high-ionization broad absorption line quasar [arXiv:0808.2105]. It is a radio-loud source that exhibits unresolved structure and steep-spectrum radio emission [arXiv:0808.2105].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1150+2819 as a HiBAL quasar with steep-spectrum unresolved VLA emission, unresolved EVN/MERLIN structure, and central-component polarization.

quotes.quote: J1150+2819 is a HiBAL quasar. The VLA observation at 20 cm shows an unresolved image with a flux density of about 14.2 mJy. The spectral index between 20 cm and 3.6 cm is = -1.2 . The estimated radio loudness is logR=1.43 (Becker et al. 2000). The naturally weighted image with the EVN (Fig.3(a)) still displays unresolved structure at 18 cm.

gmw_00006021.name: J1413+4212
gmw_00006021.overview: J1413+4212 is a high-ionization broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:0808.2105] at a redshift of z=2.8130 [arXiv:1806.02016]. It exhibits a flat radio spectrum with a compact core-jet structure [arXiv:0808.2105] and contains two narrow absorption line systems [arXiv:1806.02016].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1413+4212 as a HiBAL quasar with flat radio spectrum, compact EVN core-jet morphology, MERLIN-consistent flux, and polarized central emission.

quotes.quote: J1413+4212 is a HiBAL quasar. It is a point source with 18.7 mJy in the FIRST catalog. The flux density measured with the VLA at 3.6 cm in the D-configuration is 11.3 mJy. This gives a flat spectral index = -0.2 . The naturally weighted image with the EVN (Fig.3(b)) displays a short core-jet structure at 18 cm with 17.1 mJy.

sources.summary: This z=2.8130 quasar has two narrow absorption line systems, with the 34521 km/s system significantly enhanced between SDSS observations.

quotes.quote: Quasar SDSS J141334.38+421201.7 with z_ em =2.8130 . It was spectroscopically observed by the SDSS on MJD = 52823 , 56093 and 57519. In the quasar spectra, we detect 2 \ NALs with z_ abs =2.3968 and 2.8070, and _ =34 521 and 472 \ from the quasar system. The system with _ =34 521 was significantly enhanced by W_r^ 1548 =3.2 _ W_r^ 1548 during MJD=56093 and 57519.

gmw_00006022.name: J1655+3945
gmw_00006022.overview: J1655+3945 is a high-ionization broad absorption line quasar [arXiv:0808.2105]. While unresolved in VLA observations, high-resolution EVN imaging reveals a resolved radio structure that is elongated toward the southwest [arXiv:0808.2105].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1655+3945 as a HiBAL quasar with unresolved VLA morphology, flat radio spectrum, an EVN structure elongated southwest, and polarized central emission.

quotes.quote: J1655+3945 is a HiBAL quasar. The VLA observation at 20 cm shows an unresolved image. The flux density of J1655+3945 in the FIRST catalog is 10.2 mJy. The spectral index between 20 cm and 3.6 cm is = -0.2 . The naturally weighted EVN image depicts a resolved structure elongated to the south west with a total of 12.1 mJy (Fig.3(d)).

gmw_00006023.name: J0839+3900
gmw_00006023.overview: J0839+3900 is a low-ionization broad absorption line quasar located at a redshift of z=6.905 [arXiv:1810.11926]. It exhibits strong blueshifted absorption [arXiv:1810.11926], but remains undetected in both [C II] emission and far-infrared dust continuum observations [arXiv:2404.15413].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0839+3900 as a z=6.905 BAL quasar with strong blueshifted absorption and a Gemini/GNIRS spectrum identifying it as the highest-redshift known LoBAL quasar.

quotes.quote: J0839+3900 is a broad absorption line (BAL) quasar at z=6.905 with strong blue-shifted \ absorption. We obtained deep Gemini/GNIRS spectrum which shows that J0839+3900 is a Low-ionization BAL (LoBAL) quasar with strong blue shifted \ absorptions. It is the highest redshift known LoBAL quasar. The NIR spectrum and related physical parameter measurements will be reported in the future together with NIR spectra of other z>6.5 quasars in a subsequent paper (Yang, J. in preparation ).

sources.summary: The paper reports J0839+3900 as one of the [ C 2 ] undetected NOEMA quasars, observed in shallower data and lacking a FIR continuum detection.

quotes.quote: Out of 31 targeted objects, only four quasars (J0829+4117, J0839+3900, J1129+1846, and J1216+4519) are undetected in [ C 2 ] and two quasars have marginal line detections at a 2.6 level (J0213 0626) and a 2.1 level (J0837+4929), respectively. Three (J0829+4117, J0839+3900, and J1216+4519) out of the four [ C 2 ] undetected quasars are from the NOEMA observations, which have a depth that is two times shallower than that of the ALMA observations. The FIR continuum flux and flux uncertainty measured from the 1D spectral fitting are also listed in Table tbl:c2fit . All objects observed with ALMA, except for J1129+1846, are detected in the dust continuum at high significance. Similar to the [ C 2 ] results, the FIR continua of J0829+4117, J0839+3900, and J1216+4519 are not detected by NOEMA.

gmw_00006024.name: J0910--0414
gmw_00006024.overview: J0910--0414 is a broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:1810.11926] located at a redshift of z=6.63 [arXiv:1810.11926]. Its host galaxy is a rotation-dominated system with a clear velocity gradient [arXiv:2404.15413], and it represents an exception to the regional trend of overmassive supermassive black holes [arXiv:2404.15413].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0910--0414 as a BAL quasar first identified near z=6.8, then revised with deeper GMOS spectroscopy to z=6.63 with strong blueward absorption.

quotes.quote: J0910--0414 is a BAL quasar. It was initially identified as a z 6.8 quasar from our low SNR FIRE spectrum. However, after obtaining a deep GMOS optical spectrum, we found that it is a z=6.63 BAL quasar where most of the flux blueward of \ is absorbed.

sources.summary: The paper identifies J0910--0414 as a clear-velocity-gradient, rotation-dominated quasar host and the exception to the sample trend of overmassive SMBHs.

quotes.quote: Further more, two galaxies have v_ proj /2 _ int <0.4 but with clear velocity gradient (J0246--5219 and J0910--0414). We therefore conclude to classify J0038--1527, J0224--4711, J0229--0808, J0246--5219, J0439+1634, J0525--2406, J0706+2921, J0910--0414, J1058+2930, and J2002--3013 (43 ) as rotation-dominated galaxies (Fig. fig:mom_vel ).

Most of the quasars studied in this work, with the exception of J0910--0414, exhibit overmassive SMBHs when compared to local relations. The ratio M_ BH /M_ dyn is approximately three times higher than the local relation on average, spanning a broad range of 1.2--14.5.

gmw_00006025.name: J0706+2921
gmw_00006025.overview: J0706+2921 is a highly luminous quasar located at a high redshift of z > 6.5 [arXiv:1810.11926]. It has an absolute magnitude of -27.51, making it the most intrinsically luminous known quasar at this redshift [arXiv:1810.11926].

sources.summary: The paper presents J0706+2921 as the most luminous known z>6.5 quasar, discusses its extreme absolute magnitude, and uses it to frame early black-hole growth constraints.

quotes.quote: J0706+2921 is the most luminous z>6.5 quasar known to date (J0439+1634 reported in fan18 is brighter but has lower intrinsic luminosity after correcting for lensing magnification). It has an absolute magnitude of M_ 1450 =--27.51, and is about 0.3 magnitude brighter than the previous record holder venemans15 .

For J0706+2921, the brightest quasar in our sample, it takes 20 e folding times, or the age of the universe at z 6.6 , to grow from a 10 M_ stellar black hole, assuming the radiation efficiency =0.1. The existence of these luminous quasars helps determine whether standard models of radiatively efficient accretion from stellar seeds are still allowed, or alternative models of BH seed formation and BH accretion (super-Eddington or radiatively inefficient) are required e.g. volonteri06 .

gmw_00006026.name: J0910+1656
gmw_00006026.overview: J0910+1656 is a faint quasar located at a high redshift of z=6.72 [arXiv:1810.11926]. The object is characterized by strong Lyman-alpha emission, which enhances its observed brightness in the y and z bands despite being faint in the rest-frame 1450 Angstroms [arXiv:1810.11926].

sources.summary: The paper notes J0910+1656 as a faint z=6.72 quasar with low selection probability, selected because strong Ly emission boosts its observed y and z-band brightness.

quotes.quote: We note that there are two faint quasars have probabilities below 30 : J0910+1656 at z=6.72 and J1216+4519 at z=6.654 . In particular, J0910+1656 has a probability of only 10 . The reason we can select J0910+1656 is that this quasar has a strong Ly emission and thus is bright in both y_ ps1 and z_ DELS , although it is faint in rest-frame 1450 .

gmw_00006027.name: J1216+4519
gmw_00006027.overview: J1216+4519 is a faint quasar at a redshift of z=6.654 [arXiv:1810.11926]. It is undetected in both ionized carbon emission and far-infrared dust continuum observations [arXiv:2404.15413].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1216+4519 as a faint z=6.654 quasar with low selection probability but bright observed y and z-band flux relative to its M1450 value.

quotes.quote: We note that there are two faint quasars have probabilities below 30 : J0910+1656 at z=6.72 and J1216+4519 at z=6.654 . J1216+4519 is not that extreme but has similar situation with J0910+1656. J1216+4519 is bright in y_ ps1 and z_ DELS but faint in M_ 1450 .

sources.summary: The paper reports J1216+4519 as one of the [ C 2 ] undetected NOEMA quasars, observed in shallower data and lacking a FIR continuum detection.

quotes.quote: Out of 31 targeted objects, only four quasars (J0829+4117, J0839+3900, J1129+1846, and J1216+4519) are undetected in [ C 2 ] and two quasars have marginal line detections at a 2.6 level (J0213 0626) and a 2.1 level (J0837+4929), respectively. Three (J0829+4117, J0839+3900, and J1216+4519) out of the four [ C 2 ] undetected quasars are from the NOEMA observations, which have a depth that is two times shallower than that of the ALMA observations. The FIR continuum flux and flux uncertainty measured from the 1D spectral fitting are also listed in Table tbl:c2fit . All objects observed with ALMA, except for J1129+1846, are detected in the dust continuum at high significance. Similar to the [ C 2 ] results, the FIR continua of J0829+4117, J0839+3900, and J1216+4519 are not detected by NOEMA.

gmw_00006028.name: UGC 8313
gmw_00006028.overview: UGC 8313 is a small SBc-type spiral galaxy that exists as a companion to NGC 5055 [arXiv:2207.11923]. Diagnostic data place the galaxy entirely within the star-forming region, showing a correlation between velocity dispersion and emission line ratios [arXiv:2207.11923].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 8313 as a small SBc companion of NGC 5055 whose BPT data lie in the star-forming region with a velocity-dispersion correlation.

quotes.quote: UGC 8313 is a companion galaxy of NGC 5055 and a small SBc-type galaxy (Tully (1988) [ 10 ] .

For UGC 8313, as shown in Fig. 5, all of the points are located in the star formation region. There is a correlation between and for 25 km/s (Fig. 6). UGC 8313: There is a correlation between and for 25 km/s. It is interesting to locate these points on the map to comment.

gmw_00006029.name: UGC 8508
gmw_00006029.overview: UGC 8508 is an irregular dwarf galaxy that is a possible member of the M101 group [arXiv:2207.11923]. Its ionized gas disc contains an unclosed expanding shell spanning approximately 350 pc [arXiv:1203.6213], and its neutral hydrogen distribution features a 550 pc cavity [arXiv:1203.6213].

sources.summary: The paper finds UGC 8508 has an inclined I-sigma lane tracing a roughly 350 pc unclosed expanding shell, plus an HI cavity without clear ionized-gas expansion.

quotes.quote: UGC 8508 The observed distribution of is very similar to previous objects: a minimal velocity dispersion inside the HII regions surrounded by the diffuse gas with increased . In addition, the I- diagram reveals an inclined lane, which should correspond to a shell according to MunozTunon1996 . Indeed, most of the points of this lane (marked in Fig.  fig1_1 with red and green) are concentrated within a huge arch of HII regions on the edge of the western part of the disc of ionized gas. Morphologically and kinematically this resembles a half of an unclosed expanding shell with a diameter of approximately 350 pc. The kinematic age of this shell, we designated as UGC 8508 1, is about 4 Myr (Table  tab_shells ).

In a recent paper Warren2011 showed that the eastern half of the galaxy has a cavity in the HI distribution with a diameter of about 550 pc, so that the bright HII regions are located along its borders. However, this region (denoted as `Cavern' in our figure) is not distinguishable by the kinematics of ionized gas -- velocity dispersion is small, except for several spots with increased in the heart of this cavity, where almost no emission is present. They are difficult to be interpreted as a separate shell, most likely we are talking about an increase of turbulent velocities on the border of HII regions.

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 8508 as an irregular dwarf and possible M101-group member; its data are insufficient for a firm BPT relation and show no shock-dominated gas.

quotes.quote: UGC 8508 is a irregular dwarf galaxy and Kraan-Korteweg Tammann(1979) [ 11 ] listed it as a possible member of the M 101 group.

Figure 7 shows the results for UGC 8508. Our data is not enough to comment, but it seems that there is not BPT- relation in this galaxy (Fig. 8). UGC 8508: Although it seems that there is not a correlation between and , our data is not enough to accurately comment on this issue. We did not detect a gas significantly ionized by shocks.

gmw_00006030.name: CGRaBS J0809+5341
gmw_00006030.overview: CGRaBS J0809+5341 is a high-redshift flat-spectrum radio quasar and blazar [arXiv:1607.06533, arXiv:1502.05660]. The source features a compact radio core [arXiv:1607.06533, arXiv:1502.05660] and experienced a giant optical flare in April 2014 [arXiv:1607.06533, arXiv:1502.05660].

sources.summary: The paper studies CGRaBS J0809+5341 as a high-redshift flat-spectrum radio quasar whose extreme optical flare motivated EVN and KaVA VLBI monitoring of compact core variability.

quotes.quote: CGRaBS J0809+5341 (J0809+5341, hereafter) is a flat-spectrum radio quasar a blazar; Massaro09 at high redshift, z = 2.133 Healey08 . Recently it showed a bright flare in unfiltered optical observations Shumkov14,Balanutsa14 . The observations were made with the MASTER-Tunka auto-detection system on the nights of 2014 April 13 and April 19. The absolute magnitude of the flaring source was extremely high, M=-30.0 on April 13 and M=-30.5 on April 19, making it (during the short flaring period) possibly the brightest among all known quasars.

The VLBI structure Figure fig:evn shows the 5-GHz EVN image of J0809+5341, characterized by a single compact component, apparently the radio ``core''. It is conventionally interpreted as the inner section of the synchrotron-emitting jet that becomes optically thick at the given observing frequency Blandford79 . This image resulted from fringe-fitting to the J0809+5341 data. The position of the emission peak in the phase-referenced image (not reproduced here) is right ascension 08^ h 09^ m 41 7330 and declination +53 41 25 093 (J2000), which is in good agreement with that derived from the analysis of the data from the VLBI calibrator database Data from http://astrogeo.org maintained by L. Petrov, solution rfc 2014d .

sources.summary: The paper studies CGRaBS J0809+5341 as a high-redshift FSRQ/blazar whose giant optical flare triggered multiwavelength observations and leptonic SED modeling.

quotes.quote: CGRaBS J0809+5341 (hereafter J0809+5341) is a high redshift FSRQ z = 2.144 0.002 , 2014A&A...563A..54P which was overlooked for a long time due to its faintness and/or prolonged quiescence. It was predicted as a candidate ray emitter by 2008ApJS..175...97H , but was not detected by any earlier ray surveys. It is radio bright F_ 8.4 GHz = 183.4 mJy, 2007ApJS..171...61H , having a flat radio spectrum and exhibits compact core morphology in the Faint Images of the Radio Sky at Twenty centimeters (FIRST) observations.

J0809+5341 underwent a giant optical outburst on 2014 April 19 2014ATel.6096....1B , when its unfiltered apparent magnitude reached 15.7 mag (see Figure fig:optical_flare ). This corresponds to an absolute magnitude of 30.5 mag, thereby making it one of the brightest quasars in the Universe. Compared to the archival Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) observations, the source was 5 mag brighter on 2014 April 19.

gmw_00006031.name: NGC 3309
gmw_00006031.overview: NGC 3309 is a giant elliptical galaxy located in the core of the Hydra I cluster [arXiv:1101.3786, arXiv:1205.5289]. It is a companion to the central galaxy NGC 3311, showing no direct signs of interaction [arXiv:1809.01163, arXiv:2303.00044] and lacking a central dust lane [arXiv:1205.5289].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3309 as a regular giant elliptical in Hydra I with a compact X-ray component, depleted gas, and possible ram-pressure stripping near NGC 3311.

quotes.quote: The Hydra I cluster (Abell 1060) is an X-ray bright, non-cooling flow, medium compact cluster in the southern hemisphere, whose central region is dominated by a pair of non-interacting giant elliptical galaxies, NGC 3311 and NGC 3309. NGC 3309 is a regular giant elliptical (E3) and NGC 3311 is a cD galaxy with an extended halo vasterberg91 . X-ray properties of Hydra I Except for two peaks associated with the bright elliptical galaxies NGC 3311 and NGC 3309, the X-ray emission from the hot intracluster medium (ICM) in the Hydra I (A 1060) cluster is smooth and lacks prominent spatial substructures.

The emission components of NGC 3311 and NGC 3309 themselves are small, extending to only 10" 2.5 kpc, suggesting that both galaxies lost most of their gas in earlier interactions with the ICM. In both galaxies, the X-ray gas is hotter than the equivalent temperature corresponding to the central stellar velocity dispersions, and in approximate pressure equilibrium with the ICM Yamasaki02 .

The second possibility is that, similarly as for NGC 3311, also the PNs in NGC 3309 may be severely ram pressure stripped by the galaxy's motion through the dense ICM in the cluster core. This would require that NGC 3309 moves rapidly through the cluster core, and is physically rather close to NGC 3311.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3309 as a giant elliptical near NGC 3311, with low-dispersion light, higher radial velocity, and possible stripped outer material contributing to the cD halo.

quotes.quote: The same is true for the directions East and South-East, although the opposite directions are dominated by the low-velocity dispersion light of NGC 3309 (see orange circles in the right panels of Figures fig:velsig_profiles and fig:velsig_pa ). The high velocity dispersion east of HCC 007 is probably caused by the superposition of the galaxy's stellar population at high radial velocity (see Fig. fig:hcc007_maps ) with that of NGC 3311's stellar halo at lower radial velocities. Low velocity dispersion regions are those around the giant elliptical NGC 3309 ( 200 km s ^ 1 ) and HCC 007 ( 80 km s ^ 1 ), which dominate the light in those slits.

It is also mostly negative around NGC 3309 (see left second panel from top in Fig. fig:h3h4_profiles , negative spatial axis). That means that the LOSVD has an extended tail towards lower velocities. In the case of NGC 3309 this is probably due to a contribution of NGC 3311's halo light to the dominating stellar populations of NGC 3309 itself, which has a higher radial LOS velocity than NGC 3311.

NGC 3311 has the close pair NGC 3309. Although no direct interaction signs are seen in our data, it is plausible that some outer material of NGC 3309 has already been stripped off in the cluster potential and might contribute to the central cD halo in the future.

sources.summary: NGC3309 is described as a massive elliptical companion of NGC3311, with no interaction signs, an R^1/4 profile, a negative dispersion gradient, and measured stellar populations, abundances, and IMF profile.

quotes.quote: The brightest companion of NGC3311 is NGC3309, a massive elliptical galaxy with a line-of-sight velocity offset of 250 km/s from the BCG, and with a separation on the sky of only 100 , but with no signs of interaction with the BCG arnaboldi12 . Although close to each other in the cluster core, the two giant ellipticals have different surface brightness profiles, with NGC3309 characterized by a typical R ^ 1/4 profile, and NGC3311 by multiple components arnaboldi12 . Also their velocity dispersion profiles are remarkable different: while the central 5 kpc region of NGC3309 has a symmetric negative gradient, typical of ellipticals, NGC3311 has a peculiar inverse positive gradient. This is an important sign that suggests that the two objects have had a different formation and evolutionary history, opening the question of what has been the driver of such differences.

In this work we further investigate the stellar population radial profile of the two main elliptical galaxies of the Hydra I cluster, NGC3311 and NGC3309, adding for the first time details on the chemical content with the retrieval of many elemental abundances necessary to retrieve their IMF radial profile with good precision. We derive relations among stellar properties, retrieved with the same data set, analysis and models, in order to isolate possible driver(s) of the first phase of formation of NGC3311 and NGC3309.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3309 as a nearby giant elliptical whose globular-cluster contamination is small, with low specific frequency suggesting cluster loss to NGC 3311 and a foreground location.

quotes.quote: One might suspect a certain level of contamination of the cluster system of NGC 3311 by clusters from the nearby giant elliptical NGC 3309, which has a projected distance of only 100 from NGC 3311. Already harris83 and mclaughlin95 estimated this contamination globally to be small considering the huge cluster system of NGC 3311. wehner08 quote 16500 2000 as the total number of NGC 3311 clusters in comparison to only 374 210 in the case of NGC 3309 which means an abnormally low specific frequency of about 0.9 for NGC 3309. This suggests that NGC 3309 might have lost part of its original cluster system to NGC 3311.

These observations support the assumption that NGC 3311 is at the center of the cluster potential and NGC 3309 spatially at a larger distance from NGC 3311 than the projected one. Yamasaki et al. also point out the good isothermality (the XMM-Newton temperature slightly decreases) and apparent equilibrium of the X-ray gas in NGC 3311. Signs of an ongoing merger are at least not noticable. This is in line with the relative distance between NGC 3309 and NGC 3311 given by mieske05 using surface brightness fluctuations. NGC 3309 appears to be somewhat in the foreground.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3309 as a giant elliptical in the Hydra I core, modeling its photometry and noting its R1/4 profile and lack of a central dust lane.

quotes.quote: The core of the Hydra I cluster is dominated by two giant elliptical galaxies, NGC 3311 and NGC 3309. Early CCD surface photometry showed that both NGC 3311 and NGC 3309 are fitted by an R ^ 1/4 law within 30'' distance from their centers vast91 . On the basis of the large R_e = 98'' value from the R ^ 1/4 fit, classified NGC 3311 as a cD galaxy. In the center of this galaxy, a complex dust lane is found, and the observed surface brightness profile thus falls below the best fit R ^ 1/4 profile. Both the dust lane and the core in the central profile are confirmed by HST WFPC2 imaging laine+03 . NGC 3309 does not have a dust lane in its core vast91 . published VLT/FORS1 images of the Hydra cluster in the V-band, that were used by to derive the average radial surface brightness profile for NGC 3311 down to _V= 26.5 mag arcsec ^ 2 . They classified NGC 3311 as a cD galaxy because of the low central surface brightness and extended radial profile. In the present work we derive accurate quantitative photometry for the galaxy NGC 3311, NGC 3309 and the diffuse light in the Hydra I core.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3309 as a large Hydra I companion whose light must be modeled but which shows no strong interaction with NGC 3311.

quotes.quote: The large companion NGC 3309 is also bound to the Hydra I cluster, with a central systemic velocity of V=4099 27 2008A&A...486..697M . However, NGC 3309 does not seem to be interacting with NGC 3311, as indicated by the lack of distortions in the photometric profiles of both galaxies, and contributes to the surface brightness profile of NGC 3311 only at distances larger than 20 kpc see also Section sec:sercomponents 2012A&A...545A..37A .

We used the galfitm software 2013MNRAS.435..623V , an updated version of galfit 2002AJ....124..266P , to perform a parametric structural decomposition of the three main galaxies in the core of Hydra I, including the cD galaxy NGC 3311, the large elliptical NGC 3309 situated Northwest of NGC 3311 in Fig. fig:strategy , and the S0 galaxy HCC 007 to the South of NGC 3311. We provide further details of the fitting method in what follows.

gmw_00006032.name: UGC731
gmw_00006032.overview: UGC 731 is classified as an Sd spiral galaxy [arXiv:1112.2548] and a dwarf irregular galaxy [arXiv:2311.03876]. It features small emission knots distributed asymmetrically over its disk [arXiv:1112.2548], along with lopsided kinematics [arXiv:0901.4222] and an asymmetric, disturbed neutral hydrogen disk [arXiv:2311.03876].

sources.summary: The paper says 731's H I and H-alpha data mostly agree, while its lopsided kinematics explain the steeper de Blok and Bosma rotation curve.

quotes.quote: 731 . Observed in \ both by SMvdBB and dBB. The agreement between the \ data and the SMVdBB data is good, whereas the \ rotation curve by de dBB rises more steeply than the \ rotation curve. The difference between these two \ curves can be explained by the lopsided kinematics of this galaxy (see Swaters et al. \ 1999). Its approaching side has a more steeply rising rotation curve than the receding side. The \ data in SMvdBB samples both sides, whereas in the dBB data, which is less deep, \ emission is biased to the approaching side.

sources.summary: The paper finds UGC731 has fits close to Newtonian gravity and notes disturbed asymmetric H I disks as a possible reason for the smaller fractional index.

quotes.quote: Specifically, we consider the 8 galaxies in SPARC that are classified as dwIrr and are flagged as having high quality data on the rotation curve: D631-7, DDO64, DDO161, UGC731, UGC5005, UGC5414, UGC7608, NGC3741. The latter is the galaxy with the best dataset, and actually constitutes the object with the most extended rotation curve (relative to the half-light radius) measured to date.

In the other cases, namely UGC731 and UGC7608, s is close to 1 , the estimates of the fitting parameters in FG and in the Newtonian setting are consistent to within 1 , and the overall quality of the fits are comparable. We have looked for some property of these two galaxies that could correlate with their smaller values of s , but were unable to reach a definite conclusion. Maybe an interesting evidence comes from the kinematics of their HI disks, which appear asymmetric and disturbed by a past or ongoing gravitational interaction ; this could possibly alters the shape of the outer rotation curve and originate a variant outcome when modeling it in the FG framework.

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC 731 as an Sd galaxy with a velocity-based distance estimate, asymmetric emission knots across its disk, and previously obtained photometry and velocity-field data.

quotes.quote: UGC 731 = DDO 9. This is an Sd galaxy, the distance to which has been estimated from the radial velocity with a Hubble constant H_0 = 72 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 . Small emission knots are distributed asymmetrically over the galactic disk. Detailed R photometry and the velocity field for this galaxy have been obtained by Swaters [ 16 ] .

gmw_00006033.name: UGC7608
gmw_00006033.overview: UGC7608 is a dwarf irregular galaxy [arXiv:2311.03876] characterized by bright ultraviolet sources in its northwestern region [arXiv:2105.13394]. Its neutral hydrogen disk appears asymmetric and disturbed, which may indicate a past or ongoing gravitational interaction [arXiv:2311.03876].

sources.summary: The paper finds UGC7608 has fits close to Newtonian gravity and notes disturbed asymmetric H I disks as a possible reason for the smaller fractional index.

quotes.quote: Specifically, we consider the 8 galaxies in SPARC that are classified as dwIrr and are flagged as having high quality data on the rotation curve: D631-7, DDO64, DDO161, UGC731, UGC5005, UGC5414, UGC7608, NGC3741. The latter is the galaxy with the best dataset, and actually constitutes the object with the most extended rotation curve (relative to the half-light radius) measured to date.

In the other cases, namely UGC731 and UGC7608, s is close to 1 , the estimates of the fitting parameters in FG and in the Newtonian setting are consistent to within 1 , and the overall quality of the fits are comparable. We have looked for some property of these two galaxies that could correlate with their smaller values of s , but were unable to reach a definite conclusion. Maybe an interesting evidence comes from the kinematics of their HI disks, which appear asymmetric and disturbed by a past or ongoing gravitational interaction ; this could possibly alters the shape of the outer rotation curve and originate a variant outcome when modeling it in the FG framework.

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 07608 as an irregular galaxy with bright northwest UV sources, bumpy azimuthal photometry, and blue colors tied to high burst fractions.

quotes.quote: UGC 07608 UGC 07608 (DDO 129) is an irregular galaxy with bright UV sources in the north west region of the galaxy (upper right panel of Figure fig:ugc07608_4panel ). Its irregular morphology made UGC 07608's azimuthally averaged photometry appear bumpy, seen in Figure fig:ugc07608_fulldata and previously seen with the photometry in patterson1996 . However, the coarser placement of ellipses for the SED fitting analysis makes the knots and clumps of H and UV emission become less apparent (Figure fig:ugc07608_sfh ). The high EW(H ) coupled with the rather blue colors seen in UGC 07608 are correlated with the high f_ burst and low t_ ySP in the cumulative SFHs in all annuli.

gmw_00006034.name: J0831+3646
gmw_00006034.overview: J0831+3646 is a changing-look active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1711.08122]. Between 2002 and 2015, the galaxy transitioned into a bright state with the emergence of broad Balmer emission lines and exhibited photometric variability [arXiv:1711.08122].

sources.summary: The paper presents J0831+3646 as a new turn-on changing-look AGN whose broad Balmer lines emerged between SDSS and LAMOST spectra and whose photometry varies.

quotes.quote: In Figure fig:cl_example , the spectra separating for 13 years show that there was no H emission in 2002 in the SDSS (black), while the broad H , as well as broad H , emission lines emerged in the spectra taken in 2015 in the LAMOST (blue). The residual spectrum (gray spectrum at the bottom of the right panel), between the bright epoch and faint epoch spectra, distinctly shows the emerging of broad H and H . The left panel in Figure fig:cl_example shows the photometric data from several surveys, including SDSS, PS1, DELS, WISE, CRTS, and PTF. The imaging data taken in separated epochs help identify the variability of objects.

gmw_00006035.name: J1104+0118
gmw_00006035.overview: J1104+0118 is a turn-off changing-look active galactic nucleus candidate [arXiv:1711.08122]. Observations in April 2017 revealed that its optical g-band and mid-infrared W1-band magnitudes dimmed significantly, accompanied by a redder continuum flux and the disappearance of its broad Mg II emission line [arXiv:1711.08122].

sources.summary: The paper reports J1104+0118 as a turn-off changing-look AGN candidate whose optical and mid-infrared dimming led to XLT spectroscopy with no broad Mg II detection.

quotes.quote: In April 2017, We observed one candidate (with the XLT using a 2 3 slit), J1104+0118, with g band and W1 band magnitudes dimmed by more than 1.8 mag and 0.6 mag, respectively. The continuum flux of J1104+0118 obviously became much redder than that in the SDSS spectrum, and the broad Mg II emission line was not detected in the new spectrum. Higher quality spectra are needed for further analysis in detail.

gmw_00006036.name: PKS 0038+09
gmw_00006036.overview: PKS 0038+09 is a broad-line radio galaxy [arXiv:1008.2683, arXiv:1903.10768] featuring a compact, flat-spectrum core [arXiv:1709.00312] and diffuse line emission [arXiv:1903.10768]. It resides in a dense environment [arXiv:1008.2683] and displays a faint, 40-kiloparsec tidal tail that indicates past or ongoing galaxy interactions [arXiv:1008.2683].

sources.summary: The paper uses 3C18 to diagnose ionospheric and IPS variability, describing its compact flat-spectrum core, lobes, extended morphology, and possible lobe hotspot contributions.

quotes.quote: With no filtering of ionospheric scintillation timescales, the source appears to break into two with an East and West component. Inspection of images of the source made on a 10 s imaging cadence reveal that the variability of the source is dominated by East-West movement of the source by a fraction of a beam. The variance is therefore highest on the East and West flanks where the gradient of the PSF is steepest. After filtering, a weak source is still visible in the variability image, however the extended morphology is now gone.

If this is indeed due to IPS from a compact component in 3C18, then this would be consistent with higher frequency radio observations, which show a compact core with lobes approximately 1 \ in extent 1993MNRAS.263.1023M . The core is flat spectrum with a flux density 70 mJy at 5 GHz and above and references therein 2008ApJ...678..712D . This is somewhat less than the scintillating flux density measured. It is possible that the ionosphere is still contributing slightly to the measured scintillating flux density, or that hotspots in the lobes, whose alignment to the core is close to perpendicular to the solar wind direction, are contributing to the IPS power.

sources.summary: MUSE finds diffuse east-west line emission around 3C 018, redshifted gas on both sides, high velocity dispersion, and declining line ratios outward.

quotes.quote: 3C 018: FR II/BLO, 1 = 3.17 kpc. Diffuse line emission, elongated in the EW direction, surrounds the broad lined nucleus out to 15 kpc. The lines are redshifted on both sides of the nucleus, by 200 \ on the West and by 100 \ on the East where they are slightly blue-shifted in the perpendicular N-S direction. The velocity dispersion is always much larger than the instrumental width, with typical values of 200 . The / \ ratio decreases the nucleus toward the extended regions, from 4 to 0.5.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 0038+09 as a powerful radio source whose GMOS-S morphology shows disturbed structure, companions, shells, fans, dust, tails, bridges, or multiple nuclei indicating interaction or merger activity.

quotes.quote: This BLRG/FRII at z=0.19 is found to have about two thirds of its K-band flux in the nuclear unresolved component Inskip10 . The galaxy appears to be in a dense enviroment, with several companions within 20 . Our GMOS-S image (Figure pks0038_online ) reveals a faint and long tidal tail of surface brightness _V = 26.2 mag arcsec ^ 2 extending up to 40 kpc to the NW --- this is the faintest tidal feature detected in our imaging survey. The galaxy was observed with a seeing of FWHM 1 , which makes it difficult to identify other possible signs of disturbance.

gmw_00006037.name: PKS 1151-34
gmw_00006037.overview: PKS 1151-34 is a radio source and quasar that exhibits a highly disturbed morphology [arXiv:1008.2683]. This system features a tidal tail, a secondary nucleus, and a broad fan of emission, indicating that the host galaxy is strongly interacting with a late-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:1008.2683].

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 1151-34 as a powerful radio source whose GMOS-S morphology shows disturbed structure, companions, shells, fans, dust, tails, bridges, or multiple nuclei indicating interaction or merger activity.

quotes.quote: The presence of a YSP in this QSO/CSS is indicated by the presence of PAH features in its Spitzer MIR spectrum Dicken10 . Our GMOS-S image (Figure pks1151_online ) reveals a spectacular extended structure for this system, with a prominent tidal tail/arc feature 34 kpc to the NW that connects to a compact secondary nucleus 27 kpc to the North of the quasar, as well as a broad fan of emission extending 38 kpc to the East of the nucleus. The overall morphology of the extended structure to the N-NW suggests that the quasar host galaxy is strongly interacting with a late-type spiral galaxy (possibly a barred spiral galaxy). Since the spiral structure of the interacting galaxy is reasonably coherent (although somewhat asymmetric), it appears that we are observing the system around the first peri-centre of the interacting galaxies, rather than in the final stages of a merger as the nuclei coalesce.

gmw_00006038.name: PKS 1355-41
gmw_00006038.overview: PKS 1355-41 is a radio source and quasar located at a redshift of z=0.31 [arXiv:1008.2683]. Its disturbed morphology features a sharp shell and a bright tail, indicating interaction or merger activity [arXiv:1008.2683].

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 1355-41 as a powerful radio source whose GMOS-S morphology shows disturbed structure, companions, shells, fans, dust, tails, bridges, or multiple nuclei indicating interaction or merger activity.

quotes.quote: This QSO at redshift z=0.31 has the best seeing among all the GMOS-S observations presented here (FWHM 0.4 ). This fact is clearly reflected in the high-quality of the images and the level of detail of the identified features (Figure pks1355_online ). There is a sharp shell feature 24 kpc to the SE of the quasar and covering an angle of 60 , along with a bright and sharp tail extending up to 22 kpc to the SE along PA=121 and pointing towards the arc. Although the latter feature is closely aligned with the axis of the large-scale radio structure (PA=125 ), the high frequency radio map presented in Dicken08 shows no sign of any radio emission from the tail that might suggest that it is non-thermal in nature.

gmw_00006039.name: PKS 0347+05
gmw_00006039.overview: PKS 0347+05 is a broad-line radio galaxy [arXiv:0810.5675] that forms part of a spectacular interacting system with a QSO/Seyfert 1 nucleus [arXiv:1008.2683]. This system features a bridge linking the two galaxies, at least three tidal tails [arXiv:1008.2683], and potential undetected star formation [arXiv:0810.5675].

sources.summary: The paper flags PKS0347+05 as a far-infrared outlier behind the Galactic plane, possibly affected by two AGN in the aperture and undetected star formation.

quotes.quote: PKS0347+05 . This object lies behind the Galactic plane and, despite the relatively large correction of the [ OIII ] flux for Galactic extinction, it still lies above our fitted regression line. In a recent analysis of near-IR images for the source it was found that there are, in fact, two AGN within our MFIR photometric aperture for this object. If both AGN radiate at MFIR wavelengths this could perhaps explain some of the apparent excess relative to the correlation. Additionally, since PKS0347+05 is a BLRG, its AGN is likely to outshine any starburst signatures at optical wavelengths. Therefore, it is possible that this object could also have star formation activity that has not so far been detected at optical wavelengths.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 0347+05 as a powerful radio source whose GMOS-S morphology shows disturbed structure, companions, shells, fans, dust, tails, bridges, or multiple nuclei indicating interaction or merger activity.

quotes.quote: The WLRG/FRII PKS 0347+05 is part of a spectacular interacting system, which includes a QSO/Seyfert 1 nucleus 26 kpc to the SW of the radio galaxy host itself (Figure pks0347_online ). This object provides some of the best evidence for the triggering of radio/AGN activity as a result of a galaxy interaction in our sample. In addition, Spitzer MIR and FIR data for the WLRG reveal the presence of a YSP, based on the detection of a FIR excess Dicken09 and strong PAH features Dicken10 . Our GMOS-S image shows a bridge linking the two interacting galaxies, and at least three tidal tails extending to the SW of the system.

gmw_00006040.name: PKS 0039-44
gmw_00006040.overview: PKS 0039-44 is a narrow-line radio galaxy that features a double nucleus with a separation of 4 kpc [arXiv:1008.2683]. Its disturbed structure includes shell features to the northwest and southeast, as well as a faint tail pointing toward a companion 70 kpc to the west [arXiv:1008.2683].

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 0039-44 as a powerful radio source whose GMOS-S morphology shows disturbed structure, companions, shells, fans, dust, tails, bridges, or multiple nuclei indicating interaction or merger activity.

quotes.quote: The nucleus of this NLRG appears to be double in our GMOS-S optical image (separation 4 kpc; see inset in Figure pks0039_online ). Shell features are detected 16 kpc to the NW ( _V =23.3 mag arcsec ^ 2 ) and 16 and 27 kpc ( _V =23.3 and 24.1 mag arcsec ^ 2 respectively) to the SE of the nucleus. Although we do not consider it for the morphological classification of PKS 0039-44, it is possible to unveil a faint tail pointing to the star-like companion at 70 kpc to the West. This tentative tail appears co-aligned with the system of shells.

gmw_00006041.name: PKS 1938-15
gmw_00006041.overview: PKS 1938-15 is a broad-line radio galaxy situated in a very dense environment [arXiv:1008.2683]. It displays a disturbed optical morphology with a 20-kiloparsec tail or fan to the northeast and appears to be interacting with a companion galaxy located 18 kiloparsecs to the east [arXiv:1008.2683].

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 1938-15 as a powerful radio source whose GMOS-S morphology shows disturbed structure, companions, shells, fans, dust, tails, bridges, or multiple nuclei indicating interaction or merger activity.

quotes.quote: This BLRG/FRII lies in a very dense environment, and in our optical image appears to have several diffuse companions within a 10 radius (Figure pks1938_online ). We detect a tail/fan of _V =23.2 mag arcsec ^ 2 extending up to 20 kpc to the NE of the radio galaxy. It also looks very probable that PKS 1938-15 is interacting with the companion lying at 18 kpc to the East. There are neither optical long-slit spectra nor infrared data suitable for assessing the degree of emission line contamination of the detected features in this galaxy.

gmw_00006042.name: PKS 1602+01
gmw_00006042.overview: PKS 1602+01 is a broad-line radio galaxy that exhibits a disturbed morphology, including a bright fan, a shell, and a knot-like feature indicating merger or interaction activity [arXiv:1008.2683]. Its radio emission reveals a resolved jet and lobe structure with a 35-degree misalignment and S-symmetry [arXiv:1809.04050].

sources.summary: The paper finds 3C 327.1 has resolved jet and lobe emission, a 35 degree misalignment, S-symmetry, asymmetric backflow, and precession indicators.

quotes.quote: Among the broad line radio galaxies, only 3C 17 and 3C 327.1, have high-quality radio maps with well resolved jet as well as lobe detections (Fig. fig:2JyBLRGs ). Though the lobe axis is now much more difficult to determine, because of the strong contrast in brightness between the likely Doppler boosted jets and the fainter parts of the lobes, the misalignment is evident in both cases. We estimate misalignment angles of 21 for 3C 17 and 35 for 3C 327.1, much more than the 5 for Cygnus A. Both sources show curved jets with a sharp bend at the brightest jet knot (hotspot), suggesting that the jets may be deflected there before hitting the lobe boundary a second time at the final hotspot.

The sources also have S-symmetry and the backflow on the jet side of 3C 327.1 is strongly asymmetric. These features are clear indicators for precession according to the criteria defined in Sect. sec:pcrits . Because the length scales are very similar to Cygnus A, the precession period must also be of the order of 10^6 yrs.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 1602+01 as a powerful radio source whose GMOS-S morphology shows disturbed structure, companions, shells, fans, dust, tails, bridges, or multiple nuclei indicating interaction or merger activity.

quotes.quote: This BLRG/FRII at redshift z=0.46 shows several signs of interaction in our i' band GMOS-S image (Figure pks1602_online ). Towards the North of the galaxy nucleus we detect a bright and smooth fan of _V =21.34 mag arcsec ^ 2 . On the opposite side there is a shell of _V =23.3 mag arcsec ^ 2 . Finally, and immersed in the shell envelope, we detect a sharp knot-like feature that extends up to 23 kpc and that is closely aligned (PA=148 ) with the inner axis of the radio jet (PA=145 ; see radio images in Morganti99 ).

gmw_00006043.name: PKS 0117-15
gmw_00006043.overview: PKS 0117-15 is a radio galaxy that features a high-surface-brightness triple nucleus system [arXiv:1008.2683]. Its disturbed optical morphology includes a western shell, an arc-like structure, and a companion galaxy, suggesting active galaxy interactions [arXiv:1008.2683].

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 0117-15 as a powerful radio source whose GMOS-S morphology shows disturbed structure, companions, shells, fans, dust, tails, bridges, or multiple nuclei indicating interaction or merger activity.

quotes.quote: Our optical image of this NLRG/FRII, taken in excellent seeing conditions (FWHM=0.5 ), shows a high-surface-brightness triple nucleus system of 9 kpc diameter co-aligned along PA=8 (Figure pks0117_online ). In addition, we detect a shell on the western side of the radio galaxy extending up to 21 kpc, for which we measure a surface brightness of _V =25.1 mag arcsec ^ 2 , as well as an arc-like structure (identified as "I" in Table data ) of _V =21.3 mag arcsec ^ 2 and a companion galaxy 9 kpc and 17 kpc to the NE of the central nucleus respectively. Interestingly, the NE arc shows a gap close to its interception with the axis of the large scale radio structure (PA=35 ). This suggests a possible interaction between the arc and the radio jet.

gmw_00006044.name: PKS 0252-71
gmw_00006044.overview: PKS 0252-71 is a radio galaxy and active galactic nucleus surrounded by an amorphous halo [arXiv:1008.2683]. This halo points toward a fainter companion galaxy located 33 kiloparsecs to the south, indicating a possible interaction between the two systems [arXiv:1008.2683].

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 0252-71 as a powerful radio source whose GMOS-S morphology shows disturbed structure, companions, shells, fans, dust, tails, bridges, or multiple nuclei indicating interaction or merger activity.

quotes.quote: The NLRG/CSS galaxy PKS 0252-71 appears to be surrounded by an amorphous halo which points towards a fainter companion galaxy at 33 kpc to the South, thus indicating a possible interaction between the galaxies (Figure pks0252_online ). Note that the bright object 3 to the SE of the radio galaxy host is a star. Unfortunately, the relatively poor seeing of this image (FWHM=1 ) prevents a clear detection of other possible signs of disturbance.

gmw_00006045.name: PKS 0235-19
gmw_00006045.overview: PKS 0235-19 is an extended radio galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0003076] hosting a Seyfert 1 nucleus [arXiv:1801.01751]. Its disturbed morphology features tidal tails, dust, and a potential bridge to a neighboring galaxy, indicating interaction or merger activity [arXiv:1008.2683].

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 0235 19 as the non-3C southern source in the sample, a Parkes radio source with a Seyfert 1 nucleus and KDA EXT-preferred spectra.

quotes.quote: PKS 0235 19 This is the only source in the sample not covered by the 3C catalogue due to its location in the southern hemisphere. Nonetheless, PKS 0235 19 is listed in the Parkes Radio Catalogue (Wright Otrupcek 1990). Its nucleus has been classified as a Seyfert 1 galaxy with broad Balmer lines (Tadhunter et al. 1998). The spectra predicted with the KDA and KDA EXT models are shown in Figure 8. The relevant ^ 2 and values, along with the resulting flux densities calculated for these two models, are presented in Table 9, confirming the preference for the KDA EXT solution.

sources.summary: The paper presents 0235-197 as an extended radio galaxy with low-frequency variability, weak 320 MHz polarization, and possible refractive scintillation from a compact hot-spot component.

quotes.quote: Multi-wavelength VLA observations of two extended radio galaxies, 0235-197 and 1203+043 are presented. There is some evidence from earlier studies that these two sources exhibit low frequency ( <1 GHz) variability. This work shows that both sources have linear polarizations, if any, below the detection limits at 320 MHz, so we cannot explain the variability as being due to instrumental polarization effects as has been suggested for 3C159. Refractive scintillation may be the cause of the variability in 0235 197. This would require the existence of a bright, compact component in one of the hot spots seen in these observations.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 0235-19 as a powerful radio source whose GMOS-S morphology shows disturbed structure, companions, shells, fans, dust, tails, bridges, or multiple nuclei indicating interaction or merger activity.

quotes.quote: Our GMOS-S image of this BLRG/FRII at redshift z=0.62 (Figure pks0235_online ) shows tidal tails of similar surface brightness to both the NE and SW of the nucleus along PA=52 , with a maximum extent of 15 kpc to the NE ( _V =22.2 and 22.4 mag arcsec ^ 2 for the NE and SW tails, respectively). Apart from the tidal tails, another interpretation of the observed structures is that they represent a warped disk structure with the BLRG nucleus at its centre. The tail/disk structure is significantly mis-aligned by 42 degrees from the axis of the large scale radio structure (PA=94 ). Although we do not consider it for the morphological classification of the galaxy, there is tentative evidence for a bridge linking the radio galaxy with the smaller galaxy 165 kpc to the NE.

gmw_00006046.name: PKS 2135-20
gmw_00006046.overview: PKS 2135-20 is a radio galaxy and active galactic nucleus classified as a broad-line radio galaxy [arXiv:1008.2683]. Its disturbed optical morphology features a broad northern fan, which indicates a past interaction or merger event [arXiv:1008.2683].

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 2135-20 as a powerful radio source whose GMOS-S morphology shows disturbed structure, companions, shells, fans, dust, tails, bridges, or multiple nuclei indicating interaction or merger activity.

quotes.quote: Our optical image of this BLRG/CSS (Figure pks2135_20_online ) shows a broad fan on the northern side of the radio galaxy, of surface brightness _V =23.5 mag arcsec ^ 2 . This feature is very likely the result of a past interaction, consistent with the detection of a YSP in the nuclear region of this galaxy from optical spectroscopy Holt07 , FIR excess Dicken09 , and the detection of strong PAH features Dicken10 . The results regarding the continuum or line-emitting nature of this fan are inconclusive based on our long-slit spectra. Note that PKS2135-20 is one of the only two galaxies (the other is PKS 2314+03) in our sample that would be classified as ULIRGs based on their MIR and FIR luminosities.

gmw_00006047.overview: The active galaxy 0134 is classified as a narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:2203.13419, arXiv:2208.06581] that exhibits super-Eddington accretion [arXiv:2203.13419, arXiv:2208.06581]. It displays drastic X-ray spectral variability [arXiv:2203.13419, arXiv:2208.06581], which may be driven by complex, variable warm absorption along the line of sight [arXiv:2203.13419, arXiv:astro-ph/9810105].

sources.summary: The paper describes 0134 as an exceptional super-Eddington NLS1 whose 2019 observations caught a low X-ray state with weak soft excess, hard-band lag, and likely clumpy-disc-wind absorption or reflection.

quotes.quote: We have conducted a new long-term multi-wavelength campaign on one of the most super-Eddington narrow-line Seyfert 1s (NLS1s) known, namely 0134. In this first paper, we report deep simultaneous X-ray observations performed by \ and \ in 2019-12-19, during which 0134 \ was fortuitously at one of its lowest X-ray flux states. However, there is a clear rise above 4 keV which implies that the intrinsic source flux may be higher. The X-ray spectra observed between 1996 and 2019 show drastic variability, probably due to complex, variable absorption along the line of sight. Unusually, the soft X-ray excess appears extremely weak in all these spectra, even when the hard X-ray spectrum has a steep slope of 2.2 .

The unusual properties of 0134 \ motivated us to conduct a new multi-wavelength campaign on this exceptional NLS1 (principal investigator: C. Jin). This program comprised joint observations in 2019-12-19 with \ ( Jansen.2001 ), \ ( Harrison.2013 ), \ ( Gehrels.2004 ) and the 2.3-m optical telescope in the Siding Spring Observatory (SSO). These observations happened to coincide when 0134 \ was in its X-ray low-flux state exhibiting many interesting properties.

sources.summary: The paper studies 0134 as an extreme super-Eddington NLS1 with weak UV lines, strong X-ray variability, and proposed weak-line Seyfert behavior.

quotes.quote: 0134 \ is one of the most super-Eddington narrow-line Seyfert 1 (NLS1) galaxies, on which we conducted a monitoring campaign from radio to X-rays. In this paper, we present a detailed analysis of its optical/UV spectra and broadband spectral energy distribution (SED). Our study shows that the preferred black hole mass of 0134 \ is M_ BH 2 10^ 7 M_ , giving a mass accretion rate through the outer disc of m _ out 20 (assuming zero spin), compared to the observed luminosity ratio L_ bol /L_ Edd 6 . This reduction in radiative efficiency is expected for super-Eddington flows, as power can be lost via advection and/or disc winds. We find that the optical/UV lines of 0134 \ resemble those from weak-like quasars (WLQs), as it has notably weak C iv and N v emission lines.
It also has drastic X-ray variability, again similar to that recently observed in some other WLQs.

We suggest a new category of weak-line Seyfert (WLS) galaxies to describe sources like 0134, and interpret its (so far unique) properties in a model, where the lower-disc-temperature in the higher-mass black holes leads to the UV-line-driving mechanism, which enhances the super-Eddington radiation-pressure-driven wind.

sources.summary: The paper examines RX J0134.3-4258's strong spectral variability as warm absorption, noting an ultrasoft survey spectrum, a later flat PSPC spectrum, and a viable warm-absorbed flat power-law model.

quotes.quote: The strong spectral variability of RX J0134.3-4258 (from 4.4 in the survey observation, to 2.2 in our subsequent pointing) is examined in terms of warm absorption.

This NLSy1 exhibited an ultrasoft spectrum in the survey observation (Dec. 1990; formally 4.4 ). Interestingly, the spectrum had changed to flat in our subsequent pointed PSPC observation (Dec. 1992; 2.2 ). This kind of spectral variability is naturally predicted within the framework of warm absorbers, making the object a very good candidate for warm absorption.
We find that a warm-absorbed, intrinsically flat powerlaw can indeed describe the survey observation.

sources.summary: The paper treats HE0132-4313 as an A4/NLSy1 outlier with unusually large line-width discrepancies and excludes it from one scaling-law fit without changing the result much.

quotes.quote: This is due to an outstanding case, HE0132-4313 , a NLSy1 with FWHM \ / FWHM \ 5 , a behaviour frequent for the \ in strong \ emitters but apparently rarer for .

One data point has been excluded applying a \ clipping algorithm (the one A4 outlier, HE0132-4313 ). This selective procedure is justified by the fact that only some of the most extreme sources of Population A (not all of them) show large blueshifts and only one ( HE0132-4313 ) a FWHM in excess to \ by a large factor, deviating at more than 3 times the sample rms. Removal of HE0132-4313 provides however only a minor, not significant change in the fitting parameters.

gmw_00006048.name: PSOJ108+08
gmw_00006048.overview: PSOJ108+08 is a high-redshift quasar located at a redshift of 5.9647 [arXiv:2305.00998]. It features a small proximity zone that may be prematurely truncated by a nearby high-ionization metal absorber [arXiv:2305.00998].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PSOJ108+08 as the sample's second-smallest proximity-zone quasar, with flux extending beyond the zone and a nearby high-ionization absorber that may truncate it.

quotes.quote: The quasar PSOJ108+08 is at a relatively lower redshift of 5.9647 with a magnitude of 27.59 . This quasar has the second smallest proximity zone size in our sample, with R_ p =1.99  pMpc. As we see in Figure  fig:fullsample , the spectrum of this quasar does not show a damping wing. Although the proximity zone size is small, the flux blueward of the \ line extends all the way up to 6  pMpc i.e., nearly 3 times the proximity zone size and immediately increases above our 10 threshold beyond the proximity zone. We find a high-ionization metal absorber at 2.53 pMpc from this quasar, indicating that the proximity zone might be prematurely truncated due to absorption of the quasar flux by this absorber.

gmw_00006049.name: J0738+3156
gmw_00006049.overview: J0738+3156 is a single, undisturbed galaxy hosting a double-peaked active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2209.02717]. It has no close companion within 3 arcseconds [arXiv:2209.02717], and it exhibits a disturbed, rotation-dominated optical kinematics profile [arXiv:2209.02717].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0738+3156 as a single, undisturbed double-peaked AGN with no close Keck-detected companion, disturbed rotation-dominated optical kinematics, no VLBA detection, and RFI-limited absorption data.

quotes.quote: Out of the ten double-peaked AGN, five (J0738+3156, J0842+0547, J1203+1319, J1243-0058 and J1517+3353) appear as single, undisturbed systems in the available optical images [ Dark Energy Camera Legacy Survey DECaLS; dey2019 , PanSTARRS-1 chambers2016 and SDSS ] . The remaining AGN are either part of galaxy pairs and interacting systems or show disturbed morphology. J0009-0036 has a companion galaxy at a projected separation of 40 kpc and a velocity separation of 460 . J1526+4140 is undergoing merger with another galaxy at 5 kpc and 150 . The optical continuum image of J0912+5320 shows two peaks and a tidal tail, while that of J1352+6541 shows signatures of a possible tidal disturbance. J1356+1026 is a post-merger system that has been observed as part of our \ 21-cm absorption survey in low- z galaxy mergers dutta2018 . Long-slit spectroscopy and NIR imaging of this system show double nuclei separated by 2.9 kpc greene2011,shen2011 . Moreover, there have been observations of extended \ emission in this system, suggesting large-scale outflows powered by feedback from the obscured, radio-quiet quasar greene2012 . Three of the AGN that appear as single systems (J0738+3156, J0842+0547 and J1203+1319) have been observed with the Keck Laser Guide Star Adaptive Optics system and the NIR camera NIRC2 mcgurk2015 . Based on these imaging observations, J1203+1319 has a double spatial structure on scales of <3 arcsec, indicating it is a candidate dual AGN. J0738+3156 and J0842+0547, on the other hand, show single spatial structure with no companions within 3 arcsec. Furthermore, long-slit optical spectra have been obtained for four AGN (J0738+3156, J0842+0547, J1243-0058 and J1517+3353) by comerford2018 . Based on these spectra, J0842+0547 and J1243-0058 are classified as outflows, whereas J1517+3353 is classified as an outflow composite, i.e. an outflow that includes many gas clouds at different velocities. On the other hand, J0738+3156 has a disturbed rotation-dominated profile.

Based on high-resolution ( 0.2 arcsec) multi-band (8-12 GHz) VLA observations muller2015 , J0009-0036 shows a two-sided radio jet suggesting that a radio jet-driven outflow could produce the double-peaked emission. In addition, six of the AGN have been imaged with VLBA at milliarcsecond (mas; 10 pc) resolution liu2018a . Three of them (J0009-0036, J0738+3156 and J1356+1026) remain undetected in the VLBA 8.4 GHz images. J0912+5320 shows three components in the VLBA image, an unresolved central core and northern and southern components extending over 25 mas, indicative of a jet structure. J1243-0058 shows a compact, unresolved component in the VLBA image, and there are no other radio sources stronger than 1 mJy within 1 arcsec. J1352+6541 shows a faint component at 2.4 mas from the primary component.

The observations of two of the sources, J0738+3156 and J1352+6541, were affected by strong radio frequency interference (RFI), preventing us from reliably studying the associated \ 21-cm absorption in these cases. The \ 21-cm spectra towards the remaining eight sources, along with the double-peaked \ emission lines in the SDSS spectra, are shown in Fig. fig:sdss_gmrt_spec . Among the eight sources with reliable \ 21-cm absorption spectra, we detect absorption from three sources (J1356+1026, J1517+3353, J1526+4140). In two cases, J0009-0036 and J0912+5320, we find tentative absorption features at the expected position of the \ 21-cm line. We reobserved the source J0009-0036 using the same observational set-up for an additional 7 hours in October 2019. We combined the spectra of J0009-0036 from the two different observing runs using inverse noise weighting. The peak optical depth of the absorption feature is detected at 3 significance in the combined spectrum. In the case of the absorption towards J0912+5320, the peak optical depth is detected at 5 significance. However, the absorption lines towards both J0009-0036 and J0912+5320 are narrow (velocity width encompassing 90 of the total optical depth, 90 \ 30-40 ), whereas most associated \ 21-cm absorption lines tend to broader on average ( 100 ). Further, the absorption profiles in the stokes LL and RR spectra do not resemble each other perfectly, although they are consistent within the uncertainties. Since there could be low-level sporadic RFI affecting the absorption features, we consider these as tentative detections for our analysis. The two tentative \ 21-cm absorption lines are shown in Fig. fig:ten_hi_spec . For the remaining three sources, J0842+0547, J1203+1319, J1243-0058, where we do not detect absorption, we place 3 upper limit on the integrated optical depth for a velocity width of 100 .
