Since this object lies right along the tidal stream between M81 and NGC 3077, and given its young population and lack of a built-up RGB, we consider this object to be a tidal dwarf formed along this bridge of material likely having a distance similar to that of these 2 giants, 3.7 - 3.9 Mpc.

gmw_00006083.name: d0926+70
gmw_00006083.overview: The galaxy d0926+70 is classified as a transition dwarf galaxy with properties intermediate between dwarf irregulars and dwarf spheroidals [arXiv:1309.4130]. It features an irregular shape, a cluster of blue stars near its centroid, and recent star formation at very low rates, though no HI gas has been detected [arXiv:1309.4130].

sources.summary: The paper classifies d0926+70 as a small dI/dSph transition galaxy with a few central blue stars, low-metallicity older populations, no HI detection, and recent low-rate star formation.

quotes.quote: dI/dSph Transition: d0926+70 This galaxy was considered a dwarf irregular in our original discovery paper. In the WFPC2 imaging, it is found to contain several blue stars clustered near the galaxy centroid, although perhaps slightly off-center (Figure Locs1 ). This is difficult to determine as the object is not entirely imaged within the WFPC2 field, and the shape of the galaxy appears to be slightly extended and irregular as well.

Given the excess of blue stars towards the core of this object, the irregular shape of the galaxy, and the roughly determined young age for the blue stellar component, we consider this object to be a dwarf transition object with properties intermediate between dIs and dSphs. Such objects known in the Local Group include e.g.

Similarly, d0926+70 appears to have had a dominant early period of star formation without significant chemical enrichment and exhibits evidence for later and recent star formation at very low rates based on the presence of a handful of blue and red He burning stars and possible low metallicity AGB stars. The total HI gas mass measured for LGS 3 is 2.7 10^5 M _ hid11 . sambit do not detect any HI in d0926+70 to an upper limit of M _ HI = 3.1 10^5 M _ .

gmw_00006084.name: d0944+71
gmw_00006084.overview: The dwarf spheroidal galaxy d0944+71 is located at a large distance from other massive galaxies [arXiv:1309.4130]. It is dominated by a primarily old stellar population, though a handful of blue stars in its central region suggest a minor star formation episode may have occurred as recently as 150 million years ago [arXiv:1309.4130].

sources.summary: The paper describes d0944+71 as the brightest new dSph, mostly old but with possible blue stars, low HI, and a large separation from M81.

quotes.quote: It is easily seen on the lower panel of Figure 3d1 well separated from other large galaxies at supergalactic coordinates (2.6, 2.1, 0.1). This large distance may explain the very low level of star formation this galaxy may have undergone perhaps as recently as 150 Myr ago.

Our brightest new dSph, d0944+71, is quite similar to these BCDs in several respects. In the magnitude-size plane (Figure remr ), it is found at similar brightness and size, with a surface brightness only slightly lower than that of the lowest surface brightness BCD, d1028+70. It has an RGB approximate metallicity of Z = 0.0015 , similar to BCD d1012+64, and has a similar size. Although we can confidently say that this galaxy has a predominantly old stellar population and has not recently formed stars, there are a handful of stellar detections blueward of the red sequence which could belong to a young > 150 Myr population and which are found spatially coincident with the central region of the galaxy.
The brightest of these, d0944+71 with M _ ^ = -12.4 , may have experienced a minor episode of star formation as recently as 150 Myr ago, but otherwise exhibits a primarily old stellar population.

gmw_00006085.name: d0939+71
gmw_00006085.overview: The object d0939+71 is a very small dwarf spheroidal galaxy [arXiv:1309.4130]. It hosts a pure, old stellar population of low metallicity with a poorly populated but defined red giant branch [arXiv:1309.4130].

sources.summary: The paper identifies d0939+71 as a very small M81 dSph with a poorly populated but defined RGB and an old, low-metallicity stellar population.

quotes.quote: Classical and Ultra-faint dSphs For ACS observed galaxies, we find pure old stellar populations in the very small galaxies d0939+71, d0944+69, and d1015+69. These have poorly populated, but still reasonably well defined RGBs. The only stars present in the CMDs (Figures trgb5 trgb6 ) are those which could be described by an old red giant population having low metallicity. For d0939+71 and d1015+69, 12.5 Gyr isochrones suggest an RGB metallicity between 0.0001 < Z < 0.001 , consistent with an old, single age/metallicity population.

gmw_00006086.name: d1015+69
gmw_00006086.overview: The galaxy d1015+69 is a small dwarf spheroidal galaxy [arXiv:1309.4130]. It contains a pure, old stellar population of low metallicity with a poorly populated red giant branch [arXiv:1309.4130].

sources.summary: The paper establishes d1015+69 as a small M81 early-type dSph with a poorly populated RGB and an old, low-metallicity stellar population.

quotes.quote: Classical and Ultra-faint dSphs For ACS observed galaxies, we find pure old stellar populations in the very small galaxies d0939+71, d0944+69, and d1015+69. These have poorly populated, but still reasonably well defined RGBs. gmw_00006087.name: d0944+69
gmw_00006087.overview: The object d0944+69 is a dwarf spheroidal galaxy with a very poorly populated red giant branch [arXiv:1309.4130]. It is a small and faint dwarf galaxy with a size that likely places it within the range of ultra-faint dwarf galaxies [arXiv:1309.4130].

sources.summary: The paper describes d0944+69 as the smallest and faintest new dSph, with a sparse RGB, old stellar population, and size near the ultra-faint dwarf regime.

quotes.quote: The RGB for d0944+69 is very poorly populated, but may have a slightly higher metallicity than these other two galaxies. The isochrones which best bound this RGB have metallicities between 0.0001 < Z < 0.002 . This is also our smallest and faintest dwarf at M_ ^ = -6.8 with size R _e = 90 pc (as estimated from ^ MegaCam data) or R _e = 130 pc and M_ I = -9.1 (as measured from F814W ACS data), likely within the range of the ultra-faint dwarf galaxies ( M_V > -8.0 ) being discovered within the Local Group.

The smallest object in our sample, dSph d0944+69, was originally measured in our MegaCam data to have a size of 90 pc, which falls within the 40-100 pc size gap between most dwarf galaxies and globular clusters gil07 . This can be seen in Figure remr as the point much smaller and fainter than any of the other new detections. The size gap region is bounded by two dashed vertical lines, and d0944+69 falls between them, albeit towards the galaxy scale boundary with a 1 upper limit in size extending above 100 pc. Current measurements using the ACS data and the bright RGB population places the size at 130 pc.

gmw_00006088.name: d0955+70
gmw_00006088.overview: The galaxy d0955+70 is a small dwarf galaxy located near M81 [arXiv:1309.4130]. It hosts a very small, pure old stellar population that is likely metal-poor [arXiv:1309.4130].

sources.summary: The paper places d0955+70 near M81 as a small, metal-poor dwarf spheroidal with a sparse RGB and pure old stellar population.

quotes.quote: Other objects, d0926+70, d0955+70, d1006+67, d1014+68, d1041+70, d0939+70, d0944+69, d1015+69, have uncertainties in distance ranging 200 kpc to 1 Mpc. The TRGB measurements themselves place all these objects near to M81 at 3.69 Mpc. The large uncertainties are due to the nature of these objects. They all appear to be small dwarfs with very small stellar populations.

Galaxies observed with WFPC2 having pure old stellar populations as found from the CMDs (Figure trgb7 ) include d0955+70 and d1006+67. These are likely to be metal poor, although the metallicities are poorly constrained due to large photometric uncertainties. Besides the shallow depth of the imaging, the RGBs are poorly populated since these galaxies host very small stellar populations. Three other galaxies observed with WFPC2 (d1041+70, d1014+68, and d0934+70, Figures trgb8 and trgb6 ) also display predominantly old stellar populations although may host a few stars above the tip of the RGB.

gmw_00006089.name: d1006+67
gmw_00006089.overview: The galaxy d1006+67 is a small dwarf spheroidal galaxy located near M81 [arXiv:1309.4130]. It hosts a very small, metal-poor stellar population that is purely old [arXiv:1309.4130].

sources.summary: The paper places d1006+67 near M81 as a small, metal-poor dwarf spheroidal with a sparse RGB and pure old stellar population.

gmw_00006090.name: d0934+70
gmw_00006090.overview: d0934+70 is a small dwarf spheroidal galaxy associated with the M81 group [arXiv:1309.4130]. It hosts a predominantly old stellar population with a sparse red giant branch, alongside a few asymptotic giant branch stars that suggest a small amount of star formation occurred as recently as 1 Gyr ago [arXiv:1309.4130].

sources.summary: The paper classifies d0934+70 as a small M81 dwarf spheroidal with uncertain TRGB distance, a sparse RGB, and possible 1 Gyr AGB stars.

quotes.quote: Galaxies observed with WFPC2 having pure old stellar populations as found from the CMDs (Figure trgb7 ) include d0955+70 and d1006+67. These are likely to be metal poor, although the metallicities are poorly constrained due to large photometric uncertainties. Besides the shallow depth of the imaging, the RGBs are poorly populated since these galaxies host very small stellar populations. Three other galaxies observed with WFPC2 (d1041+70, d1014+68, and d0934+70, Figures trgb8 and trgb6 ) also display predominantly old stellar populations although may host a few stars above the tip of the RGB.

We find that a 1 Gyr, Z=0.002 isochrone could explain the handful of AGB stars in d0934+70 coinciding with this track. For d1041+70 and d1014+68, AGB sequences for ages 1 and 4 Gyr with Z = 0.002 approximately bound the few AGB stars in these galaxies. Although there may have been some small amount of star formation as recently as 1 Gyr ago, there is no evidence for significant intermediate (or young) populations in any of these aforementioned galaxies. Thus, we also consider these 5 galaxies to be dwarf spheroidals.

gmw_00006091.name: d1014+68
gmw_00006091.overview: The galaxy d1014+68 is classified as a dwarf spheroidal galaxy [arXiv:1309.4130]. It hosts a very small stellar population that is predominantly old, with only weak evidence of a few intermediate-age asymptotic giant branch stars [arXiv:1309.4130].

sources.summary: The paper classifies d1014+68 as a small M81 dwarf spheroidal with predominantly old stars and only weak possible intermediate-age AGB evidence.

gmw_00006092.name: d1041+70
gmw_00006092.overview: The galaxy d1041+70 is a small dwarf spheroidal galaxy associated with the M81 group [arXiv:1309.4130]. It is characterized by a predominantly old stellar population, with only a few potential intermediate-age asymptotic giant branch stars [arXiv:1309.4130].

sources.summary: The paper classifies d1041+70 as a small M81 dwarf spheroidal with predominantly old stars and only weak possible intermediate-age AGB evidence.

gmw_00006093.name: PKS 1127-14
gmw_00006093.overview: PKS 1127-14 is a gamma-ray emitting flat-spectrum radio quasar [arXiv:1311.7647, arXiv:2405.04935] at a redshift of z = 1.187 [arXiv:2405.04935]. It features a 30-arcsecond-long X-ray and radio jet [arXiv:0804.1564, arXiv:2405.04935] that exhibits offsets between its radio and X-ray emission knots [arXiv:0905.2818, arXiv:2405.04935].

sources.summary: PKS 1127-145 is used as a background quasar sightline; its field supplied 58 galaxies, and its STIS/UVES spectra yielded 38 absorber lines plus 23 metal lines.

quotes.quote: The number of galaxies that could be used in the calculation that were found in the field of PKS 1127-145 was 58.

The \ absorbers used in the cross-correlation function were detected in the absorption spectra of the background quasars HE 1122-1648 and PKS 1127-145. These quasars were selected because high resolution (R =30000 ), STIS echelle spectroscopy obtained using the E230M grating exists within the HST archive. These archived spectra cover the wavelength range 2050-3111 , the range of wavelengths needed to locate \ lines at a redshift of z 1 . With such a high dispersion, it was hoped that low column density ( 13 ) HI absorption lines could be resolved and Voigt profiles fitted in order that their column density and b-parameter could be determined.

Fewer \ lines were detected in the STIS spectrum of PKS 1127-145 due to the lower quasar redshift. 38 \ lines were found together with 23 metal lines in both the STIS and UVES spectra. Therefore over a total path length of z =1.08 we detected 135 \ absorbers that could be used in the correlation function.

sources.summary: The paper treats PKS 1127-14 as a former GPS quasar reclassified as blazar-like/FSRQ, with kiloparsec jet evidence, LAT association, gamma-ray variability, and parsec-scale Compton emission.

quotes.quote: An example is the case of PKS 1127-14, which was initially classified as a GPS quasar. X-ray and radio observations have revealed the presence of a kiloparsec scale jet Sie02, Sie07 . This together with the observed multi-band flux variability and superluminal expansion of the core region points to a blazar nature of the source Bla04 . We still included the source in our sample for comparison with the other GPS/CSS quasars.
None of the sources in the sample is associated with a ray source in the LAT 2-year Point Source Catalogue 2FGL except for PKS 1127-14, associated with the ray source 2FGL1130.3-1448 Nol12 .

PKS1127-14 is the only object associated with a ray source with a high TS value (514). However, the classification of this source has been revised from a GPS to a FSRQ Hea07 . Its ray lightcurve shows variability, further supporting the interpretation of a blazar-like nature of the core.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 1127 145 as a ray-emitting FSRQ with a long X-ray jet whose radio/X-ray knot offsets complicate emission models, and locates the 2020-2021 high-energy flare in the VLA core.

quotes.quote: PKS 1127 145, at z = 1.187 drinkwater97 , is one of the few ray-emitting FSRQs with a prominent X-ray jet extending for 30 siemiginowska02 . This is one of the longest jets observed so far in X-rays. It was discovered in the first observation of PKS 1127 145 with the Chandra X-ray Observatory (hereafter ). Three main knots are detected in X-rays along the inner part of the jet, whereas the radio emission peaks at the two outer knots, associated with weak X-ray emission. The misalignment between radio and X-ray emission challenges our understanding of the jet structure and dominant radiation mechanism at the origin of the high-energy emission siemiginowska02,siemiginowska07 .

Neither in X-rays nor at radio frequencies we observe any brightening of a jet knot in PKS 1127 145. Despite short, Chandra observations would have been deep enough to detect the flare if it had been from the kpc-scale X-ray jet. On the other hand, the X-ray flux of the quasar core is roughly 2.6 times higher than that reported in siemiginowska02 and comparable to that observed by Swift just after the flaring episode dammando20b , locating the flaring region within the VLA core.

sources.summary: The paper discusses PKS 1127-145 as a quasar jet with observed radio/X-ray knot offsets, finding offset magnitudes compatible with the model for certain jet speeds and viewing angles.

quotes.quote: Finally, we discuss on offsets of quasar jet in PKS 1127-145 which located at z=1.187 and 1'' corresponds to 11.5kpc sta98 . The largescale radio/X-ray offsets are found as 2''.1 for knot A, 1''.4 for knot B and 0''.8 for knot C sie02 . The jet radius R is around 2'' for every konts sie02 and satisfies equation ( eq:condition_R ) for B=1mG . However, the viewing angle and velocity of the jet are not well known yet sie02, sie07 so we examine parameters which meet the condition _X > 2'' by equation ( eq:delta_x ). Consequently we find when > 5 or <0.7/ or >1.3/ the magnitudes of the offsets are comptible with our model.
Finally we argued that the observed offsets in FR I radio galaxies M87 and Cen A and other kpc jets of quasar PKS 1127-145 are well interpreted with our model.

sources.summary: The paper treats PKS 1127 145 as a z=1.187 HI-absorbed quasar with two-day continuum and HI variability, using changing absorption strengths to infer small-scale intervening HI structure.

quotes.quote: PKS 1127 145 is a 5-7 Jy, z=1.187 quasar which exhibits continuum and HI-spectral variability on a time scale of t 2 days (Kanekar Chengalur 2001). The intervening system absorbs 0.5 Jy, and is located at a redshift z_ abs =0.3127 . After shifting all HI spectra to the same continuum flux level, differences of 35 mJy were found in the strengths of the absorption lines from one observation to the next, showing that the strength of the lines varied relative to the continuum. The fluctuations are reported to be consistent with variations only in the depths of the components, and not with changes in their positions or widths.

The time scale of changes in the HI spectrum implies a length scale over which the HI varies significantly in the intervening system. Assuming that the temporal variations are due to changes in the line of sight to a superluminal background source, variability on a time scale of t implies substantial changes in the HI structure on the angular scale of (see ( vabs ) in Appendix A) (for =1 and H_0=65 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 ) = 1.7 t 1 day as .

sources.summary: The paper reports a large-scale X-ray and radio jet in PKS 1127-145 and notes that its hard X-ray spectrum may make it an exception among the GPS/CSS sample.

quotes.quote: Large scale X-ray jets were discovered in two sources in our sample: PKS1127-145 siem2002,siemi2007 and B2 0738+313 siem2003 . A re-analysis of the radio data confirmed the presence of the radio jets. Both jets have similar morphologies in X-ray and radio.

We note that PKS 1127-145 maybe an exception because it has both a hard X-ray spectrum ( = 1.2 0.03 ) and a small _ ox equal to 1.29. Therefore X-ray spectral analysis of a photon index and other spectral features for a larger sample are needed to firmly established such conclusions.

sources.summary: Suzaku spectra of PKS 1127 145 show a low-energy photon deficit; a free-absorption power-law fit implies a column density above the Galactic value.

quotes.quote: PKS 1127 145 We first fitted the XISs spectra with a single power-law model with a Galactic absorption of N_ H = 3.83 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 (Murphy et al. 1996). We obtained the photon index of = 1.41 0.01 ^2_ = 1.01 for 331 dof), but the residuals show a substantial deficit of photons at low energies (Figure fig:QHBspec ). To investigate this deficit in more detail, we fitted the spectra with a single power-law and a free absorption model. This model represents well the spectra with the best chi-squared value of 0.82 for 330 dof (Figure fig:1127spec ), indicating that the column density is higher than the Galactic value at the 99.9 confidence level. For this model the photon index is = 1.51 0.02 and the unabsorbed X-ray flux is F_ 2-10 keV = (3.36 0.05) 10^ 12 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper notes PKS 1127-145 is a Gigahertz-Peaked Source with unpublished XMM-Newton data, usable after flare cleaning, and a Chandra X-ray jet too compact for EPIC.

quotes.quote: This source is in the list of Gigahertz-Peaked Sources (GPS) by Stanghellini et al. (1998), Stanghellini (2003). The present XMM-Newton data set has never been published. It shows high background, but, once cleaned of soft-proton flares, it is possible to extract useful information. This source also has a 30'' sized jet, observed in X-rays with Chandra (Siemiginowska et al. 2002), but that is still too small for the PSF size of EPIC.

sources.summary: PKS1127 is used as a high-redshift jet example whose Knot C and beaming parameters yield similar synchrotron and IC/CMB constraints.

quotes.quote: PKS1127 has a redshift of z=1.16 so beaming models do not require a large (Harris Krawczynski 2002, Siemiginowska et al.
Knot C is located 28 '' from the core.
For this source, there is not much difference between synchrotron and IC/CMB models insofar as our analysis is concerned.

gmw_00006094.name: SDSSJ165931.92 + 023021.92
gmw_00006094.overview: SDSSJ165931.92 + 023021.92 is a galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.2 [arXiv:1501.06586]. Morphological analysis of the galaxy reveals asymmetric features, including spiral arms and a minor merger or large clump in its southern region [arXiv:1501.06586].

sources.summary: The paper uses SDSSJ165931.92 + 023021.92 to demonstrate morphological fitting across CFHT and SDSS images, recovering consistent parameters and residual spiral-arm or merger features.

quotes.quote: Panel (a) shows a Canada France Hawaii Telescope (CFHT) I band image of the z 0.2 galaxy SDSSJ165931.92 + 023021.92 KacprzakG_14a . Panel (e) shows an band image of the galaxy from the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) at a spatial resolution of 1 1. For each data set, we show the fitted (convolved) model, the residual map, and the one-dimentional SB profile. One sees that the intrinsic modeled morphological parameters found from the SDSS data (PSF FWHM=1 1) are in good agreement with the higher-resolution data (PSF FWHM=0 7). Moreover, the residuals in both data sets show the spiral arms and a minor merger (or a large clump) in the southern part of the galaxy, showing that a smooth axis-symmetric model can be used to unveil asymmetric features.

gmw_00006095.name: SUMSS J221330-475426
gmw_00006095.overview: SUMSS J221330-475426 is a distant BL Lacertae object associated with the source 3FGL J2213.6-4755 [arXiv:1903.10011]. It has a lower-limit redshift of 1.529, which is determined from narrow intervening absorption lines along its line of sight [arXiv:1903.10011].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SUMSS J221330-475426 as the optical counterpart of 3FGL J2213.6-4755, a BL Lac with a lower-limit redshift above 1.529 from intervening absorption.

quotes.quote: The remaining one is SUMSS J221330-475426, associated with 3FGL J2213.6-4755 and with a lower limit on its z of 1.529. This lower limit is due to the presence of some absorption lines from intervening systems along the line of sight.

It is worth mentioning that SUMSS J221330-475426, the associated counterpart the BCU 3FGL J2213.6-4755, is a BL Lac source with a measurable redshift of > 1.529 based on FeII and MgII absorption doublets. As these doublets are very narrow, we conclude they must be from an intervening system, thus making the z value a lower limit. This means it is one of the few distant BZBs lying within the end of the distance distribution for BL Lacs bzcat09 .

gmw_00006096.name: G25
gmw_00006096.overview: G25 is an intermediate-redshift active galactic nucleus host galaxy [arXiv:1310.3619]. Its emission line clouds exhibit an oxygen-to-hydrogen abundance ratio that is less than half of the solar value [arXiv:1310.3619].

sources.summary: The paper models G25 as an intermediate-redshift galaxy whose cloud profiles, oxygen abundance, and spectral fits place it at the limit of the high-shock models.

quotes.quote: To understand the trend of the line ratios emitted from the clouds, we show in Figs 2 and 3 the profile of the electron density, of the electron temperature and of the O ^ ++ /O, N ^+ /N and H ^+ /H ion fractional abundances across the clouds in the galaxy G25.

Some of the galaxies (G25, G26) show O/H < 0.5 solar in the neighborhood of AGNs and almost solar O/H in other AGN except for G53 and G107 at z=0.72 and 0.67, respectively, which also show N/H lower than solar by factors of 3 and 5.
It can be seen that in G53, G59, G63, G74 and G90 the models calculated by > 200 nicely fit the data, G25 and G26 are at the limit.

gmw_00006097.name: G26
gmw_00006097.overview: The galaxy G26 is characterized as an active galactic nucleus dominated system [arXiv:1310.3619]. It exhibits subsolar oxygen abundance in the neighborhood of its active nucleus, and its high-shock component is not observed [arXiv:1310.3619].

sources.summary: The paper finds subsolar oxygen near the AGN in G26, notes its high-shock component is not observed, and characterizes it as probably AGN dominated.

quotes.quote: Some of the galaxies (G25, G26) show O/H < 0.5 solar in the neighborhood of AGNs and almost solar O/H in other AGN except for G53 and G107 at z=0.72 and 0.67, respectively, which also show N/H lower than solar by factors of 3 and 5.
In G26 the high shock component is not observed.
G26 seems also AGN dominated.

gmw_00006098.name: G53
gmw_00006098.overview: G53 is a Type-2 emission-line galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.72 [arXiv:1212.3008, arXiv:1310.3619]. It is characterized as a weak active galactic nucleus experiencing intense star formation, with a nitrogen abundance lower than solar by a factor of three [arXiv:1212.3008, arXiv:1310.3619].

sources.summary: G53 is classified as Type-2 with narrow lines, but its BPT, MIR, and hard X-ray behavior point to a weak AGN with intense star formation.

quotes.quote: Finally, the galaxies fitted with Type-2 templates show narrow emission lines (G53, G56, G57, G93, G105, G106, and G110), as well as the SB-cont G27, whose SED was fitted with a Seyfert 2/starburst template in Ramos09 .

The position of the sources G53 and G59 in the BPT diagram is noticeable. We classified G53 as a Type-2 , and its L _X>L_X^ AGN , but it is well below the H II boundary in the more restrictive Kewley01 definition. This source is likely a weak AGN with intense star formation.
Exactly the opposite happens to G53, which is definitely in the H II region of the BPT, but has typical MIR and hard X-ray AGN fluxes.

sources.summary: The paper treats G53 as a modeled RA13 galaxy with an unmodeled red component, low nitrogen abundance at z=0.72, and acceptable high-shock spectral fits.

quotes.quote: The spectra lacking the data (e.g. the red component of the galaxies G53 and G74) were not modelled because the line flux ratios could not be corrected.

Some of the galaxies (G25, G26) show O/H < 0.5 solar in the neighborhood of AGNs and almost solar O/H in other AGN except for G53 and G107 at z=0.72 and 0.67, respectively, which also show N/H lower than solar by factors of 3 and 5.
It can be seen that in G53, G59, G63, G74 and G90 the models calculated by > 200 nicely fit the data, G25 and G26 are at the limit.

gmw_00006099.name: G60
gmw_00006099.overview: G60 is an active galactic nucleus (AGN) dominated galaxy [arXiv:1310.3619] that is classified as a Type-2 AGN [arXiv:1212.3008]. Its spectrum exhibits broad recombination-line components [arXiv:1212.3008], and its spectral energy distribution requires shock-dominated modeling to fit its X-ray data [arXiv:1310.3619].

sources.summary: G60 has broad recombination-line components and falls in the AGN region, although closer to the H II/AGN boundary than G63.

quotes.quote: For seven of the galaxies, namely G17, G55, G60, G63, G78, G90, and G107, an additional broad Gaussian component was needed to reproduce the observed line profiles of the permitted lines.

On the other hand, there are another three galaxies with broad lines detected in their spectra (G55, G60, and G107) which were not classified as Type-1 AGN from their SED fits. Note, however, that those broad components have FWHMs 1500 km s ^ 1 , and the luminosities of their broad H components are considerably lower than those of the galaxies G63, G78, and G90, as we show in Table comparison_broad .

The Type-2 s G60 and G93 also lie in the AGN region of the diagram, but with ratios more typical of the Seyfert/LINER transition zone. G60 shows broad H and H components in its spectrum, but appears much closer to the boundary between the H II and AGN regions than, for example, G63.

sources.summary: The paper says G60 requires additional shock-dominated SED modelling and concludes from spectra and SEDs that it is most likely AGN dominated.

quotes.quote: For all the sample galaxies, except for G60, G63 and G90, a black body radiation curve calculated by a single temperature T _ bb contribute to the modelling of nearly all the observed data by RA13.
Therefore we added in G60 and G107 diagrams the SED of shock dominated models calculated with a high in order to fit the X-ray data.
The SEDs confirm that G60 and G63 are AGN dominated.

gmw_00006100.name: G62
gmw_00006100.overview: G62 is classified as a starburst-dominated galaxy [arXiv:1212.3008, arXiv:1310.3619] that exhibits a simple starburst profile [arXiv:1310.3619]. It has low X-ray luminosity and lacks confirmed nuclear activity, suggesting its emission is driven by intense star formation [arXiv:1212.3008].

sources.summary: G62 has low X-ray luminosity, SB-dominated spectra, and no confirmed nuclear activity, leaving star formation or heavy obscuration as possible explanations.

quotes.quote: Only 8/28 sources have L _X<L_X^ AGN , namely G43, G47, G55, G59, G62, G76, G105 and G107. Thus, for these galaxies, either their X-ray and MIR emission are due to intense star formation, or to a heavily obscured AGN.
The galaxies classified as SB-dom (G26, G45, G62, and G91) show very similar spectra, with relatively faint H emission and H either in absorption or detected with very low signal-to-noise.

Summarising, in view of all the discussed diagnostic diagrams, X-ray luminosities and SED classification, we cannot confirm the presence of nuclear activity in 4/28 sources (14 of the sample): G47, G55, G62 and G107.

sources.summary: The paper adds G62 because its parameters differ from the other objects, identifies it as a simple starburst, and finds its line-ratio modelling starburst dominated.

quotes.quote: G62 was added to the sample because the characteristic parameters are different from those of the other objects, being a simple starburst.

G62 shows the characteristic parameters different from the bulge observed from the other sample objects (Fig. 3), but similar to those observed by Kobulnik Zaritsky (1999) from HII regions in compact galaxies (see Contini 2013c, in preparation), so it has been modelled only by a starburst dominated model.

The results of model calculations show that G59, G60, G63 are most likely AGN dominated, G62 is SB dominated while the other galaxies are ambiguously defined by the line ratios presented in RA13 spectra.

gmw_00006101.name: G63
gmw_00006101.overview: G63 is a Type-1 active galactic nucleus host galaxy [arXiv:1212.3008] that is dominated by its active nucleus [arXiv:1310.3619]. Its optical and near-infrared spectra exhibit broad permitted-line structures [arXiv:1212.3008] and double-peaked emission lines corresponding to different kinematic components [arXiv:1212.3008].

sources.summary: G63 is a Type-1 galaxy with broad optical or NIR components, including double-peaked and broad permitted-line structure in its spectra.

quotes.quote: Double-peaked emission lines corresponding to different kinematic components (G63).
For seven of the galaxies, namely G17, G55, G60, G63, G78, G90, and G107, an additional broad Gaussian component was needed to reproduce the observed line profiles of the permitted lines.

The three galaxies fitted with Type-1 templates (G17, G63, and G78; see Figure seds ) show broad components in their optical and/or NIR spectra. In fact, these are the only three galaxies with broad lines of FWHM > 3000 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper models both components of G63, reports enhanced nitrogen, and concludes from model calculations and SEDs that the galaxy is AGN dominated.

quotes.quote: Both blue and right component spectra of G63 are accounted for.

The results of model calculations show that G59, G60, G63 are most likely AGN dominated, G62 is SB dominated while the other galaxies are ambiguously defined by the line ratios presented in RA13 spectra.
The SEDs confirm that G60 and G63 are AGN dominated.

gmw_00006102.name: G90
gmw_00006102.overview: G90 is a mixed group of unresolved galaxies [arXiv:2108.09415] with a Seyfert 1, starburst, and ultraluminous infrared galaxy spectral energy distribution [arXiv:1212.3008]. The system features a strong contribution from star-heated dust [arXiv:1212.3008], high nitrogen abundance [arXiv:1310.3619], and a possible obscured active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2108.09415].

sources.summary: G90 has broad H and H components, a Sy1/starburst/ULIRG SED, strong star-heated dust contribution, and probably real but uncertain extreme star formation.

quotes.quote: G90 shows broad H and H components of FWHM 1800 km s ^ 1 , in agreement with its SED classification as a SB-cont : the fitted template for this galaxy is a Sy1/starburst/ULIRG SED.

The position of the galaxies G17 and G90 in Figure alonso is remarkable. They are the brightest 24 sources in the whole sample studied in Barmby06 13.72 0.01 and 14.51 0.01 mag at 24 for G17 and G90 respectively. and despite their strong H and H broad components, they lie well below the AGN area of the diagram, indicating a strong contribution from star-heated dust at 24 .

The position of the galaxies G63 and G90 in the two panels of Figure ha_mips_xrays confirms what we discussed in Section ha_sfr . G63 does not have a large L _ 24 /L _X value as it is the case for G90. Thus, the extreme SFR measured from its H luminosity is likely the result of an overestimation of the A _V , whereas in the case of G90 it is probably real, although affected by a large uncertainty. In addition, G90 has its H emission somehow affected by sky line contamination.

sources.summary: The paper reports high nitrogen in G90, treats it as needing extra blackbody components, and says high-shock models fit its data well.

quotes.quote: The O/H relative abundances are mostly solar (adopting 6.6 10 ^ 4 Allen 1976, 8.5 10 ^ 4 refers to Anders Grevesse 1989) in the gas heated and ionized by the starburst (Table 4), while N/H ranges from 3 times solar (G63 and G90) to a minimum of 0.2 solar in G107.

The temperature ranges between 2000 and 3000 K for all the galaxies except for G90 where T _ bb = 1000 K and T _ bb = 300 K are required to finish the fit of the NIR and FIR data, respectively (Table 5).
It can be seen that in G53, G59, G63, G74 and G90 the models calculated by > 200 nicely fit the data, G25 and G26 are at the limit.

sources.summary: The paper describes Galaxy ID 05229 as unresolved galaxies in the GTC-OTELO image, with infrared/X-ray emission, poor galaxy-template SED fitting, a possible obscured AGN, and competing redshift measurements.

quotes.quote: Galaxy ID 05229 is actually a mixed group of unresolved galaxies in the GTC-OTELO low-resolution image. This object is a bright infrared and X-ray source source irx-8 georgakakis10 , and OTELO galaxy templates poorly fit the observed SED, probably because the dominant object in the image may be an obscured AGN source CFRS-14.1157 zheng04 . The spectral redshift reported by matthews13 is 1.14825, but zheng04 find a redshift of 1.0106 that is in better agreement with both the SED fitting (0.85) and the neural network prediction (0.98).

gmw_00006103.name: G107
gmw_00006103.overview: G107 is a spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.67 [arXiv:1212.3008, arXiv:1310.3619]. It exhibits a very low nitrogen abundance [arXiv:1310.3619] and may host either a weak, diluted active galactic nucleus or intense star formation [arXiv:1212.3008].

sources.summary: G107 is described as a spiral/NG object with broad recombination components and composite diagnostics, consistent with a weak diluted AGN or intense star formation.

quotes.quote: Examples of spiral galaxies are G74 and G107, which were fitted with spiral templates in Ramos09 .

The galaxy G107, which shows broad components in its recombination lines, although with FWHMs 900 km s ^ 1 only, is located in the composite region between the Kauffmann03 and Kewley06 definitions. This agrees with our SED classification as a NG and with a possible star-forming origin of its X-ray luminosity Pereira11,Ranalli12 , as discussed above. Thus, G107 might be either a very weak AGN whose optical/IR emission is diluted by the host galaxy, or a spiral galaxy with intense star formation (see Figure acs and Table 1 in Ramos09 ).

Summarising, in view of all the discussed diagnostic diagrams, X-ray luminosities and SED classification, we cannot confirm the presence of nuclear activity in 4/28 sources (14 of the sample): G47, G55, G62 and G107.

sources.summary: The paper describes G107 as a z=0.67 object with very low nitrogen abundance and adds shock-dominated SED models to fit its X-ray data.

quotes.quote: The O/H relative abundances are mostly solar (adopting 6.6 10 ^ 4 Allen 1976, 8.5 10 ^ 4 refers to Anders Grevesse 1989) in the gas heated and ionized by the starburst (Table 4), while N/H ranges from 3 times solar (G63 and G90) to a minimum of 0.2 solar in G107.

Some of the galaxies (G25, G26) show O/H < 0.5 solar in the neighborhood of AGNs and almost solar O/H in other AGN except for G53 and G107 at z=0.72 and 0.67, respectively, which also show N/H lower than solar by factors of 3 and 5.
Therefore we added in G60 and G107 diagrams the SED of shock dominated models calculated with a high in order to fit the X-ray data.

gmw_00006104.name: 2MASX J04412715+2433110
gmw_00006104.overview: 2MASX J04412715+2433110 is a steeply inclined disk galaxy located at a distance of 53 Mpc [arXiv:1301.2591]. Near-infrared polarimetry reveals a multi-kiloparsec coherent magnetic field geometry that is predominantly parallel to the galaxy's disk [arXiv:1301.2591].

sources.summary: The paper maps near-infrared polarization across this inclined disk galaxy, removes Taurus foreground effects, and interprets the corrected polarization as coherent disk-parallel magnetic structure.

quotes.quote: Deep H  band (1.6  m) linear imaging polarimetry toward Taurus serendipitously included the galaxy 2MASX J04412715+2433110 with adequate sensitivity and resolution to map polarization across nearly its full extent. The observations revealed the galaxy to be a steeply inclined ( 75 ) disk type with a diameter, encompassing 90 of the Petrosian flux, of 4.2 kpc at a distance of 53 Mpc. Because the sight line passes through the Taurus Molecular Cloud complex, the foreground polarization needed to be measured and removed.

Corrected for the polarized foreground, the galaxy polarization values range from zero to 3 . The polarizations are dominated by a disk-parallel magnetic field geometry, especially to the northeast, while either a vertical field or single scattering of bulge light produces disk-normal polarizations to the southwest. The multi-kpc coherence of the magnetic field revealed by the infrared polarimetry is in close agreement with short wavelength radio synchrotron observations of edge-on galaxies, indicating that both cool and warm interstellar media of disk galaxies may be threaded by common magnetic fields.

gmw_00006105.name: NGC 0959
gmw_00006105.overview: NGC 0959 is a late-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:1004.3575] that contains a large central spheroidal structure, which may be a bulge or a pseudo-bulge [arXiv:1405.4854]. Gravitational interaction with its nearest projected group member, NGC 0949, may have produced an extinction ridge and a stellar population gradient in its disk [arXiv:1004.3575].

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 0959 as a late-type spiral, using multiwavelength pixel diagrams and extinction correction to reveal stellar-population structure, dust effects, a bar, and possible interaction-driven gradients.

quotes.quote: We present the results of a study of the late-type spiral galaxy NGC 0959, before and after application of the pixel-based dust extinction correction described in Tamura \ 2009 (Paper I). Galaxy Evolution Explorer ( GALEX ) far-UV (FUV) and near-UV (NUV), ground-based Vatican Advanced Technology Telescope (VATT) UBVR , and Spitzer /Infrared Array Camera (IRAC) 3.6, 4.5, 5.8, and 8.0 \ images are studied through pixel Color-Magnitude Diagrams (pCMDs) and pixel Color-Color Diagrams (pCCDs). We define groups of pixels based on their distribution in a pCCD of \ versus \ colors after extinction correction.

The group environment and/or gravitational interaction with NGC 0949---the group member nearest in projection on the sky---may well have played a role in producing the extinction ridge and the stellar population gradient in the disk of NGC 0959. We believe this certainly deserves further study. We have presented the results of a study of NGC 0959 using color composite images, a pCCD, and a pixel coordinate map, and demonstrated the importance and potential of a pixel-based extinction correction.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 0959 in its seven-galaxy dwarf sample, measuring stellar and gas kinematics and noting its spheroidal central structure, unresolved HI, and rotation-curve behavior.

quotes.quote: We present new constraints on the density profiles of dark matter (DM) halos in seven nearby dwarf galaxies from measurements of their integrated stellar light and gas kinematics. The gas kinematics of low mass galaxies frequently suggest that they contain constant density DM cores, while N-body simulations instead predict a cuspy profile. We present a data set of high resolution integral field spectroscopy on seven galaxies and measure the stellar and gas kinematics simultaneously.

In four cases, NGC 2552, NGC 2976, NGC 5204, and NGC 5949, the rounder structure is a compact nuclear star cluster that has negligible influence on the global kinematics of the galaxy. In the remaining three systems, NGC 959, UGC 2259, and UGC 11707 there are larger spheroidal structures that may be either bulges or, more likely, pseudo-bulges. Some of these transitions occur at the same radii where we seek to measure the DM density slopes, so the DM density slope and stellar axial ratio could be covariant at some level.

gmw_00006106.name: UGC 02259
gmw_00006106.overview: UGC 02259 is a low surface brightness, barred dwarf spiral galaxy [arXiv:1405.4854, arXiv:astro-ph/0212102] that contains a central spheroidal structure, likely a pseudo-bulge [arXiv:1405.4854]. Its rotation curve is characterized by a cuspy dark matter halo profile [arXiv:2310.20272] and a non-monotonic baryonic acceleration profile [arXiv:2307.09507].

sources.summary: The paper includes UGC 02259 in the dwarf-galaxy kinematic sample, treating its central spheroidal structure, HI profile, and agreement between stellar- and gas-traced rotation curves.

quotes.quote: We present new constraints on the density profiles of dark matter (DM) halos in seven nearby dwarf galaxies from measurements of their integrated stellar light and gas kinematics. The gas kinematics of low mass galaxies frequently suggest that they contain constant density DM cores, while N-body simulations instead predict a cuspy profile. We present a data set of high resolution integral field spectroscopy on seven galaxies and measure the stellar and gas kinematics simultaneously.

In four cases, NGC 2552, NGC 2976, NGC 5204, and NGC 5949, the rounder structure is a compact nuclear star cluster that has negligible influence on the global kinematics of the galaxy. In the remaining three systems, NGC 959, UGC 2259, and UGC 11707 there are larger spheroidal structures that may be either bulges or, more likely, pseudo-bulges. Some of these transitions occur at the same radii where we seek to measure the DM density slopes, so the DM density slope and stellar axial ratio could be covariant at some level.

sources.summary: The paper highlights UGC 2259 as an unambiguously barred dwarf/LSB galaxy whose steep inferred halo slope is tied to bar-driven disk dynamics and failure of the minimum-disk assumption.

quotes.quote: That any such classification remains uncertain is illustrated by the fact that none of the galaxies in the SMBB sample are classified in NED as unambiguously barred (Hubble type SB), except UGC 2259 [ Hubble type SB(s)dm ] . SMBB classify this galaxy as unbarred , even though the bar with perpendicular spiral arms is easily visible on the Digital Sky Survey image. The inner mass-density slope found for this galaxy is steep at = -0.86 0.18 .

As bars are disk dynamical features (e.g., lia2002 ), they imply a significant disk mass. Such heavy disks more than compensate for any underestimate of the slope which might be caused by the noncircular motions they induce. The minimum disk assumption fails, and with it the steep halo slopes inferred in this extreme limit.

sources.summary: UGC02259 is presented as an upward-hook galaxy with monotonic total acceleration and non-monotonic baryonic acceleration, causing its RAR track to peel upward before bending back.

quotes.quote: Finally, UGC02259 (green triangles) exhibits upward hooks. This galaxy has a monotonic \ that always increases as decreases ( p_ tot < 2) , but an \ profile that is non-monotonic, peaking at a finite radius where p_ bar =2 . As we follow \ from the outside in, it approaches its peak, such that p_ bar 2 (from below) whilst p_ tot < 2 , which drives m = (p_ tot 2)/(p_ bar 2) 1 . Such a steep positive slope means that its RAR peels steeply upward away from the average relation before hooking back towards m <0 as the \ profile begins to decline ( p_ bar > 2 ).

sources.summary: The paper treats UGC02259 as one of two clearly cuspy SPARC rotation curves, with a cuspy profile fit, a small or unresolved core size, and elevated c_200.

quotes.quote: Cuspy RCs Among the 21 most reliable SPARC RCs, two galaxies, namely, UGC12506 and UGC02259, are unmistakably characterized as cuspy in nature with values of -1.57 and -1.15, respectively. Notably, the NFW component of the best-fit cored profiles for these two RCs aligns precisely with their cuspy profile fits. In both cases, the core size ( r_c ) is either slightly less than or closely matches the radius at which the first data point is available. Additionally, the best-fit values of c_ 200 for UGC12506 and UGC02259 are 3 and 1.5 higher than the mean MCR, respectively, suggesting that these RCs exhibit an extremely cuspy nature.

gmw_00006107.name: WHDF-LAB-01
gmw_00006107.overview: WHDF-LAB-01 is a high-redshift galaxy classified as a quasar at a redshift of z=2.6 [arXiv:2107.00694]. It exhibits compact, symmetric far-infrared continuum emission with an optical extent that is approximately 1.47 times larger than its submillimeter dust extent [arXiv:2107.00694].

sources.summary: The paper identifies LAB-01 as a quasar-like WHDF submillimetre source, compares its optical and ALMA extents, and finds compact symmetric FIR continuum emission.

quotes.quote: LAB-01 the SIMBAD IDs for all these objects follow the " [ BHM2012 ] WHDF-LAB-01" SIMBAD ID naming convention. : This source has a HST-i+SPIES MIR counterpart classed in Shanks_SMGs as a quasar at z=2.6 based on detection of weak X-rays and the optical-MIR SED fit. The SED fit also gave A_V=1.75 0.25 mag and dust temperature, T=40 6K .

Figs. fig:HST_i , fig:H-band indicate that the counterpart is classed as a galaxy in HST-i with FWHM 1.8kpc and unresolved in ground-based H band. The ALMA image in Fig. fig:lab_sources (a) is reasonably symmetric with deconvolved image FWHM axis sizes of 1.4 1.1 kpc. LAB-01 thus has an 1.47 larger extent in the i band compared to its ALMA FIR continuum extent.

gmw_00006108.name: LAB-02
gmw_00006108.overview: LAB-02 is a high-redshift submillimetre source with an optical counterpart consistent with a quasar [arXiv:2107.00694]. The galaxy exhibits elongated dusty submillimetre emission that may represent a small disc or bar, though it lacks visible spiral features [arXiv:2107.00694].

sources.summary: The paper describes LAB-02 as a quasar-consistent submillimetre source with a resolved optical counterpart, elongated dusty submillimetre emission, and no detected spiral features.

quotes.quote: LAB-02 : The optical counterpart to this source was classed as being consistent with a quasar at z=3.1 0.25 on the basis of the SED fit with A_V=0.25 0.38 mag and T=39 9K . Fig. fig:HST_i shows that the counterpart is resolved with FWHM=1.5kpc. This compares to the elongated 2.3 0.8 kpc of the sub-mm source which lies at 7 kpc NE of the HST i source.

Clearly, in the sub-mm we are seeing a dusty disc that may be small enough to be classed as a bar, possibly similar to those seen by in some ALESS sources, although other interpretations cannot be ruled out e.g. a merger or a lensed galaxy. But again no spiral features are visible. This may be consistent with dust heating from the nucleus affecting features at a few kpc radius.

gmw_00006109.name: SPT 2332 53
gmw_00006109.overview: SPT 2332 53 is a high-redshift millimeter source located at a redshift of z=2.738 [arXiv:1206.4550]. The source is gravitationally lensed by a foreground galaxy cluster and resolves into three separate components at 350 micrometers [arXiv:1206.4550].

sources.summary: The paper describes SPT 2332 53 as a z=2.738 millimeter source lensed by a galaxy cluster, with extended LABOCA emission resolved into multiple SABOCA components.

quotes.quote: SPT 2332 53 has a redshift of z=2.738 , derived from Ly and ionized carbon (C iv 1549 ) in an optical spectrum with VLT/FORS2 from program (285.A 5034; PI: Chapman) and carbon monoxide CO(7--6) with APEX/Z-Spec. The redshift of the foreground lens, which is a cluster of galaxies, is z=0.403 . A paper providing a full characterization of this source, including unambiguous evidence for it being lensed by the cluster of galaxies, is in preparation (Vieira et al. ).

For SPT 2332 53 the LABOCA 870 m emission is clearly extended in the east-west direction, spanning 1' . At 350 m the source resolves into three separate, unresolved blobs. We denote these sources A, B and C, going from east to west (see Fig. \ figure:saboca-laboca-maps ).

gmw_00006110.name: SPT 0529 54
gmw_00006110.overview: SPT 0529 54 is a high-redshift galaxy that exhibits point-like emission at 870 microns [arXiv:1206.4550]. At 350 microns, the source is resolved into an extended structure with multiple peaks, indicating that it is strongly lensed [arXiv:1206.4550].

sources.summary: The paper finds SPT 0529 54 is resolved at 350 microns with multiple peaks, has point-like 870-micron emission, and shows morphology indicative of strong lensing.

quotes.quote: SPT 0529 54 appears to be resolved at 350 m , with S/N 3-4 emission extending to the north and east. This three-component substructure is enclosed within the area of a LABOCA beam, and the source appears point-like at 870 m .
Similarly in SPT 0529 54, the LABOCA source is resolved into an extended SABOCA source with multiple peaks, again indicative of strong lensing.

gmw_00006111.name: id=2112549
gmw_00006111.overview: The object id=2112549 is a high-redshift galaxy candidate at a redshift of z=8.2434 [arXiv:2310.03063]. Its spectrum displays a clear Lyman break at 1.1 micrometers and a strong oxygen emission line at 4.6 micrometers [arXiv:2310.03063].

sources.summary: The paper identifies id=2112549 as a spectroscopic high-redshift double-break galaxy candidate with detector-gap emission lines, a Lyman break, and O III 5007 at z=8.2434.

quotes.quote: We have one source (id=2112549) whose emission lines mostly fall inside the NIRSpec detector gap. Only one strong emission line at _ obs 4.6 \ m is found -- at the edge of the detector but detected at high significance in spite of the proximity to the detector edge. In addition to this line, the spectrum of this source also shows a clear break at _ obs 1.1 \ m.

This Lyman break would exactly match up with the strong emission line being Oiii _ 5007 at z=8.2434 . We thus fit a single Gaussian profile to the emission line, whose peak and part of blue wing lie on the detector. Since other strong emission lines that fall into the detector gap, such as H _ , are expected to be covered with the F410M/F444W filters, it is impossible to estimate the contributions of all emission lines to the total fluxes in these filters.

gmw_00006112.name: 1ES 2321 + 419
gmw_00006112.overview: The blazar 1ES 2321 + 419, also known as BZB J2323+4210, is a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1701.04305, arXiv:0903.2606]. It exhibits an X-ray variability component with a near periodicity of approximately 420 days [arXiv:0903.2606].

sources.summary: The paper rejects an old z=0.059 claim for BZB J2323+4210, detects narrow absorption at z=0.267, and interprets it as likely intervening rather than host-galaxy starlight.

quotes.quote: BZB J2323+4210 : From a poor optical spectrum perlman1996 claimed the detection of two starlight absorption features identified as Mg I (5175 )and Na I (5892 ) and they proposed at redshift z = 0.059. We disprove this redshift because the Na I absorption coincides with the telluric absorption at 6280 . shaw2013a does not confirm the above redshift either.

Our high SNR ( 200) spectrum is characterized by a power law emission ( F_ ^ ; = - 1.2). We clearly detect an absorption doublet at 5000 (EW 0.25 ) and an absorption line at 7465 . We identify these features as Ca II 3934, 3968 , and Na I 5892 absorption lines at z = 0.267. If these lines were ascribed to the starlight of the host galaxy we would expect to observe some modulation imprinted on the continuum, which are not present. Moreover this redshift appears remarkable inconsistent with the lower limits (z _ lim > 0.65) derived from the non detection of the host galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper identifies an approximately 420-day X-ray variability component in 1ES 2321 + 419, supported by several timing methods and interpreted through a helical relativistic jet model.

quotes.quote: Nonetheless, an apparently real periodic component of about 17 days was detected for the blazar AO 0235 + 164 and it was confirmed by discrete correlation function and periodogram analyses. For 1ES 2321 + 419 a component of variability with a near periodicity of about 420 days was detected by all of these methods. We discuss several possible explanations for these nearly periodic components and conclude that they most likely arise from the intersections of a shock propagating down a relativistic jet that possesses a helical structure.

The lower-left panel of Fig. \ 3 displays the SF for 2321 + 419, binned in 4 day intervals; following an almost flat region consistent with noise out to about 50 days the deepest dips are at about 425 and 850 days but are rather broad. To see whether this hint of a period is genuine, we performed other analyses on the same data set ( 3.2--3.4).

gmw_00006113.name: NGC 5291N
gmw_00006113.overview: NGC 5291N is a young, clumpy tidal dwarf galaxy candidate [arXiv:1509.05404, arXiv:1509.08873] that contains virtually no pre-existing stellar component [arXiv:astro-ph/0108513]. The galaxy is highly gaseous [arXiv:1509.08873], hosts a young starburst [arXiv:astro-ph/0703002], and exhibits disk kinematics dominated by rotation [arXiv:1509.05404].

sources.summary: NGC 5291N is one of the main tidal dwarf candidates, with decoupled velocity gradients, possible radial motions, high dispersion, and preference for a flat rotation curve.

quotes.quote: The three largest TDG candidates (NGC 5291N, 5291S, 5291SW) are associated with velocity gradients that are kinematically decoupled from the underlying debris (B07). In Sect. sec:3Dmodels we model the kinematics of these TDGs.

Some radial motions may be present in NGC 5291N and VCC 2062, as hinted to by the tilt of their minor-axis PV-diagrams. For these two objects, we built additional models including a global radial component ( V_ rad ). It is unclear whether the inclusion of V_ rad substantially improves the models, but we can constrain the ratio V_ rad /V_ rot to be smaller than 0.3. Hence, the disc kinematics is dominated by rotation and radial motions can be neglected to a first approximation. We stress that the inclusion of V_ rad has no effect on the measured values of V_ rot .
For NGC 5291N and NGC 7252NW, a flat rotation curve is preferable to a solid-body one, as indicated by the observed emission at high rotation velocities near the disc center.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 5291N as a young tidal dwarf analogue of high-redshift clumpy galaxies, mapping its gaseous, clumpy ISM, emission-line ratios, dust, and diffuse continuum.

quotes.quote: The object investigated in this paper, NGC 5291N (NED NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database. distance: 63.1 Mpc), was originally classified as a BCDG Maza91 . It however stands out as it shows no hint for an old stellar component Boquien07,Boquien10c , is very gaseous -- M _ gas is around 85 of the total mass Bournaud07 - and has a clumpy structure. Its metallicity, about half-solar thereafter DM98 Duc98 is higher than in classical BCDGs, but not so different than that of a number of distant star--forming galaxies with metallicities greater than 0.4 Z_ at z = 2 Erb06, Stark08, Steidel14, Zanella15 .

Our target, NGC 5291N, is the most massive of them. Similar to Tidal Dwarf Galaxies , NGC 5291N has a low dark matter (DM) content. As a matter of fact, NGC 5291N shares many properties with the 10^8 individual clumps, which formed within the gravitationally unstable gas-dominated disk of distant clumpy galaxies Zanella15 , and are also presumably dark-matter poor.

sources.summary: The paper reports CO detection in NGC5291N, treats it as part of the evolved NGC5291 TDG system, notes little pre-existing stellar content, and says its line width remains uncertain.

quotes.quote: For NGC5291N and NGC4676N CO(2-1) a first order (i.e. linear) baseline was subtracted. CO detections were obtained for the NGC 7252 West (hereafter NGC7252W), NGC 4676 North (hereafter NGC4676N), NGC 5291 North and NGC 5291 South (hereafter NGC5291N and NGC5291S), Stephan's Quintet source 'B' (hereafter NGC7319E), and very probably the NGC 4038/9 South (``The Antennae", hereafter NGC4038S) TDGs.

For the NGC5291 TDGs the stellar enhancement is total and the HI enhanced by a factor of a few, although it is still extended; they are clearly more evolved by these criteria than Arp245N. NGC7319E is not currently classifiable because we do not know whether it belongs to an extended optical structure or not.

Tidal features are not a homogeneous class -- some have virtually no pre-existing stellar component (e.g. NGC5291N) while others (e.g. Arp245N) have a significant contribution from disk stars. To take this into account, we tried to sum the masses of the gaseous and stellar components in the last panel and indeed the trend shows a smaller dispersion. Although the mass to light ratio, M/L _ B , certainly varies within the sample we chose a ratio of M/L _ B = 2 M/L_ B, , midway between that of young and evolved stellar populations. The ``Virial masses" of the sample span a larger range than the gas + star masses. Some of this may be due to the uncertain line width of NGC5291N.

sources.summary: The paper focuses on NGC 5291N as a bona-fide tidal dwarf complex, with MUSE observations and prior line-ratio and metallicity study.

quotes.quote: Three star-forming complexes (NGC 5291N, NGC 5291S and NGC 5291SW) are associated with strong \ concentrations that display a velocity gradient decoupled from the underlying collisional material, pointing to rotation within a self-gravitating potential well Bournaud2007, lelli2015gas . In this paper, we focus on NGC 5291N and NGC 5291S because no single-dish CO observations are available for NGC 5291SW.

MUSE data NGC 5291N was observed on 26 June 2014 (Program ID: 60.A-9320; PI: P.-A. Duc) without adaptive optics (AO) for a total exposure time of 1800 sec. These observations are presented in Fensch2016 , who provides an in-depth study of line ratios and gas metallicity. NGC 5291N was re-observed on 19 June 2017 during a MUSE AO commissioning run (Program ID: 60.A-9100(G)), but the AO system could not significantly improve on the external seeing, so we will not use these data.

sources.summary: NGC 5291N is identified with region 21, one of the brightest HI-ring knots and a possible dwarf-galaxy progenitor, with young starburst and spectral properties discussed.

quotes.quote: We observe that star-forming knots are spread over 180 kpc, all along the HI structure. They are actually located on or close to individual HI peaks. The brightest regions (in H , NUV and 8.0 m) are located near the apparent tip and points of maximum column density of the HI ring: they correspond to our regions 1, 3 and 21; the latter two, also known as NGC 5291S and NGC 5291N, have been discussed in the literature as the possible progenitors of dwarf galaxies, and are probably associated with gravitationally bound gas clouds bournaud2004a .

The IRS spectra of the two most luminous regions (regions 3 and 21 in this paper) also show variations in their PAH strengths and dust continuum shortwards of 5 m and above 9 m, which are not yet understood.

Combining the two criteria, we conclude that the regions that are currently the most active ones (i.e. region 1, 3 and 21) are all very young but should have had their SF peak already 5 10^6 years ago while the more quiescent ones may be a few 10 10^6 years older, still sustaining star formation but at a lower rate than before.

gmw_00006114.name: GLASS-5429
gmw_00006114.overview: GLASS-5429 is a high-redshift galaxy observed at redshift z=6 [arXiv:2208.04292]. Its modeled star formation began at or before z=12, allowing the galaxy to accumulate a substantial stellar mass of approximately 1 to 2 billion solar masses by its observation epoch [arXiv:2208.04292].

sources.summary: The paper uses GLASS-5429 as a worked high-redshift example whose modeled star formation begins by z=12 and accumulates substantial stellar mass by observation epoch.

quotes.quote: In Figure 7 we show in 3 columns 3 examples from Fig. 3 -- GLASS-5429, GLASS-3665, and GLASS-3459. For each star formation begins at or before z=12 and accumulates by OE to 1-2 10^9 .

Using GLASS-5429 (left) as an example, and starting at the bottom, has attempted to fit the observed SED (OE, z=6 ) with only stars born at z=12 and z=11 . The best fit is for a mass of 9 10^8 (right plot) at z=12 , and no z=11 contribution, producing an SED (magenta line -- center panel) that is a very poor fit to the observed NIRCam fluxes (black points with errors), as indicated by inspection and the high \ (left panel). In this case there is no contributed star formation at z=11 because its SED would be moderately blue, so there is no amount that would improve the fit to the moderately age population of the observed SED.

gmw_00006115.name: VCC 144
gmw_00006115.overview: VCC 144 is a blue compact dwarf galaxy located in the southern extension of the Virgo cluster [arXiv:2306.04513]. It is the optically faintest galaxy in its radio catalog and is currently undergoing an intense starburst [arXiv:2306.04513].

sources.summary: The paper describes VCC 144 as an optically faint blue compact dwarf undergoing intense starburst activity and possibly current galaxy formation in Virgo southern extension.

quotes.quote: VCC 144 VCC 144 is discussed in Brosch1997VCCCluster as a blue compact dwarf galaxy with an extended envelop which is currently undergoing an intense starburst. They name it as an example of galaxy formation currently taking place in the Southern extension of the Virgo cluster. It is the optically faintest galaxy in our radio catalog.

gmw_00006116.name: VCC 241
gmw_00006116.overview: VCC 241 is a small, edge-on irregular galaxy [arXiv:1206.6916, arXiv:2306.04513] that exhibits a cometary morphology with intense star-forming regions displaced from its older stellar distribution [arXiv:astro-ph/0311030]. Its low-surface-brightness radio emission extends westward, likely tracing cosmic ray electrons in stripped gas [arXiv:2306.04513].

sources.summary: The survey groups this Magellanic irregular with cometary systems whose off-center, intense star-forming regions are decoupled from the smoother old stellar light.

quotes.quote: UGC 7326 (classified Im), UGC 7866 (an IABm) and UGC 8098 (an SBm, also named NGC 4861; see also Conselice et al. who present ground-based and Hubble Space Telescope imaging of this galaxy) all show highly asymmetric or `cometary' morphologies (Fig. Figd ) in their continuum-subtracted H + [ N ii ] images, with extremely intense star formation centres which are displaced very significantly from the centres of the old stellar distributions. This is reflected in long central troughs in the EW curves, with sharp rises to very high values when the star formation centres are reached (all growth curves are centred on the peaks of the old stellar light in the R band images).

The decoupling between the smooth old stellar distribution and the highly clumped and asymmetric sites of ongoing star formation is particularly marked in these galaxies. The overall EW values for these three galaxies lie in the range 5--20 nm, much higher than is typical for even late-type spiral galaxies. Local EW values in apertures centred on the H ii complexes lie in the range 50-100 nm for these galaxies.

sources.summary: The LOFAR maps show VCC 241 as an irregular galaxy with low-surface-brightness radio emission and a westward extension likely tied to stripped gas.

quotes.quote: VCC 241 (IC 3105) This irregular galaxy shows low-surface brightness emission in our LOFAR maps. In the south of the galaxy, the radio emission appears to extend towards west. This feature is coincident with extra-planar regions traced by VESTIGE (Boselli priv. comm.). Thus, it is likely that the low-frequency radio continuum emission distribution traces cosmic ray electrons advected due to ram-pressure and/or tracing the star-formation in the stripped gas.

sources.summary: The appendix describes IC 3105 as a small edge-on galaxy, reports agreement with Arecibo H I measurements, and derives its H I mass.

quotes.quote: HIPASS J1217+12 : IC 3105 is a small edge-on galaxy at a distance of 14 Mpc. Our \ value agrees well with the Arecibo \ measurements by Schneider et al. (1990). We derive an \ mass of 3.2 10^8 .

gmw_00006117.name: VCC 664
gmw_00006117.overview: VCC 664 is a late-type spiral galaxy located in the Virgo cluster [arXiv:2306.04513]. It exhibits low-resolution radio-continuum contours elongated to the west, suggesting the galaxy is undergoing a ram-pressure stripping event [arXiv:2306.04513].

sources.summary: The paper reports the first radio-continuum detection of VCC 664, interpreting its westward low-resolution contours as evidence of ram-pressure stripping.

quotes.quote: VCC 664 (IC 3258) To our knowledge, we detect this late-type spiral galaxy for the first time in the radio continuum. It is among the optically faintest Virgo cluster members in our catalog. The 3 contours in the low-resolution map are elongated towards the west. The orientation of the tail opposite to the cluster center and the lack of any associated stellar component in the optical image suggest that the galaxy is suffering a ram pressure stripping event. This could also explain the deficiency of the object Koppen2018RamApproach .

gmw_00006118.name: VCC 699
gmw_00006118.overview: VCC 699 is a star-forming dwarf galaxy located in the Virgo cluster [arXiv:2306.04513]. It exhibits a very high star-formation rate and a relatively small mass, with a LOFAR detection that may indicate a galactic outflow or an orbit moving away from the cluster center [arXiv:2306.04513].

sources.summary: VCC 699 is discussed as a Virgo star-forming dwarf whose offset LOFAR detection may trace an orbit away from the cluster center or a galactic outflow.

quotes.quote: VCC 699 (IC 3268) This galaxy is another Virgo star-forming dwarf galaxy from Grossi2016Star-formingDust sample. The LOFAR detection is slightly offset from the optical image but towards the projected center of the Virgo cluster. If real, this would imply an orbit moving away from the cluster center. In view of the relative small mass and very high star-formation rate, interpretation as an galactic outflow would be even more compelling.

gmw_00006119.name: VCC 1532
gmw_00006119.overview: VCC 1532 is a galaxy newly identified as a candidate for ongoing ram-pressure stripping [arXiv:2306.04513]. Radio maps of the galaxy reveal a radio emission gradient on the edge facing the cluster center, with a tail extending in the opposite direction [arXiv:2306.04513].

sources.summary: VCC 1532 is newly flagged as an ongoing ram-pressure stripping candidate because LOFAR shows a leading-edge radio gradient and opposite-side tail.

quotes.quote: VCC 1532 (IC 800) The LOFAR maps of VCC 1532 reveal a gradient of the radio emission at the edge of the galaxy facing the cluster center, and a tail in the opposite direction. To our best knowledge, the galaxy was not previously reported to show signs of ram-pressure stripping. Thus, we note it as a new candidate for ongoing ram-pressure stripping.

gmw_00006120.name: VCC 1686
gmw_00006120.overview: VCC 1686 is a star-forming dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2306.04513] that exhibits bright, polarized radio emission [arXiv:astro-ph/0510392]. It features a southern spur of radio and H-alpha emission [arXiv:2306.04513], which may represent a large-scale outflow [arXiv:2306.04513] or indicate a potential interaction with NGC 4569 [arXiv:astro-ph/0510392].

sources.summary: The paper detects polarized radio emission from IC 3583, notes fields oriented toward NGC 4569, and suggests a possible interaction or faint radio bridge between the galaxies.

quotes.quote: At the position of the dwarf galaxy IC 3583 bright total power emission is seen. The disk of NGC 4569 and both lobes show significant polarization at 4.85 GHz (Fig. tp6 , pi6 ). In the disk the B-vectors (i.e. E-vectors rotated by 90 ) are parallel to the disk plane, but in the extended radio structures the B-vectors are mostly perpendicular to the disk. The mean polarization degree in the disk is 5 increasing to 15 in the extraplanar radio-emitting regions. The radio emission from IC 3583 is polarized as well, with a degree of polarization of about 12 . The B-vectors are oriented towards NGC 4569.

On this image IC 3583 is separated from NGC 4569. Our observations at 8.35 GHz show that the polarized emission on the western and NW disk side is concentrated in three separated features (Fig. pi3 ): the most northern polarized peak is associated with IC 3583 and the ridge of polarized emission seen at 4.85 GHz west of the disk appears to be composed of two regions at the distance of about 3 from the centre of NGC 4569. The fact that they apparently coincide with regions showing little total power emission is due to a five times higher noise level in total power channels (due to confusion with background sources and an unpolarized atmospheric noise). We can thus detect significant polarization even where the total power signal is weak and very noisy. Both polarization peaks west of the disk show B-vectors perpendicular to the disk plane. The map at 1.4 GHz made from the line-free channels of the H i observations by Vollmer et al. ( ) has the highest angular resolution of our data, but contains no polarization channels. The eastern lobe seems again to be a detached structure, a thin radio bridge connecting it to the disk of NGC 4569 is also confirmed (Fig. tp20 ). The western extension has a more complex structure and seems to be connected to the disk. In its southern part a relatively narrow ridge of total power emission extends away from the disk. It coincides roughly with the southern polarized extension seen west of the disk at 8.35 GHz (Fig. pi3 ). Bright radio emission from IC 3583, which might be connected to that of NGC 4569 via another putative faint radio bridge, is also visible in Fig. tp6 and tp3 . The possible interactions between the galaxies has already been discussed by Tsch oke et al. ( ) mentioning the faint southern H spur, seen in the H + NII image of IC 3583, pointing towards NGC 4569. This is confirmed by a new Fabry Perot H image and velocity field obtained for these galaxies (Chemin et al. ).

sources.summary: The paper discusses VCC 1686 as a star-forming dwarf with a southern LOFAR spur and possible large-scale outflow rather than tidal interaction with VCC 1690.

quotes.quote: VCC 1686 (IC 3583) VCC 1686 has projected location very close to VCC 1690, but both galaxies differ by more than 1300 km s ^ 1 in radial velocity, making a tidal interaction between the two galaxies very unlikely. Still, there is spur of LOFAR emission in the south of VCC 1686 pointing towards VCC 1690. This feature has also a counterpart in at least two independent H maps of VCC 1686. The origin of the H feature is unclear, but the radio emission is more extended than the optical image of the galaxies over the whole quadrant, so maybe this is a large-scale outflow of VCC 1686. This would be consistent with the high star-formation rate of this dwarf galaxy.

gmw_00006121.name: VCC 1791
gmw_00006121.overview: VCC 1791 is a low-mass star-forming dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2306.04513]. Its radio emission extends beyond its optical body, suggesting the presence of an outflow, with the brightest radio peak coinciding with three blue compact knots [arXiv:2306.04513].

sources.summary: The paper treats VCC 1791 as a low-mass star-forming dwarf whose radio emission extends beyond its optical body, suggesting an outflow.

quotes.quote: VCC 1791 (IC 3617) VCC 1791 also belongs to the Grossi2016Star-formingDust sample. It has a relatively high starformation rate (but less than that of VCC 1686) but also a relatively low mass. Therefore the extent of the radio emission beyond the optical body of the galaxy is suggestive of an outflow. The brightest peak of the radio emission is coinciting with three very blue compact knots.

gmw_00006122.name: SDSS J043703.67+245606.8
gmw_00006122.overview: SDSS J043703.67+245606.8 is an Sb-type spiral galaxy located approximately 70 Mpc away [arXiv:2101.07932, arXiv:1705.09762]. It features a boxy bulge, two counterclockwise-wound spiral arms [arXiv:1705.09762], and a central supermassive black hole whose velocity is offset from the host galaxy [arXiv:2101.07932].

sources.summary: The paper describes 0437 as a disturbed Sb spiral whose SMBH velocity is offset from the host galaxy, possibly from merger, binary, or recoil dynamics.

quotes.quote: One galaxy from the Pesce_2018 sample -- SDSS J043703.67+245606.8, hereafter 0437 -- showed a statistically significant ( > 5 ) difference between the SMBH and host galaxy line-of-sight velocities; Pesce_2018 thus identified 0437 as a promising candidate for hosting either a recoiling or binary SMBH. 0437 is an approximately Sb-type spiral galaxy located at a distance of 70 Mpc Greene_2016,Pjanka_2017 . As measured by the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) Here we quote the quantities compiled in the NASA-Sloan Atlas, http://nsatlas.org/ . , 0437 has an band absolute AB magnitude of M_r = -21.37 and an estimated stellar mass of 7.2 10^ 10 M _ . The megamaser system in 0437 was mapped by hereafter Gao_2017 Gao_2017 , who also modeled the maser rotation curve and determined an SMBH velocity of 4818 10.5 . Pesce_2018 used a SDSS spectrum to measure the recession velocity of 0437 to be 4887.6 7.1 .

Multiple lines of evidence -- including the different inferred systemic velocities on different spatial scales, the ``anomalous'' spectral structure, and the gradient in stellar systemic velocity with radius -- point to the conclusion that the galaxy 0437 has been dynamically perturbed sometime in the recent past, likely through an interaction with another galaxy. Of particular interest is the apparent difference between the systemic velocity of the SMBH and that of any other dynamical tracer, indicating that the SMBH in this galaxy is in motion with respect to the surrounding material. Pesce_2018 explored plausible causes of such relative motion, ultimately settling on three possibilities: (1) the SMBH originates from an external galaxy that is in the process of merging with 0437; (2) the SMBH is part of a binary system, and the velocity offset we observe is the result of its orbital motion; or (3) the SMBH is recoiling from a recent merger event.

sources.summary: The paper treats J 0437+2456 as a faint likely Sb galaxy with high inclination, large spiral arms, a boxy bulge, and a 110 pc central region consistent with a small bulge.

quotes.quote: J 0437+2456 The luminosity distance to J 0437+2456 is 70 Mpc 2015Pesce . It is a faint, probably Sb-type galaxy with no Hubble Type in the literature. The inclination is quite high, 60 .

Two counterclockwise-wound spiral arms are clearly visible extending from a ``boxy'' (peanut-shaped) bulge on larger scales. The central region of J 0437+2456 is marked by a flattened maximum in position angle profile at 0.4 arcsec ( 110 pc), corresponding to a flat region in eccentricity. This region corresponds to an elliptical structure in the structure map and seems consistent with a small-scale bulge.

sources.summary: The paper fits J0437+2456 as an inclined megamaser disk whose maser groups are not horizontally aligned, fixing the black-hole offset and finding no azimuthal scatter needed.

quotes.quote: In this work we perform a Bayesian statistical fit to estimate the mass-to-distance ratio and the recessional redshift of 10 different black holes hosted at the centre of active galactic nuclei, namely the galaxies NGC 5765b, NGC 6323, UGC 3789, CGCG 074-064, ESO 558-G009, NGC 2960, NGC 6264, NGC 4388, J0437+2456 and NGC 2273. Our general relativistic method makes use of the positions in the sky and frequency shift observations of water megamasers circularly orbiting the central black hole on their accretion disks.

J0437+2456 The inclination angle for this maser disk is of _0 = 81 °, hence the three groups of masers do not seem horizontally aligned. The y_0 offset was fixed at the geometric center of the highly frequency-shifted masers y_0 = -0.040 mas. By varying the y_0 position along the height of the maser disk we found variations in M/D up to 2 , which is inside the error of the posterior M/D . For this megamaser system there is no need to consider a spread in the azimuthal angle since the statistical fit with =0 already renders a reduced ^2 = 1.038 .

gmw_00006123.name: CDFS-4417
gmw_00006123.overview: CDFS-4417 is a high-redshift galaxy at a redshift of z = 3.472 [arXiv:1912.10716] with a stellar metallicity of approximately 0.2 solar [arXiv:1112.2403]. It exhibits intense, dust-obscured star formation [arXiv:1403.0743], though the presence of an active galactic nucleus cannot be firmly excluded [arXiv:1912.10716].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes the individual CDFS-4417 spectrum, fits UV metallicity indicators, finds roughly 0.2 solar stellar metallicity, and compares its gas-phase metallicity with the combined sample.

quotes.quote: The spectrum of the CDFS-4417 galaxy was good enough to be used individually. A series of simulations were also used to estimate the uncertainties on the continuum level in each target galaxy.
