The object has an ionised gas mass 3.8 10^ 5 M_ . Moreover, the motion of the gas is well described by a gas in circular orbit in the plane of a disk and is being affected by interaction wtih Mrk 1172. We conclude that SDSS J020536.84-081424.7 is most likely a dwarf irregular galaxy ( ).

From the results summarized above we conclude that the faint nebular emission line region close in projection to Mrk 1172 is actually a gas rich low-metallicity dwarf galaxy, most likely a dwarf irregular.

gmw_00006200.name: Mrk 1172
gmw_00006200.overview: Mrk 1172 is a massive early-type galaxy with a stellar mass of approximately 1.2 × 10^{11} M_{☉} [arXiv:2106.14925]. Its stellar content is dominated by an old stellar population of about 10 billion years, and it is gravitationally interacting with a nearby dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2106.14925].

sources.summary: The paper treats Mrk 1172 as a massive early-type galaxy with old stellar populations, a measured stellar mass, and kinematic evidence for interaction with the nearby dwarf galaxy.

quotes.quote: In this work we characterise the properties of the object SDSS J020536.84-081424.7, an extended nebular region with projected extension of 14 14 kpc ^2 in the line of sight of the ETG Mrk 1172, using unprecedented spectroscopic data from MUSE. We perform a spatially resolved stellar population synthesis and estimate the stellar mass for both Mrk 1172 (1 10^ 11 M _ ) and our object of study ( 3 10^ 9 M_ ). While the stellar content of Mrk 1172 is dominated by an old ( 10 Gyr) stellar population, the extended nebular emission has its light dominated by young to intermediate age populations (from 100 Myr to 1 Gyr) and presents strong emission lines such as: H , [ O iii ] , 4959, 5007 , H , [ N ii ] , 6549, 6585 \ and [ S ii ] , 6717, 6732 .

We estimate the values of 3.0 10^ 9 M_ for \ and 1.2 10^ 11 M_ for Mrk 1172. In the case of the latter, the order of magnitude of this value is coherent to what is expected for massive ETG like Mrk 1172, and is in agreement with a previous estimates for the stellar mass for this galaxy 8.91 10^ 10 M_ , omand2014 .

From this fit we observe that the position angle of the line of the nodes and the disc inclination are similar for both galaxies, thus indicating that the motion of the gas is being affected by the ETG. We thus conclude that both galaxies are interacting.

gmw_00006201.name: PGC 26148
gmw_00006201.overview: PGC 26148 is a low-surface-brightness galaxy [arXiv:1203.4284] that features a bright classical bulge and a distinct bar structure [arXiv:1607.01889]. The galaxy's inner rotation curve displays a waving pattern attributed to retrograde orbits [arXiv:1203.4284], while flat color gradients suggest stellar mixing across its bulge, bar, and disk [arXiv:1607.01889].

sources.summary: The paper reports VLT observations of PGC 26148, confirms its low-surface-brightness disk, and describes an unusual inner rotation curve consistent with barred-galaxy retrograde orbits.

quotes.quote: The photometric and spectroscopic observations of PGC 26148 and PGC 37759 were carried out with the Very Large Telescope (VLT) of the European Southern Observatory (ESO) at Paranal Observatory on 2006 March 23-25 and 2008 February 17-18. The two galaxies were observed with the UT1 unit mounting the FOcal Reducer and low dispersion Spectrograph 2 (FORS2).
It is B-=1.20 for PGC 26148 and B-=1.21 for PGC 37759, and therefore ^0_ 0,B =22.61 and ^0_ 0,B =22.62 mag/arcsec ^2 respectively.

The rotation curve along the major axis of PGC 26148 is characterized by negative values ( v 10 km s ^ 1 ) in the innermost 1 arcsec, where the velocity dispersion shows a decrease and h_4 peaks to 0.1. Further out, the rotation velocity increases to a maximum v 20 km s ^ 1 at 4 arcsec and then decreases to zero at the last observed radius. This waving pattern in the rotation curve has been detected in several barred galaxies betgal97 and it is due to the presence of retrograde orbits wozpfe97 .

sources.summary: The paper says CGCG 006-023 was classified as unbarred in NED but has a clear bar and bright classical bulge, with flat bulge-bar and disk-bar colors suggesting stellar mixing.

quotes.quote: CGCG 006-023- Classified as not barred in NED. But has a bright, classical bulge and a clear bar.

(i) For the galaxies CGCG 381-048, UGC 11754, PGC 68495, UGC 10405, UGC 5035, 0223-0033, UGC 9087, UGC 3968, 1300+0144, PGC 60365, CGCG 006-023, 1252+0230 the (J-K _s ) color of their bulge and bar regions are almost the same i.e. they have a flat color gradient. We can conclude that the bulge and bar have almost similar stellar populations and low dust content.

(iii) In some galaxies like CGCG 381-048, NGC 5905, UGC 11754, 0223-0033, UGC 3968, PGC 60365, CGCG 006-023 the disk and the bar show similar colors in their bars and bulges. In these galaxies bar might have helped the mixing of stellar population and metallicity.

gmw_00006202.name: PGC 37759
gmw_00006202.overview: PGC 37759 is a low-surface-brightness disk galaxy [arXiv:1203.4284]. It exhibits regular kinematics, with a rotation velocity that peaks at approximately 140 km/s before remaining constant at larger radii, and a velocity dispersion that decreases from 70 km/s in the center to 40 km/s outwards [arXiv:1203.4284].

sources.summary: The paper reports VLT observations of PGC 37759, confirms its low-surface-brightness disk, and describes regular kinematics with flat outer rotation and declining velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: The photometric and spectroscopic observations of PGC 26148 and PGC 37759 were carried out with the Very Large Telescope (VLT) of the European Southern Observatory (ESO) at Paranal Observatory on 2006 March 23-25 and 2008 February 17-18. The two galaxies were observed with the UT1 unit mounting the FOcal Reducer and low dispersion Spectrograph 2 (FORS2).
It is B-=1.20 for PGC 26148 and B-=1.21 for PGC 37759, and therefore ^0_ 0,B =22.61 and ^0_ 0,B =22.62 mag/arcsec ^2 respectively.

PGC 37759 shows a regular kinematics. The rotation velocity reaches a maximum of about 140 km s ^ 1 at about 8 arcsec from the centre and it remains constant at larger radii. No rotation is observed along the minor axis. The velocity dispersion is about 70 km s ^ 1 in the centre and decreases to 40 km s ^ 1 outwards.

gmw_00006203.name: B4
gmw_00006203.overview: B4 is a high-redshift galaxy candidate identified through a blind carbon monoxide line detection [arXiv:1408.3585]. It has two possible photometric-redshift counterparts, with the most probable counterpart placing the galaxy at a redshift of z=4.41 via the CO(4-3) transition [arXiv:1408.3585].

sources.summary: The paper treats B4 as a robust blind CO-line candidate with two possible photometric-redshift counterparts, favoring the zphot 3.92 interpretation and CO(4-3) at z=4.41.

quotes.quote: B4 Has no counterpart in the D14 catalogue. There are two possible counterparts within 1.5 in the photometric redshift catalogue (henceforth FLY99) with z_ phot 3.92 and z_ phot 0.48 . Given the quoted uncertainties of 0.1 (1+z_ phot ) this could therefore correspond to either ID within 1 uncertainties, corresponding to either the CO( 3 2 ) or CO( 4 3 ) transition in the first case or the CO( 1 0 ) transition in the second. Given the volumes searched at these relative redshifts the z_ phot 3.92 counterpart is the more probable, with the CO( 4 3 ) transition closer to the frequency of the line in our data.

The final detection has two possible counterparts in the FLY99 photometric redshift catalogue within 1.5 with redshifts that are within 1 of the CO detection. The most likely of these has the detection represent the CO( 4 3 ) transition at a redshift of z= 4.41 .

gmw_00006204.name: PKS 2233 148
gmw_00006204.overview: PKS 2233 148 is a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1808.06138] with an optical and near-infrared spectral energy distribution dominated by synchrotron emission [arXiv:astro-ph/0111171]. On parsec scales, the active nucleus drives a conical radio jet [arXiv:1808.06138] that exhibits synchrotron self-absorbed core shifts [arXiv:1808.06138].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes PKS 2233 148 as a BL Lac object, finding a conical parsec-scale jet, synchrotron self-absorbed core shifts, evolving spectra, and a distant gamma-ray production zone.

quotes.quote: We study the jet physics of the BL Lac object PKS 2233 148 making use of synergy of observational data sets in the radio and ray energy domains. The four-epoch multi-frequency (4--43 GHz) VLBA observations focused on the parsec-scale jet were triggered by a flare in rays registered by the Fermi LAT on April 23, 2010. We also used 15 GHz data from the OVRO 40-m telescope and MOJAVE VLBA monitoring programs. Jet shape of the source is found to be conical on scales probed by the VLBA observations setting a lower limit of about 0.1 on its unknown redshift.

Nuclear opacity is dominated by synchrotron self-absorption, with a wavelength-dependent core shift r_ core [ mas ] 0.1 _ [ cm ] co-aligned with the innermost jet direction. The turnover frequency of the synchrotron spectrum of the VLBI core shifts towards lower frequencies as the flare propagates down the jet, and the speed of this propagation is significantly higher, about 1.2 mas yr ^ 1 , comparing to results from kinematics of jet components measured in the source previously.

sources.summary: The paper treats PKS 2233 148 as a BL Lac candidate with synchrotron-dominated optical/NIR emission, observed in J, H, and K bands but lacking enough comparison-star data for firm variability conclusions.

quotes.quote: PKS 2233 148: A candidate for a BL Lac (according to Padovani Giommi (1995)), at z>0.609 . (The redshift of z=0.325 quoted in Johnston et al. \ (1995) and on NED The NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database, http://nedwww.ipac.caltech.edu/ appears to be due to a mis-reading of a table in Schmidt Green (1983).) This has a similar optical/NIR SED to PKS 2240 260, also dominated by synchrotron emission.

It must be noted here that PKS 2233 148 did not have a second comparison star in the small ( 2 ) field of view of CASPIR. This means that the full variability analysis cannot be done for this source. However, a light-curve for m is presented (Fig. fig-idv3 ), using the one available star.

gmw_00006205.name: host galaxy of SN 2017dwh
gmw_00006205.overview: The host galaxy of SN 2017dwh is an extremely faint dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1810.11051]. It is characterized by a low metallicity, estimated at approximately 12 + log(O/H) = 7.6 [arXiv:1810.11051].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SN 2017dwh's host as an extremely faint, low-metallicity SLSN-I host and notes that its unusual environment may be connected to the event's peculiar spectra.

quotes.quote: Finally, we note that SN 2017dwh occurred in a very faint galaxy. In Section sec:LC we described our late-time observations which revealed a non-varying source at the position of SN 2017dwh. We find the following magnitudes for this source (corrected for Galactic extinction): m_ g = 25.27 0.10 , m_ = 25.45 0.16 , and m_ i = 25.30 0.14 . Using color transformations from Jordi2006 we find an absolute B band luminosity of M_ B = -13.5 mag, indicating that the host galaxy of SN 2017dwh is the faintest detected SLSN-I host galaxy Lunnan2014,Leloudas2015,Perley2016,Schulze2018 .

Using the luminosity-metallicity relationship for dwarf galaxies found by Lee2006 we estimate a metallicity of 12 + log(O/H) 7.6 0.08 Z_ Asplund2009 . Whether the environmental properties of SN 2017dwh's host galaxy are linked to its peculiar spectral properties is difficult to assess with a single event, though we note that sample studies have not confirmed correlations between host galaxy and magnetar/explosion properties Nicholl2017,DeCia2018 first suggested by Chen2017 Chen2017 suggested that SLSNe-I with the fastest magnetar spin periods are found in the most metal-poor galaxies, which is the opposite of what our findings imply. . However, it is suggestive that such an unusual SLSN-I occurred in such an extreme environment.

gmw_00006206.name: 4C 39.25
gmw_00006206.overview: The flat-spectrum radio quasar 4C 39.25 [arXiv:1107.5456, arXiv:1712.06243] features a bent VLBI jet [arXiv:1005.2177] extending toward the southeast [arXiv:1410.6653]. This active galaxy exhibits multiwavelength variability [arXiv:1005.2177], including millimeter flux density fluctuations [arXiv:1107.5456] and radio quasi-periodicity [arXiv:1005.2177].

sources.summary: The paper monitors 0923+392 as an FSRQ whose millimeter flux fell and recovered over years, with double-peaked flux structure, spectral-index changes, and simultaneous bands.

quotes.quote: 0923+392 (4C +39.25) The 3-mm (1.3-mm) flux densities of this flat spectrum radio quasar (FSRQ) dropped from 6 Jy ( 3 Jy) in 1996 to 3 Jy ( 1.5 Jy) in 2003 and have been rising again since then to present-day levels of 4.5 Jy ( 3 Jy). For the flux density decrease from 1996 to 2003 we find characteristic (exponential) timescales of 5--7 years. The 3-mm flux distribution shows a double peak profile that can be described by two Gaussian components separated by 1.6 Jy. The 1.3-mm flux distribution shows an asymmetry indicative of a second component but no significant secondary peak.

The spectral index is 0.8 in the first years of monitoring before 2000 and steepens to 1 around 2002. The index then flattens again until reaching 0.6 in the time window 2007--2010. There appears to be no correlation between spectral index and 3-mm flux. Even though we find the steepest spectral indices for the lowest source fluxes, we find similar variations in (by 0.2) within the same flux ranges. The 1.3-mm and 3-mm lightcurves are simultaneous within the 3 confidence interval.

sources.summary: The paper finds 0923+392 has a southeast jet, mixed EVPA orientations, and opposite transverse RM gradients in its inner jet/core and farther jet.

quotes.quote: 0923+392 Figure fig:pmaps1 d shows our 7.9 GHz polarization map for 0923+392. Note that this represents the relatively rare case when the map peak does not correspond to the core; the core is located at the northwest end of the observed structure, whereas the jet structure extends toward the southeast [ see the spectral-index maps presented by Zavala Taylor (2003) and Hovatta et al. (2014) ] . The jet EVPAs are roughly aligned with the jet near the jet ridgeline, becoming more orthogonal or oblique near the southern edge of the jet.

Our RM map shows a gradient transverse to the jet in the inner jet/core region (northwest end of structure), as well as a transverse gradient in the opposite direction several mas out in the jet (southeast end of structure). A similar structure is also visible in the RM map of 0923+392 presented by Zavala Taylor (2003). Figure fig:0923RM also shows slices taken roughly perpendicular to the jet direction plotted together with their errors. The transverse RM gradients are monotonic, and have significances of approximately 3.9 in the inner jet/core region and 3.4 further out in the jet.

sources.summary: The paper presents 4C 39.25 as a flat-spectrum radio quasar target with a peculiar core-jet structure recovered with high flux after FPT-square calibration.

quotes.quote: 4C 39.25, at an angular separation of 20.2 from OJ 287, classified as a flat spectrum radio quasar at a redshift of z=0.695 2004AJ....128..502A , and with a flux density of about 2.5 Jy at 86 GHz, has a well--known peculiar core--jet structure, where its brightest component even at cm wavelengths corresponds to the optically--thin jet, whereas the additional dimmer structure on the west corresponds to its radio--core e.g. 1997A&A...327..513A . In Figure fig:fpt2pair2 (left) we show the scans where these two sources were observed. OJ 287 was observed for 7 scans within 8 hours, whereas 4C 39.25 was observed for 6 scans during 6 hours.

The gap between the scans of these two sources range from few minutes to two hours, making such observation particularly inadequate for temporal interpolation. We calibrated the phases of OJ 287 and applied the solutions (excluding the first scan) to 4C 39.25. This leads to quite well aligned phases around 0 , with an offset within 50 , similar to the one obtained for the previous source pair. The main structure is properly retrieved with over 3- confidence level and the flux recovery is around 97 indicating that, even for such large angular separation, phase compensation is feasible after FPT--square.

sources.summary: The paper describes 0923+392 as a quasar with multiwavelength variability, radio quasi-periodicity, a bent VLBI jet, unresolved VLA emission, and non-thermal infrared colors.

quotes.quote: * 0923+392 (4C 39.25) (quasar, z=0.699 ): This source exhibits variability at different wavelengths (almost periodicity in the radio). Its VLBI jet is bent. No jet is resolved on VLA scales. It is positioned in the non-thermal region of the color-color diagram.

sources.summary: The paper reports VLBA knots with differing core placements, adopts the 4.95 GHz jet direction for the X-ray search, and finds no associated extended X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: 0923 + 392 (4C +39.25) VLBA data show knots to the west of the core at a PA of -78 , moving with a maximum apparent speed of 2.9 vlba,ls00 . , however, place the core differently (at position S instead of A in the map, as suggested by ), with knots at a PA of about 100 , moving at up to 4.3 . The 4.95 GHz map shows a 2 \ long jet extending along a PA of + 75 , which we take as the direction to examine for X-ray emission. We detect no associated extended X-ray emission, on either side of the core.

sources.summary: The paper describes 5BZQJ 0927+3902 as a bright radio blazar near a dominant 3FGL source, with no gamma-ray SED information and no evidence for source confusion.

quotes.quote: 5BZQJ 0927+3902. This is a bright radio blazar (z = 0.695) about 46.1 arcminutes away from 3FGL J0923.1+3853 (which is associated to B2 0920+39). We investigate this region with a TS map, looking for signs of source confusion, but we could find none. The 3FGL \ signature dominates the emission in this region as seen by the low-energy TS map (Fig. bzq0927ts , left); the SED for 5BZQJ 0927+3902 (Fig. bzq0927ts , right) has no of \ information. This is likely a good proxy for blazars with an IC component that is MeV peaked just as the following cases: 5BZQJ 2139+1423, 5BZQJ 2022+6136, and 5BZQJ 2007+4029.

gmw_00006207.name: NGC 4485
gmw_00006207.overview: NGC 4485 is an irregular companion galaxy [arXiv:1009.4396] that is currently interacting with the barred spiral galaxy NGC 4490 [arXiv:2405.06016]. This low-mass galaxy [arXiv:2405.06016] is likely undergoing a starburst [arXiv:1807.03791], featuring compact star-forming sources in its southern outskirts [arXiv:1606.01699].

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 4485 as the nearby low-luminosity companion interacting with NGC 4490, associated with disturbed morphology, a tidal tail, and tests for companion effects in simulations.

quotes.quote: The barred spiral galaxy NGC 4490 and its very nearby companion NGC 4485 are an interacting pair of low-mass galaxies. However, the secondary galaxy, NGC 4485, has only about 5 \ of the K band luminosity of the major galaxy. The pair can thus be considered as a hierarchically merging system, in contrast to a major merger that would require a more equal mass ratio. The galaxies are situated at a distance of 8 to 10 Mpc 2013AJ....145..101K , and have a mutual projected separation of less than 10 kpc. In addition to their disturbed optical morphology indicative of the ongoing interaction, 2023MNRAS.523.3905L have recently discovered a massive tidal tail in \ that extends along the north-south direction around NGC 4490 and NGC 4485, expanding beyond the system's previously known \ envelope.

NGC 4490 is currently interacting with the nearby galaxy NGC 4485. This warrants the question whether such a configuration might affect the frequency or the properties of satellite planes in the associated satellite systems in the simulation.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4485 as the irregular companion interacting with NGC 4490, part of a nearby pair embedded in H I and associated with tidal star formation.

quotes.quote: We present an analysis of the hot interstellar medium (ISM) in the spiral galaxy NGC 4490, which is interacting with the irregular galaxy NGC 4485, using 100 ks of Chandra ACIS-S observations. The high angular resolution of Chandra enables us to remove discrete sources and perform spatially resolved spectroscopy for the star forming regions and associated outflows, allowing us to look at how the physical properties of the hot ISM such as temperature, hydrogen column density and metal abundances vary throughout these galaxies.

NGC 4490/85 are a pair of closely interacting galaxies that are relatively nearby, at a distance of 7.8 Mpc tully . NGC 4490 is a type SB(s)d spiral galaxy, and its companion, NGC 4485, is a type IB(s)m irregular galaxy devaucouleurs . The optical isophotes of NGC 4490 indicate that its disk has a high inclination of 60^ bertola , which allows us to separate emission from the hot gaseous medium in the plane and the halo of the galaxy. These galaxies are embedded in an unusually large envelope of HI gas viallefond , which clemens98 suggest may have been formed by star formation driven outflows. clemens99 studied the thermal and synchrotron components of the radio continuum emission from these galaxies and concluded that active star formation has been progressing throughout the disk of NGC 4490 at an approximately constant rate of 4.7 M _ yr ^ 1 for at least 10^ 8 years. Observations of the H emission e.g. thronson are consistent with star formation occuring throughout the disk of NGC 4490. However, H emission is also present between the galaxies, indicating that tidal interactions are important in determining the location of star formation.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4485 as the radio-quiet companion in Arp 269, with compact southern star-forming sources, thermal emission, young spectral ages, and weaker magnetic fields.

quotes.quote: The companion galaxy NGC 4485 is a rather radio quiet one, compared to its neighbour. Most of its emission is due to a group of three sources, compact in character. This group lies in the southern outskirts of the galactic disk, and clemens_ram identifies it with a bow-shock in the IGM. Emission from these sources has a significant thermal component (reaching 50 at 15.21 GHz).

As in some of the previous cases, only upper limit for the spectral age can be derived: 7.8 Myrs for the JP model and 23.5 Myrs for the CI. These limits are higher than those obtained for the disk regions; whereas this might indicate the older age of the structure formed by the shock, straight-line character of the SED suggests that this region is still a very young one.

sources.summary: NGC 4485 is the lower-mass companion in the modeled pair, showing likely starburst activity, elevated star formation, offset gas, and passage through NGC 4490.

quotes.quote: Interestingly, lee09 found that NGC 4485 is likely undergoing a starburst, as its H equivalent width is EW = 76 13 , which exceeds the logarithmic mean by 2 limit when compared to the 300 dwarfs in the 11HUGS Dwarf Galaxy Survey ( lee09 ).
NGC 4485's star formation rate inferred from the far ultraviolet (FUV) non-ionizing continuum is SFR(FUV) = 0.22 yr ^ 1 (assuming a distance of 7.14 Mpc; see lee09 table 1).
In our model, NGC 4485 passes through the gaseous disk of NGC 4490, and therefore will experience significant ram pressure stripping during this time.

gmw_00006208.name: Tololo 65
gmw_00006208.overview: Tololo 65 is an extremely metal-poor blue compact dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1511.06707] that hosts a young starburst [arXiv:0805.3501]. It exhibits a cometary morphology with a bright main body and an extended stellar tail [arXiv:1511.06707], alongside highly disturbed gas kinematics that lack a global rotation pattern [arXiv:1511.06707].

sources.summary: The paper presents VIMOS-IFU spectroscopy of Tol 65, finding a cometary, extremely metal-poor BCD with young star formation, extended tail emission, disturbed kinematics, and flat abundances likely shaped by gas infall or a minor merger.

quotes.quote: In this study we present high-resolution VIsible Multi-Object Spectrograph integral field unit spectroscopy (VIMOS-IFU) of the extremely metal-poor H ii /blue compact dwarf (BCD) galaxy Tol 65. The optical appearance of this galaxy shows clearly a cometary morphology with a bright main body and an extended and diffuse stellar tail.

The velocity field V _r in Tol 65 is highly disturbed and it shows no global rotation pattern. We found _ HII,body _ HII,tail _ HI suggesting that the gas still retains the kinematic memory of its parental cloud and a common origin. While extended regions of diffuse gas are highly disturbed, probably due to unresolved expanding shells and the effects of massive star evolution. We did not observe a spatial variation of metal (O, N, Ne, Ar and S) in the ISM of the galaxy. This agrees with previous observations in other H ii galaxies in the literature and references therein LagosPapaderos2013 .

Although the evidence is far from conclusive, we favour the idea that the most likely mechanisms to produce the flat abundance gradient and the cometary morphology in this XMP H ii /BCD is the infall/accretion of cold gas from the outskirts of the galaxy or minor merger/interaction with an small companion recently in the past of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper finds Tololo 65 has no direct Ly structure, only weak diffuse emission, and is a young pure damped Ly absorber similar to SBS 335-052.

quotes.quote: Tololo 65  -- In the last column of Fig. images_2 , no Ly \ structure can be seen for this galaxy, though the HST image shows some emission features (bottom frame), they have been smoothed to the NTT resolution. Degrading the resolution of the HST image leads to ghost features since the emission becomes as weak as the background level and is swallowed up.

TOLOLO 65 , in the same way, shows very weak Ly \ emission through a diffuse component without direct emission. GHRS spectrum thuan97b shows, with an acceptable signal-to-noise ratio although poorer than that of SBS 335-052, that this galaxy is a pure Ly \ absorber.

It is however worth noting that the small age ( 2.6 Myr) of SBS 335-052 agrees with our discussion about this galaxy as a young starburst embedded in a static H i \ leading to a damped absorption. This is also true for Tololo 65 ( 3.3 Myr) where no direct emission is seen and for which GHRS spectrum shows a damped absorption.

htbp Reddening correction and star formation rate The evolution of the L_ FIR / L_ FUV \ ratio provides an alternative to evaluate the reddening estimate reliability.

sources.summary: The paper describes Tol 65 as a complex UV starburst where bright regions mainly absorb Lyman-alpha while fainter regions show emission, with poor data quality making the net flux uncertain.

quotes.quote: Previous HST/GHRS spectra Thuan_Izotov97 have indicated that the brightest knot is a damped \ absorber. The UV morphology of Tol 65 is also rather complex but, assuming that the NW knot was acquired by the GHRS PEAK-UP maneuver, photometry performed in a 2 2 aperture consistently finds this to be an absorber. Overall we find that the continuum-bright regions are dominated by absorption but we also see \ in emission from fainter regions. \ is the galaxy for which the data quality is worst and the uncertainties are largest. Due to the effects discussed in Sect. sect:pcyg_phot , the positive net \ flux observed from this galaxy may not be real.

sources.summary: The paper treats Tololo 65 as a young-galaxy candidate whose halo colors are better matched by extended star-formation models, so photometry does not require a genuinely young age.

quotes.quote: In Fig. 1 we compare published halo colours for two young galaxy candidates: Tololo 65 and SBS 0335-052 with the predictions from spectral synthesis models (PEGASE.2 by Fioc and Rocca-Volmerange, 1999). For Tololo 65 the agreement is good when comparing models with extended (i.e. not SSP) star formation history. For SBS 0335-052, U-B gives a lower estimate, still the data can be fit with an age of several Gyr. There is nothing from the photometrical point of view that requires these galaxies to be young.

gmw_00006209.name: J092313.53+043445.0
gmw_00006209.overview: J092313.53+043445.0 is a changing-look active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z=0.65682 [arXiv:2401.01933]. The object exhibited brightening in both its spectra from 2003 to 2021 and its infrared flux from 2016 to 2021, a transition likely driven by accretion flow variations [arXiv:2401.01933].

sources.summary: The paper identifies this CL-AGN as brightening in spectra and WISE infrared light curves, interpreting the transition as likely driven by accretion-flow variations.

quotes.quote: J092313.53+043445.0 ( z=0.65682 ; Figure fig:wise_examples top panels) The spectra (top-left) show that this CL-AGN brightened during the period 2003--2021. The WISE light curves (top-right) panel show a brightening in the IR flux from this CL-AGN during the period 2016--2021. These data suggest that this CL-AGN transition was driven by variations in the accretion flow.

gmw_00006210.name: J144024.60+345624.2
gmw_00006210.overview: J144024.60+345624.2 is a changing-look active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z=0.752449 [arXiv:2401.01933]. Between 2010 and 2021, its optical continuum and line emission dimmed while its infrared flux remained essentially constant [arXiv:2401.01933].

sources.summary: The paper describes this CL-AGN as spectroscopically dimmed while WISE infrared flux stayed constant, leaving its obscuration or accretion interpretation uncertain.

quotes.quote: J144024.60+345624.2 ( z=0.752449 ; Figure fig:wise_examples bottom panels) The spectra (bottom-left) show that the continuum and \ line emission in source dimmed during the period 2010--2021. However, the corresponding WISE light curves (bottom-right) do not reflect this trend, showing an essentially constant IR flux over the same period. While the lack of (extreme) variability in the WISE data may have been interpreted as supporting an obscuration-driven CL transition, we note that the bluer \ line has not dimmed considerably, as one may expect from (dusty) gas intervening our line-of-sight. A more detailed analysis is required in order to elucidate the nature of such CL-AGNs.

gmw_00006211.name: J015854.50+001307.3
gmw_00006211.overview: J015854.50+001307.3 is a changing-look active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z=0.145004 [arXiv:2401.01933]. It has exhibited a delayed dimming transition where its blue quasar-like continuum disappeared first, followed later by the dimming of its broad emission lines [arXiv:2401.01933].

sources.summary: The paper highlights this dimming CL-AGN for a delayed transition: continuum disappeared first, then broad lines dimmed, consistent with a drastic accretion-flow change.

quotes.quote: J015854.50+001307.3 ( z=0.145004 ; Figure fig:all_spec upper-left) This dimming CL-AGN exhibits a successive dimming of the various spectral components: in the period 2010--2017, the blue quasar-like continuum has disappeared, the broad \ line had somewhat dimmed, while the broad \ did not change significantly. Then, in the period 2017--2020, \ dimmed further, and \ had started to dim, too. This delayed transition where the continuum dims first and is only later followed by the dimming of the broad emission lines is expected in the case of a drastic change in the accretion flow, albeit on somewhat long timescales .

gmw_00006212.name: J132457.29+480241.3
gmw_00006212.overview: J132457.29+480241.3 is a changing-look active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z=0.271847 [arXiv:2401.01933]. The source transitioned from a bright quasar state to a dimmer state between 2003 and 2014, but it rebrightened into a type 1 quasar-like state by 2022 [arXiv:2401.01933].

sources.summary: The paper describes this CL-AGN as having dimmed by 2014, then rebrightened in 2021-2022 with weak broad-line and quasar-like continuum recovery.

quotes.quote: J132457.29+480241.3 ( z=0.271847 ; Figure fig:all_spec middle-right) This CL-AGN had already transitioned, from a brighter quasar state to a dimmer state that has no visible broad , during the period 2003--2014, and was already noted in MacLeod16, MacLeod19 and Hutsemekers19 . However, SDSS-V spectra taken in 2021 reveal the reappearance of a (weak) broad \ and a weak quasar-like continuum. Subsequent LCOGT and HET spectra taken during 2022 show the source has kept rebrightening, reaching a type 1 quasar-like state.

gmw_00006213.name: J162829.17+432948.5
gmw_00006213.overview: J162829.17+432948.5 is a changing-look active galactic nucleus at a redshift of z = 0.2603 [arXiv:2210.07258, arXiv:2401.01933]. The source experienced a rapid dimming into a host-dominated state followed by a two-month recovery, a transition likely driven by variable line-of-sight obscuration [arXiv:2210.07258, arXiv:2401.01933].

sources.summary: SDSS J162829.17+432948.5 is reported as a rapidly changing-look quasar whose spectroscopy and photometry show month-scale continuum and broad-line transitions likely driven by variable obscuration.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of a new ``changing-look'' active galactic nucleus (CLAGN) event, in the quasar SDSS J162829.17+432948.5 at z=0.2603 , identified through repeat spectroscopy from the fifth Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS-V). Optical photometry taken during 2020--2021 shows a dramatic dimming of g 1 mag , followed by a rapid recovery on a timescale of several months, with the 2 month period of rebrightening captured in new SDSS-V and Las Cumbres Observatory spectroscopy. This is one of the fastest CLAGN transitions observed to date. Archival observations suggest that the object experienced a much more gradual dimming over the period of 2011--2013. Our spectroscopy shows that the photometric changes were accompanied by dramatic variations in the quasar-like continuum and broad-line emission.

The excellent agreement between the pre- and postdip photometric and spectroscopic appearances of the source, as well as the fact that the dimmest spectra can be reproduced by applying a single extinction law to the brighter spectral states, favor a variable line-of-sight obscuration as the driver of the observed transitions.

sources.summary: The paper reports this CL-AGN dimmed into a host-dominated state, recovered within two months, and may be best explained by variable obscuration.

quotes.quote: J162829.17+432948.5 ( z = 0.2603 ; Figure fig:all_spec bottom-right) This source was presented and discussed in detail in . Its earliest SDSS-V spectra (2020--2021) showed it to be in a much dimmer state than archival SDSS-I spectroscopy (2001), with the continuum becoming host-dominated and the broad \ line significantly dimming. It then showed a remarkable ``recovery'' within 2 months, after which it regained its bright quasar-like appearance (as corroborated by the ZTF light curve). The detailed analysis presented in led to the claim that these dramatic events may be best described by variable obscuration.

gmw_00006214.name: z=2.583 DLA galaxy
aliases_external: z=2.583 DLA galaxy toward SDSS J091826.16+163609.0
gmw_00006214.overview: The z=2.583 DLA galaxy is a relatively massive, strongly star-forming system with a disk-like morphology [arXiv:1306.2940]. It is characterized by a dusty environment and exhibits strong emission lines, including oxygen and hydrogen lines [arXiv:1306.2940].

sources.summary: The paper models the z=2.583 DLA galaxy as a massive, dusty, star-forming disk-like system with emission-line metallicity consistent with the DLA absorption metallicity.

quotes.quote: By fitting stellar population synthesis models to the photometric SED we constrain the physical properties of the z=2.583 DLA galaxy, and we infer its morphology by fitting a Sersic model to its surface brightness profile. We find it to be a relatively massive ( M_ 10^ 10 M_ ), strongly star-forming (SFR 30 M _ yr ^ 1 ), dusty ( E_ B-V =0.4) galaxy with a disk-like morphology. We detect strong emission lines from the z=2.583 DLA ( [ O ii ] 3727, [ O iii ] 4960, [ O iii ] 5007, H , and H , albeit at low signal-to-noise (S/N) ratio except for the [ O iii ] 5007 line). The metallicity derived from the emission lines is consistent with the absorption metallicity ( 12 + ( O/H ) = 8.8 0.2 ).
The z=2.583 DLA Galaxy For the z=2.583 DLA Galaxy we have the largest amount of information: detection of severeal strong emission lines and a clear detection of the galaxy in the HST images.

gmw_00006215.name: galaxy counterpart of the z=2.412 DLA
aliases_local: galaxy counterpart of the z=2.412 DLA
aliases_external: z=2.412 DLA galaxy counterpart toward SDSS J091826.16+163609.0
gmw_00006215.overview: The galaxy counterpart of the z=2.412 DLA is a high-redshift emission-line galaxy [arXiv:1306.2940]. It is detected through a very narrow [O III] 5007 emission line at a small impact parameter of less than 2 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1306.2940].

sources.original_name: galaxy counterpart of the z=2.412 DLA
sources.summary: The paper reports the first detection of the z=2.412 DLA galaxy counterpart through narrow [O III] emission at very small impact parameter.

quotes.quote: We also detect [ O iii ] 5007 emission from the galaxy counterpart of the z=2.412 DLA at a small impact parameter ( <2 kpc). Overall our findings are consistent with the emerging picture that high-metallicity DLAs are associated with relatively luminous and massive galaxy counterparts, compared to typical DLAs.

fig:OIII we show the [ O iii ] 5007 emission line from the z=2.412 DLA now at a higher S/N ratio. The line is very narrow with a FWHM of 67 12 km s ^ 1 , which is only slightly larger than the resolution of 45 km s ^ 1 . Corrected for the resolution the FWHM is 50 km s ^ 1 .

The impact parameter is consistent with 0 and a conservative upper limit is 0.25 arcsec corresponding to 2.0 kpc. In this paper we have presented new observations of the two DLAs towards Q 0918+1636 and their galaxy counterparts. The galaxy counterpart of the z=2.583 DLA was discovered previously 2011MNRAS.413.2481F , whereas the discovery of the counterpart of the z=2.412 DLA is first reported here.

gmw_00006216.name: ALESS75.1
gmw_00006216.overview: ALESS75.1 is a high-redshift submillimeter galaxy [arXiv:2002.03545]. A steep mid-infrared slope suggests the galaxy hosts an active galactic nucleus, and it has a marginal detection of hydrogen [arXiv:2002.03545].

sources.summary: The paper discusses ALESS75.1 as an SMG with possible IR AGN activity, new ALMA continuum observations, and a marginal H detection.

quotes.quote: The steep slope in mid-infrared has suggested that ALESS75.1 is an IR AGN Simpson:2014aa,Stanley:2018aa .
ALESS66.1 and ALESS75.1 were observed in Cycle 4 as a comparison sample for testing the size difference between SMGs with or without detectable AGN (Project ID: 2016.1.00735.S).
However H is not available in all but a marginal detection from one SMG, ALESS75.1.

gmw_00006217.name: A2744-01
gmw_00006217.overview: A2744-01 is a low-mass post-starburst galaxy with a stellar mass of $10^{8.9}$ solar masses [arXiv:2203.08862]. It features long tails of ionized gas and a stellar-population gradient aligned with these tails, indicating a very recent quenching of star formation [arXiv:2203.08862].

sources.summary: The paper highlights A2744-01 as a low-mass post-starburst with ionized-gas tails, recent quenching, strong burst signatures, and a stellar-population gradient aligned with the tail.

quotes.quote: Tails of ionized gas We have identified two post-starbursts that, although lacking any emission lines in their stellar disks, display long tails of ionized gas, these are A2744-01 and SMACS2131-06. We note that for A2744-01 these tails have already been identified by and .

The case of A2744-01 is particularly interesting. In Fig. fig:tail_map_A2744_01 , we plot the map of _ 1.5 for this galaxy (yellow to blue color map) and the flux map in of the line (red color map). The _ 1.5 map has a side-to-side gradient that is aligned with the tail of ionized gas in such a way that spaxels closer to the tail have larger fractions of young stellar populations (more prominent bursts).

Other properties of A2744-01 are also worth mentioning. Of all galaxies in our sample, it is the one with the lowest mass ( M_ /M_ = 8.9 ), lowest median t_Q (64 Myr, see Fig. fig:box ) and a very low t_Q of 51 Myr. It also has very strong features with a blue spectrum (see top panel of Fig.

gmw_00006218.name: A2744-07
gmw_00006218.overview: A2744-07 is a post-starburst galaxy that exhibits active galactic nucleus emission in the central region of its stellar disk [arXiv:2203.08862]. The galaxy is undergoing inside-out quenching and features two extended strips of gas stretching beyond its stellar disk [arXiv:2203.08862].

sources.summary: The paper treats A2744-07 as a post-starburst with central AGN-like emission, residual star formation, inside-out quenching, and extended gas strips around the stellar disk.

quotes.quote: We have included in our sample two objects that show emission lines in the central regions of their stellar disk, these are MACS1206-06 and A2744-07. We include these objects because we interpret this emission as due to AGN.

For 3 galaxies (A2744-07, MACS1206-06 and MACS1206-09, panels b, e and f), the t_Q maps indicate an inside-out quenching, which is consistent with the stellar population gradients of the general population of star-forming galaxies (older in the center). In the cases of A2744-07 and MACS1206-06, we find indication of AGN emission in the central region of the stellar disks (see section sec:sample ).

The case of A2744-07 Another object with peculiar emission line features is A2744-07. In Fig. fig:oii_vel we show the flux (top) and velocity (bottom) for this object. Besides the AGN in the central region (see section sec:sample ), two strips of gas can be seen in the map, one to the North-East, extending 20 kpc along and beyond the stellar disk, and one to the East, extending 12 kpc perpendicular to the stellar disk. In addition to , these features are also visible in H , but not in , which points to a soft ionization source, although the strips seem to be spatially correlated with the central AGN.

gmw_00006219.name: MACS1206-06
gmw_00006219.overview: MACS1206-06 is a post-starburst galaxy that exhibits central emission lines within its stellar disk [arXiv:2203.08862]. Although its BPT classification is ambiguous, the presence of a Chandra X-ray point source and a lack of current star formation suggest the galaxy hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2203.08862].

sources.summary: The paper identifies MACS1206-06 as the most massive sample galaxy and discusses its central emission, noting limited line coverage and an ambiguous BPT classification consistent with star formation or LINER/AGN emission.

quotes.quote: We have included in our sample two objects that show emission lines in the central regions of their stellar disk, these are MACS1206-06 and A2744-07. We include these objects because we interpret this emission as due to AGN.

Unfortunately, these lines are redshifted out of the MUSE spectral range for MACS1206-06. In Fig. fig:bpt we analyze the central spaxels of these two objects in the / vs / BPT diagram. For A2744-07 (blue points) we include spaxels with S/N>3 in all four lines. Since for MACS1206-06 we have only information on and H , we can only constrain the y-axis of the diagram.

The BPT classification for MACS1206-06 is ambiguous: not only are we unable to constrain the x-axis, but the y-axis values fall in a region where star-formation regions and LINERs (which can be AGN) coexist. However, since an X-ray point source is detected within the galaxy in the Chandra catalog Ehlert2015, Wang2016 and there is no evidence for current star-formation from the spectral synthesis, we conclude that an AGN is the most likely ionization source. For A2744-01 and SMACS2131-06 we also identify emission lines, but outside of the stellar disk. This will be discussed in section sec:tails . In this section we will go into details regarding the spectral synthesis method and the determination of physical parameters for our sample of 21 post-starburst galaxies.

gmw_00006220.name: MACS1206-09
gmw_00006220.overview: MACS1206-09 is a post-starburst galaxy that exhibits an inside-out quenching pattern [arXiv:2203.08862]. However, its outer disk regions quenched slightly earlier, suggesting a combination of secular growth and ram-pressure processes [arXiv:2203.08862].

sources.summary: The paper describes MACS1206-09 as a post-starburst whose maps suggest mostly inside-out quenching with some older outer regions, implying combined secular growth and ram pressure.

quotes.quote: For 3 galaxies (A2744-07, MACS1206-06 and MACS1206-09, panels b, e and f), the t_Q maps indicate an inside-out quenching, which is consistent with the stellar population gradients of the general population of star-forming galaxies (older in the center).
For MACS1206-09, despite the general inside-out pattern we do see that the outer parts of the disk quenched slightly earlier, pointing to a combination of inside-out and outside-in.
In fact, as stated before, for MACS1206-09 the maps point to a combination of secular growth and ram-pressure, as both the inner regions and the outskirts have older t_Q and lower _ 1.5 .

gmw_00006221.name: Q 0151+048B
gmw_00006221.overview: Q 0151+048B is a quasar with a systemic redshift of 1.92863 [arXiv:1106.3183]. It hosts a supermassive black hole with an estimated mass of 10^8.38 solar masses that accretes at 30 percent of its Eddington limit [arXiv:1106.3183].

sources.summary: The paper treats Q 0151+048B as the fainter quasar in the binary system, measuring its systemic redshift, black-hole and halo masses, Eddington ratio, and relation to qA.

quotes.quote: We measure systemic redshifts of z_ em( A ) =1.92924 0.00036 and z_ em( B ) =1.92863 0.00042 from the H and H emission lines, respectively. In other words, the two QSOs have identical redshifts within 2 . From the width of Balmer emission lines and the strength of the rest-frame optical continuum, we estimate the masses of the black holes of the two QSOs to be 10 ^ 9.33 M _ and 10 ^ 8.38 M _ for Q 0151+048A and Q 0151+048B, respectively. We then use the correlation between black hole mass and dark matter halo mass to infer the mass of the dark matter halos hosting the two QSOs: 10 ^ 13.74 M _ and 10 ^ 13.13 M _ for Q 0151+048A and Q 0151+048B, respectively.
We report bolometric accretion rates of both quasars and find that they accrete at 60 and 30 of their Eddington limits.

gmw_00006222.name: IMS J160517+554002
gmw_00006222.overview: IMS J160517+554002 is a high-redshift quasar [arXiv:2003.00709]. Spectroscopic observations confirm that this active galactic nucleus is located at a redshift of approximately 5 [arXiv:2003.00709].

sources.summary: The survey confirms IMS J160517+554002 as a z about 5 quasar and derives its spectroscopic redshift and UV absolute magnitude from Hectospec spectra and SED fitting.

quotes.quote: The Hectospec observation reveals all the candidates, IMS J160517+554002, IMS J160552+555340, and IMS J160914+554511, are quasars at z 5 . Figure fig:Spectra shows their spectra. The strong break at Ly can be seen in all spectra, suggesting that they are all indeed at z 5 . Their spectroscopic redshifts and M_ 1450 are derived with the SED-fitting using 20,000 chains of Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) simulation to these spectra. Both z_ spec and z_ spec based M_ 1450 of the three quasars are given in Table table:BIC .

gmw_00006223.name: IMS J160552+555340
gmw_00006223.overview: IMS J160552+555340 is a high-redshift quasar [arXiv:2003.00709]. Spectroscopic observations reveal a strong Lyman break, confirming that the active galactic nucleus is located at a redshift of approximately 5 [arXiv:2003.00709].

sources.summary: The survey confirms IMS J160552+555340 as a z about 5 quasar and derives its spectroscopic redshift and UV absolute magnitude from Hectospec spectra and SED fitting.

quotes.quote: The Hectospec observation reveals all the candidates, IMS J160517+554002, IMS J160552+555340, and IMS J160914+554511, are quasars at z 5 . Figure fig:Spectra shows their spectra. The strong break at Ly can be seen in all spectra, suggesting that they are all indeed at z 5 . Their spectroscopic redshifts and M_ 1450 are derived with the SED-fitting using 20,000 chains of Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) simulation to these spectra. Both z_ spec and z_ spec based M_ 1450 of the three quasars are given in Table table:BIC .

gmw_00006224.name: IMS J160914+554511
gmw_00006224.overview: IMS J160914+554511 is a high-redshift quasar located at a redshift of approximately 5 [arXiv:2003.00709]. Its spectrum displays a strong Lyman-alpha break, which is characteristic of active galactic nuclei at this epoch [arXiv:2003.00709].

sources.summary: The survey confirms IMS J160914+554511 as a z about 5 quasar and derives its spectroscopic redshift and UV absolute magnitude from Hectospec spectra and SED fitting.

quotes.quote: The Hectospec observation reveals all the candidates, IMS J160517+554002, IMS J160552+555340, and IMS J160914+554511, are quasars at z 5 . Figure fig:Spectra shows their spectra. The strong break at Ly can be seen in all spectra, suggesting that they are all indeed at z 5 . Their spectroscopic redshifts and M_ 1450 are derived with the SED-fitting using 20,000 chains of Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) simulation to these spectra. Both z_ spec and z_ spec based M_ 1450 of the three quasars are given in Table table:BIC .

gmw_00006225.name: ESO509-9
gmw_00006225.overview: ESO509-9 is a central brightest cluster galaxy located within the galaxy cluster A1736 [arXiv:2402.10337]. The galaxy has a close, bright companion named LEDA47075 and is surrounded by several bright elliptical galaxies, suggesting that its host cluster is undergoing a merger [arXiv:2402.10337].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ESO509-9 as A1736’s central BCG, noting its bright companion, surrounding ellipticals, merger context, and alignment between red-sequence and mass structures.

quotes.quote: A1736 A1736 has multiple BCGs (e.g., IC4252, ESO509-8, ESO509-9; separated by 0.7 Mpc). The central galaxy ESO509-9 has a close and bright companion LEDA47075, and several bright ellipticals are scattered 0.5 - 1 Mpc from the cluster center, suggesting that A1736 is undergoing a merger. The RS distribution aligns with the central BCG orientation (N-S) at small radii, while the mass distribution shows the alignment approximately at large radii. The mass distribution is along the NE-SW direction at small radii, and the mass peak lies to the NE ( 2' 0.1 Mpc) of the central galaxy.

gmw_00006226.name: GDS J 033246.05-275444.8
gmw_00006226.overview: GDS J 033246.05-275444.8 is an early-type galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0504127]. Located at a redshift of approximately 0.6, it has been used to analyze color-radius distributions and compare pixel-based and Voronoi-binned color mapping techniques [arXiv:astro-ph/0504127].

sources.summary: The paper uses GDS J 033246.05-275444.8 as a z 0.6 early-type example, comparing pixel and Voronoi-binned color maps to improve color-radius analysis.

quotes.quote: We compare these two methods with a i_ AB 22 galaxy at z 0.6 (GDS J 033246.05-275444.8 ). The top panels show the V-i color map (left) and color-radius distribution (right) along with the distribution of the SNR (inset). The histogram shows a wide distribution with a large number of pixels with SNR 1 . Such a distribution gives a wide scatter of colors in the outer parts of the galaxy.

The bottom panels show the same galaxy after an optimal Voronoi tessellation with a target SNR =10 . The color map appears much smoother but not loosing real inhomogeneous color distributions. The histogram of SNR shows that there is a very significant reduction in the number of (binned) pixels with a low SNR. The analysis of the color-radius distribution is thereby more robust and less prone to systematic effects from the noise.

gmw_00006227.name: J104048.6-115550.2
gmw_00006227.overview: J104048.6-115550.2 is a Lyman-break galaxy at a redshift of z=5.44 [arXiv:astro-ph/0701724]. It hosts a faint active galactic nucleus identified by NV emission, though the majority of its ultraviolet continuum and Lyman-alpha emission originates from an ongoing starburst [arXiv:astro-ph/0701724].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J104048.6-115550.2 as a z=5.44 Lyman-break galaxy hosting a faint AGN, with NV emission, spatially resolved HST imaging, and mostly stellar Ly and UV continuum emission.

quotes.quote: Out of a sample of more than fifty spectroscopically-confirmed R- band dropout galaxies at z 5 and above, only J104048.6-115550.2 at z=5.44 shows evidence for a high ionisation-potential emission-line indicating the presence of a hard ionising continuum from an AGN. Like most objects in our sample the rest-frame-UV spectrum shows the UV continuum breaking across a Ly line. Uniquely within this sample of LBGs, emission from NV is also detected, a clear signature of AGN photo-ionisation.

The object is spatially resolved in HST imaging. This, and the comparatively high Ly /NV flux ratio indicates that the majority of the Ly (and the UV continuum longward of it) originates from stellar photo-ionisation, a product of the ongoing starburst in the Lyman break galaxy. Even without the AGN emission, this object would have been photometrically-selected and spectroscopically-confirmed as a Lyman break in our survey.

gmw_00006228.name: KK 109
gmw_00006228.overview: KK 109 is a low-surface-brightness dwarf galaxy of an intermediate type between irregular and spheroidal classifications [arXiv:0710.0711]. Located at a distance of 4.51 Mpc [arXiv:astro-ph/0210414, arXiv:0710.0711], it resides in a vast region with no massive neighboring galaxies [arXiv:0710.0711].

sources.summary: The paper discusses KK 109 as a CVn I galaxy observed with WFPC2, describing its stellar population or morphology and deriving a TRGB-based distance.

quotes.quote: This dwarf irregular galaxy of low surface brightness was found by Karachentseva Karachentsev (1998). Huchtmeier et al. (2000) detected it in the H i line and determined its radial velocity, V_ LG = 241 km s ^ 1 , which is typical for CVn I members. The CM diagram of KK 109 shows the TRGB magnitude to be 24.26 0.15 mag, which yields a distance of 4.51 0.34 Mpc. Based on the WFPC2 images, we carried out surface photometry of KK 109, obtaining total magnitudes V_t = 18.14 0.2 mag, (V - I)_t = 0.8 0.1 mag, and a central surface brightness of 23.6 0.2 mag arcsec ^ 2 in the V band.

sources.summary: The paper describes KK 109 as a low-surface-brightness transition dwarf with a TRGB distance, no massive neighbors, and only an upper limit on its H-alpha flux.

quotes.quote: KK 109 . This dwarf system of low surface brightness belongs to an intermediate type between irregular and spheroidal galaxies like the known member of the Local Group, LGS-3. Its distance, 4.51 Mpc, was determined from the tip of the red giant branch, TRGB (Karachentsev et al. 2003). There are no massive neighbors ( TI=-0.6 ) in the vast vicinities of KK 109, which could cause pushing of gas from this galaxy by the ram pressure. The H flux shown in Table 2 for the galaxy corresponds only to the upper limit.

gmw_00006229.name: DDO 113
gmw_00006229.overview: DDO 113 is a low-surface-brightness dwarf spheroidal galaxy containing an old stellar population [arXiv:0710.0711]. It is a satellite companion of the galaxy NGC 4214 [arXiv:2105.01082, arXiv:0710.0711], which quenched DDO 113's star formation approximately 1 Gyr ago through environmental interactions [arXiv:2105.01082].

sources.summary: The paper recovers DDO 113 as a known NGC 4214 satellite, finds it easily detected, and interprets its recent quenching as evidence for environmental interaction with its host.

quotes.quote: We discover no new dwarf satellites, but we recover two previously known dwarfs (DDO 113 and LV J1228+4358) with M_ V <-12 that lie in our survey volume. We preview a new theoretical framework to predict satellite luminosity functions using analytic probability distribution functions and apply it to our sample, finding that we predict one fewer classical dwarf and one more faint dwarf ( M_ V 7.5 ) than we find in our observational sample (i.e., the observational sample is slightly top-heavy). However, the overall number of dwarfs in the observational sample (2) is in good agreement with the theoretical expectations. Interestingly, DDO 113 shows signs of environmental quenching and LV J1228+4358 is tidally disrupting, suggesting that low-mass hosts may affect their satellites more severely than previously believed.

We identify no significant dwarf satellite candidates from this visual search. We therefore consider only two satellite dwarf galaxies to have been found in our imaging survey, both of which were previously known: DDO 113, which is a satellite of NGC 4214 and was examined in , and LV J1228+4358, which is a large, disrupting Rich2012,Martinez-Delgado2012,Toloba2016a satellite of NGC 4449. Both of these dwarfs are easily identified by our detection algorithm, with estimated detection efficiencies of 100 .

It is worth noting that DDO 113 also shows evidence for interaction with its host, as its star formation was quenched about 1 Gyr ago Weisz2011 , likely because of cessation of cold gas inflows after its infall to NGC 4214 Garling2020 .

sources.summary: The paper classifies DDO 113 as a regular, low-surface-brightness dwarf spheroidal with an old stellar population, no detected H-alpha emission, and confused H I flux near NGC 4214.

quotes.quote: DDO 113 = KDG 90 . This is a dwarf system of regular shape and low surface brightness. It has been resolved into stars with the Hubble Space Telescope (HST). Judging by its CM-diagram, DDO 113 contains an old stellar population and can be classified as dSph. We have not found in it either HII regions or diffuse H emission. The measurement of its HI flux is hampered by close neighborhood ( 10 ) with the bright Im-galaxy NGC 4214, a companion of which DDO 113 evidently is.

sources.summary: The paper describes UGCA 276 as a small dwarf elliptical with the sample's lowest magnitude, faint central surface brightness, slight central deficit, and flat color profile.

quotes.quote: The lowest magnitude object, UGCA 276, is at 12.37 mag , and is classified as a dwarf elliptical.
UGCA 276 : small galaxy with a faint central surface brightness. The central part is slightly deficient compared to an exponential. The color profile is flat.

gmw_00006230.name: UGC 7298 and UGC 7356
gmw_00006230.overview: UGC 7298 and UGC 7356 are irregular dwarf galaxies characterized by a low neutral hydrogen content [arXiv:0710.0711] and faint emission regions [arXiv:0710.0711]. UGC 7298 has a low surface brightness [arXiv:astro-ph/0210414] and lies at a distance of 4.21 Mpc [arXiv:astro-ph/0210414, arXiv:0710.0711], while UGC 7356 is located 7.19 Mpc away [arXiv:0710.0711].

sources.summary: The paper discusses UGC 7298 as a CVn I galaxy observed with WFPC2, describing its stellar population or morphology and deriving a TRGB-based distance.

quotes.quote: This is a dIrr galaxy of low surface brightness. UGC 7298 has been resolved into stars by Tikhonov Karachentsev (1998), who estimated its distance to be 8.6 1.5 Mpc via the brightest stars. The CM diagram (Fig. 2) shows populations of blue stars and AGB stars. We determine the TRGB magnitude to be I (TRGB) = 24.12 0.15 mag, which gives a distance of 4.21 0.32 Mpc.

sources.summary: The paper treats UGC 7298 and UGC 7356 as irregular, H I-poor galaxies with measured distances and faint emission regions, noting likely H I contamination for UGC 7356.

quotes.quote: UGC 7298 and UGC 7356 . These are irregular galaxies with a low content of HI . Their distances, 4.21 Mpc (UGC 7298) and 7.19 Mpc (UGC 7356), have recently been measured by Karachentsev et al. (2003) and Tully et al. (2007). Some faint emission regions are seen in both galaxies. Possibly, the high HI flux from UGC 7356 is due to contamination from the bright neighboring spiral NGC 4258 since the anomalously high ratio (M_ HI /L_B = 13.5M_ /L_ ) looks inconsistent with the morphology of this galaxy.

gmw_00006231.name: KKH 80
gmw_00006231.overview: KKH 80 is a low-surface-brightness dwarf galaxy with a quite regular shape [arXiv:0710.0711]. It is most likely classified as a transition dwarf galaxy of the dIrr/dSph type, with only an upper limit detected for its H-alpha flux [arXiv:0710.0711].

sources.summary: The paper describes KKH 80 as a regular low-surface-brightness transition dwarf, giving only an upper limit for H-alpha flux.

quotes.quote: KKH 80 . This is a galaxy of low surface brightness and of quit regular shape. In Table 2 only the upper limit of its H flux is presented. Most likely, KKH 80 belongs to the transition dIrr/dSph type.

gmw_00006232.name: NGC 4627 and NGC 4631
gmw_00006232.overview: NGC 4627 is a dwarf elliptical satellite galaxy that is a close companion to the larger galaxy NGC 4631 [arXiv:1410.6368, arXiv:2301.00937]. The pair exhibits tidal interactions, which have caused morphological distortions in NGC 4627 and likely stripped its gas [arXiv:1410.6368, arXiv:0710.0711].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4627 as a tidally distorted dwarf elliptical satellite of NGC 4631, unlikely to be the stellar-stream progenitor but plausibly linked to vertical disk structure or gas streams.

quotes.quote: This better resolves the vertical structures of the disk and the morphological disturbances of NGC 4627, a dwarf Elliptical (dE) satellite of NGC 4631 clearly interacting with its parent galaxy. Although the extrapolation of the path of the stream _ SE across the disk passes near the position of NGC 4631 in projection, the visible tidal arms of this companion are oriented almost perpendicular to the PA of the streams (see Fig.3). Since streams trace the prints of their progenitor system, this suggests that NGC 4627 is an unlikely candidate for the progenitor of stream _ SE and stream _ NW . It is more likely that NGC 4627 is responsible for the optically detected extended vertical structure within the NGC 4631 disk (Ann et al. 2011) or, given its overall blue mean color (Sec. 4), to the complex gas streams described below.
Spurs 2 and 3, on the other hand, were interpreted to be formed by tidal interactions between NGC 4631 and NGC 4627, its tidally distorted, blue dE companion.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4627 and NGC 4631 as a projected dE/Sd pair whose distorted elliptical component may have been gas-stripped by its massive neighbor.

quotes.quote: NGC 4627 and NGC 4631 . This is a close in projection pair of dE and Sd galaxies with distances 9.38 Mpc and 7.66 Mpc, respectively. The elliptical component shows weak peripheric distortion, which is likely to give grounds to include this pair into the catalog of peculiar systems (Arp 1966). However, with errors in the estimation of the distances to the galaxies of 15 they may constitute a physical pair of the M32+M31 type, in which the dE component underwent "de-gasetion" because of the influence of a close massive neighbor.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4627 as a close major companion of NGC 4631 whose perturbations, together with NGC 4656, likely helped shape tidal tail structures.

quotes.quote: As the brightest galaxy of the N4631g, NGC 4631 has two major companions, NGC 4656 and NGC 4627, 70.5 and 5.6 kpc away in projected distance respectively, the interaction with which should have produced most of the tidal structures around NGC 4631.

The connection between the two galaxies by tail 2 was not so clear in the WSRT data of HALOGAS, or the early WSRT data of Rand94 ; probably consequently, Combes78 tended to attribute the formation of tail 2 primarily to the perturbation of the much smaller but closer companion NGC 4627, and only secondarily to NGC 4656.

Its high energy and complex morphology suggests it likely to have been perturbed by more than one galaxy (i.e. both NGC 4656 and NGC 4627), which was supported by previous particle simulations to reproduce the dense distribution in N4631g Combes78 .

gmw_00006233.name: KK 230
gmw_00006233.overview: KK 230 is an isolated dwarf irregular galaxy of low surface brightness located between the CVnI cloud and the Local Group [arXiv:0710.0711]. It lies at a distance of 1.92 Mpc and shows no signs of H-alpha emission [arXiv:0710.0711].

sources.summary: The paper describes KK 230 as an isolated, low-surface-brightness dwarf irregular between the CVnI cloud and Local Group, with no H-alpha emission and resolved H I mapping.

quotes.quote: KK 230 . This is an isolated dIrr galaxy of low surface brightness situated between the CVnI cloud and the Local Group at a distance of 1.92 Mpc. No signs of H emission are seen. The distribution of neutral hydrogen in it with high angular resolution has been investigated by Begum et al. (2006).

gmw_00006234.name: SDSS J084856.57+013647.8
gmw_00006234.overview: SDSS J084856.57+013647.8 is an intermediate active galactic nucleus host galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.349 [arXiv:2405.17552]. While some photometric fits suggest a late-type classification, high-resolution Hubble Space Telescope data indicates it is an elliptical galaxy [arXiv:2405.17552].

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSS J084856.57+013647.8 as an intermediate AGN host with a possible nearby source, a late-type fit, conflicting HST-based elliptical evidence, and a faint merger-like halo.

quotes.quote: The third object, J084856.57+013647.8 (an Intermediate AGN, z= 0.349), shows a possible second source very close to the target, as it is possible to notice in the i-band image shown in Fig. fig:image3 . It is not straightforward to recognise this second feature as another object or as an external part of the outer halo or disc, due to the redshift and consequent high scale. Only the source itself has been considered in the fit, and the best model was achieved with a single Sérsic function, without a PSF.

The Sérsic function exhibits a low Sérsic index, close to 1, and an high R _e , which can be identified with an outer disc or with GALFIT trying to fit the second source. This allows us to classify this host galaxy as a late-type one.

Although we obtained consistent results between the single- and the multi-band analysis, in the literature we found that measured a Sérsic index of 5 for the bulge of this galaxy, using HST data, indicating an elliptical galaxy. In first instance, we thought that the discrepancy between the two different classifications could have been due to the different resolution of the used data and the presence of a second source very close to the target. To verify that, we smoothed the HST image of this source, obtained with the Advanced Camera for Surveys (ACS), by convolving it with a Gaussian kernel with a FWHM equal to the quadrature difference of the two former FWHM.

gmw_00006235.name: SDSS J092607.99+074526.6
gmw_00006235.overview: SDSS J092607.99+074526.6 is an intermediate active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z = 0.441 [arXiv:2405.17552]. The galaxy appears to have a companion, though its host morphology remains unclassified [arXiv:2405.17552].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J092607.99+074526.6 as an intermediate AGN with a possible companion, modeled by two PSFs but without a recoverable host morphology.

quotes.quote: Also in the case of J092607.99+074526.6 (Intermediate AGN, z =0.441), it seems that the galaxy has a companion, as shown in Fig. fig:image4 . No results have been found in the literature on the classification of the companion. We decided to model the sources together with only 2 PSFs, each one for each source, and no more details were needed to obtain the best fit.

Also, no results in the literature have been found about the morphological type of this peculiar object. For this reason, nothing can be said regarding the morphology of this galaxy. The surface brightness profile (Fig. fig:host4 ) shows good agreement between the galaxy profile and the model itself.

gmw_00006236.name: SDSS J094525.90+352103.5
gmw_00006236.overview: SDSS J094525.90+352103.5 is a late-type, irregular or merger-like galaxy [arXiv:2405.17552] classified as a high-excitation radio galaxy [arXiv:1311.6633]. It hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2405.17552] with an asymmetric radio morphology consisting of a weak core and two lobes [arXiv:1311.6633].

sources.summary: The paper describes 0942+355 as a HEG galaxy with complex asymmetric radio structure, central X-ray emission, strong [OIII], and mixed photoionization-shock line diagnostics.

quotes.quote: 0942+355 is a galaxy classified as HEG, larger than 0810+077 and with more complex radio structure. Its 1.6 GHz asymmetric radio morphology consist of three components: weak radio core (C) and two lobes (E and W). There are also 5 GHz observations of this source Kunert10a showing only emission from the south-eastern radio lobe. In the case of 0942+355 the brightest part of the X-ray source is right in the center of the source, between the two jets.

In the pilot sample of LLC sources observed with Chandra we have found that two sources, 0810+077 and 0942+355, have the same radio and X-ray luminosities (Table lumins ). However, 0942+355 (HEG) has higher [ OIII ] emission than 0810+077 (LEG). If we compare the OII/OIII y OIII/Hb of the detections with photoionization and shock models MAPPINGS, Allen08 , 0810+077 is consistent with 100 photoionization and 0942+355 with 80 photoionization and 20 shocks Kunert10b .

sources.summary: The paper models SDSS J094525.90+352103.5 as an intermediate AGN host and confirms a late-type, irregular or merger-like galaxy with a surrounding halo.

quotes.quote: For J094525.90+352103.5 <B2 0942+35A>, ( z= 0.207, Intermediate AGN), the PSF model was obtained with two Sérsic functions and the best galaxy fit was achieved with the PSF plus a Sérsic function. This source can be classified as Type 1.5 or 1.2, showing a grade of obscuration closer to Type 1 AGN than Type 2 and justifying the presence of the PSF in the fit. The best fit parameters can be found in Tab. tab:tab1 .

We obtained consistent results for the single band analysis, confirming that this source is hosted in a late-type galaxy. This galaxy has also been visually classified as an irregular/merger by Nascimento22 . The i-band image (Fig. fig:image5 ) indeed shows a halo with irregular features surrounding the source.

sources.summary: The paper describes TXS 0942+355 as a low-luminosity compact steep-spectrum source with weak extended optical emission, a large host-galaxy component, broad H emission, and NLS1-like line ratios.

quotes.quote: TXS 0942+355 . 2010MNRAS.408.2261K labelled this sources as a low-luminosity CSS source. 2010MNRAS.408.2279K noted that the optical image showed weak extended emission. The optical spectrum manifests a large contribution of host galaxy component (Figure fig3 ). We obtain the flux and FWHM of the broad H which are 50.52 3.52 10^ 16 erg s^ 1 and 4726 605 km s^ 1 , respectively. The [ O iii ] 5007/H and Fe ii /H (the so called R4570 parameter) are 1.59 and 0.8, respectively.

gmw_00006237.name: SDSS J114311.01+053516.1
gmw_00006237.overview: SDSS J114311.01+053516.1 is an intermediate active galactic nucleus host galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.496 [arXiv:2405.17552]. While its morphology is difficult to model photometrically, visual classifications describe the galaxy as an irregular or merger-like system [arXiv:2405.17552].

sources.summary: The paper finds SDSS J114311.01+053516.1 difficult to classify morphologically, with a PSF-only fit and literature visual classification as irregular or merger-like.

quotes.quote: SDSS J114311.01+053516.1 ( z= 0.496, Intermediate AGN), it was not possible to determine the host morphological type. Building a model with Sérsic functions only brought to non-physical parameters. The best fit for the y-band image, in this case, was achieved with a single PSF and a reduced ^ 2 _ of 1.136 _ 1.125 ^ +1.988 .

In this case, nothing can be said about the morphology of this object. This source has been visually classified as irregular/merger by Nascimento22 , while the i-band image (Fig. fig:image6 ) does not show particular features. The radial surface brightness profile (Fig. fig:host6 ) shows that the model is quite in agreement with the galaxy profile, but it deviates a bit from it at large radii.

gmw_00006238.name: SDSS J115727.61+431806.3
gmw_00006238.overview: SDSS J115727.61+431806.3 is an intermediate active galactic nucleus at a redshift of 0.230 [arXiv:2405.17552]. Its host galaxy morphology is ambiguous, with single-band analysis suggesting an early-type galaxy and multiband analysis indicating a late-type galaxy [arXiv:2405.17552].

sources.summary: The paper reports conflicting single- and multiband morphology for SDSS J115727.61+431806.3, with early-type and late-type fits both plausible and no literature classification.

quotes.quote: J115727.61+431806.3 <B3 1154+435>, ( z= 0.230, Intermediate AGN) is the only case for which there is no agreement between the single and the multi-band analysis. First of all, it was challenging to retrieve a PSF for the y-band image since the only star, close enough to the target, was very bright. To properly model the PSF, seven Sérsic functions were needed.

The host galaxy was then fitted with a PSF and a single Sérsic function for the bulge (parameters can be found in Tab. tab:tab1 ). The bulge was barely resolved, and its Sérsic index was higher than 4, meaning that this AGN could be hosted in an early-type galaxy. Freezing the coordinates of the Sérsic function instead brought a low Sérsic index, indicating a disc-like host galaxy. For this source, it was not possible to calculate the positive errors since GALFIT did not converge trying to fit the model plus _ sky and so those values were reported as N/A in Tab. tab:tab1 . Multiband analysis on the g_ P1 , z_ P1 (which contains H ) and y_ P1 bands gave a different result for the morphology of the host galaxy, finding it as a late-type.

gmw_00006239.name: SDSS J140416.35+411748.7
gmw_00006239.overview: SDSS J140416.35+411748.7 is a Type 2 active galactic nucleus host galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.360 [arXiv:2405.17552]. Multiband photometric analysis reveals a shallow, disc-like component surrounding the galactic center [arXiv:2405.17552].

sources.summary: The paper models SDSS J140416.35+411748.7 as a Type 2 AGN host with a shallow disc-like component confirmed by multiband analysis.

quotes.quote: Although J140416.35+411748.7 <B3 1402+415>, ( z= 0.360) is a Type 2 AGN, it was modelled with a PSF plus a Sérsic function. The PSF model was obtained by fitting 3 Sérsic functions. The parameters of the best fit are shown in Tab. tab:tab1 .

Again, the low Sérsic index and the R_ e extending outside the bulge region can be explained by a hybrid component between the bulge and the disc, suggesting anyway a disc-like morphology. The multi-band analysis made with GALFITM on the g_ P1 , z_ P1 (containing H ) and y_ P1 band, leaving the total magnitude, the effective radius R_ e and the Sérsic index free to vary, confirms the classification found with the single-band analysis. No morphological classification has been found in the literature on this object and also in this case it is not possible to retrieve more information from the i-band image Fig. fig:image8 , apart from a shallow feature surrounding the galaxy centre and resembling a disc.

gmw_00006240.name: SDSS J140942.44+360415.8
gmw_00006240.overview: SDSS J140942.44+360415.8 is an intermediate active galactic nucleus host galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.148 [arXiv:2405.17552]. Although previously classified as an elliptical galaxy, photometric analysis and its low Seersic index reveal a late-type disk morphology [arXiv:2405.17552].

sources.summary: The paper classifies SDSS J140942.44+360415.8 as a late-type host from its low Sérsic index and i-band disc morphology, despite a literature elliptical classification.

quotes.quote: The best fit of J140942.44+360415.8 ( z= 0.148, Intermediate AGN) was obtained with a PSF (modelled with 3 Sérsic functions) and a Sérsic function representing the hybrid component between the bulge and disc (as explained in the previous subsections; parameters in Tab. tab:tab1 ). The low value of the Sérsic index leads to the classification of a late-type galaxy for this host galaxy. The GALFITM analysis, for the g_ P1 , i_ P1 (which contains H ) and y_ P1 bands, with all parameters free to vary, converges to a Sérsic index of almost 2 for all three bands.

The i-band image of the source, shown in Fig. fig:image9 , confirms the late-type features for this galaxy, showing a disc morphology. Despite this, the source has been visually classified as an elliptical by Nascimento22 . Fig. fig:host9 shows the radial surface brightness profile of the source, and the galaxy profile is well represented by the model made up of the PSF plus the Sérsic function.

gmw_00006241.name: SDSS J164311.34+315618.4
gmw_00006241.overview: SDSS J164311.34+315618.4 is an interacting binary-quasar system hosted within an irregular or spiral galaxy [arXiv:2405.17552]. Also known as 1641+320, the source exhibits multiple radio structures, including lobes that trace past activity and a jet running from the core to a western lobe [arXiv:1212.6499].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J164311.34+315618.4 as an interacting binary-quasar system whose host is not elliptical and is described as irregular or spiral.

quotes.quote: J164311.34+315618.4 ( z= 0.586, Intermediate AGN) is part of an interacting system, a merging of two AGN, as shown in Fig. fig:image10 , the i-band image of the source. The binary quasar associated with this radio source has been classified by Brotherton99 based on optical observations. Its disturbed radio morphology has been discussed by Kunert11 , where the HST images ( Martel05 ) are shown.

From the HST image (Fig. fig:host10hubble , a zoom-in of the entire image on the binary quasar) it appears clear that this source is not hosted in an elliptical galaxy, but rather in an irregular or spiral galaxy. The best fit for the entire system was obtained instead with two PSFs, one for each object, and the best PSF model of the y-band image was obtained with a single Sérsic function.

sources.summary: The paper describes quasar 1641+320 as a reactivated-source example with multiple radio structures whose lobes trace past activity and whose current jet direction runs from C to W2.

quotes.quote: An exemplary source of that kind, quasar 1641+320, was studied in and found to exhibit multiple radio structures. The source image is shown in Figure fig:1 , where the radio structures labeled by C, E, W1 and W2 are the radio core, the eastern and the two western radio lobes, respectively.
