sources.summary: The paper studies high-redshift radio galaxy TXS 0828+193 with ALMA, detecting CO in its host, measuring molecular gas, and describing its radio morphology, Ly reservoir, and halo CO sources.

quotes.quote: In this paper we present CO(3--2) line observations of the HzRG TXS 0828+193 ( z=2.57 ) and its environment using the Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array. In contrast to previous observations, we detect CO emission associated with the HzRG and derive a molecular gas mass of (0.9 0.3) 10^ 10 M_ .

The newly found CO sources do not have counterparts in any other observing band and could be high-density clouds in the halo of TXS 0828+193 and thus potentially linked to the large-scale filamentary structure of the cosmic web.

TXS 0828+193 is a Fanaroff-Riley class II radio galaxy, with extended radio lobes ( 100 kpc) and multiple hotspots 1994A&AS..108...79R, 1997ApJS..109....1C . Rest-frame UV observations show clumpy and asymmetric morphology 1999A&A...341..329P . The HzRG has an extended ( 80 kpc), kinematically unperturbed gas reservoir traced by Ly emission 2002MNRAS.336..436V .

gmw_00006312.name: companion 1
gmw_00006312.overview: Companion 1 is a high-redshift galaxy located near TXS 0828+193 that is aligned with the axis of a radio jet [arXiv:2012.12923]. It exhibits weak continuum, stellar, and CO emission, suggesting it is either a normal star-forming galaxy or the result of jet-induced star formation [arXiv:2012.12923].

sources.summary: The paper confirms companion 1 as an off-source CO emitter near TXS 0828+193, detects weak continuum and stellar emission, and considers it either a normal star-forming galaxy or jet-induced star formation.

quotes.quote: Moreover, we confirm the presence of a previously detected off-source CO emitting region (companion 1), and detect three new potential companions. The molecular gas mass of each companion is comparable to that of the HzRG. Companion 1 is aligned with the axis of the radio jet and has stellar emission detected by Spitzer . Thus this source might be a normal star-forming galaxy or alternatively a result of jet-induced star formation.

In addition, a weak continuum emission region, denoted by companion 1, is marginally detected 10 arcsec south-west from the HzRG and aligned with the radio jet. The position of companion 1 coincides with that of the CO emission region reported by 2009MNRAS.395L..16N and therefore could be thermal dust emission. However, given the low flux density of companion 1 and its alignment with the radio axis, the contamination of the continuum emission by the southern radio jet cannot be excluded.

gmw_00006313.name: IGR J13109-5552
gmw_00006313.overview: IGR J13109-5552 is a poorly studied, radio-loud active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0809.0255] optically classified as a Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:2009.03232]. It exhibits a flat radio spectrum [arXiv:0809.0255] and an elongated, potentially extended structure that may be related to a jet [arXiv:0809.0255, arXiv:2009.03232].

sources.summary: The paper describes IGR J13109-5552 as a poorly studied radio-loud AGN with uncertain Seyfert classification, elongated radio structure, flat spectrum, and limited X-ray constraints.

quotes.quote: The sky region containing IGR J13109-5552 has not been mapped by the NVSS, but the source is present in the Molonglo Galactic Plane Survey 2nd Epoch (MGPS-2) Compact Source Catalogue b54 ; a cut-off image of the source shows an unresolved but elongated structure. IGR J13109-5552 has less information at radio frequencies but the few available data provide a flat spectrum also in this case ( 0.3). No information on CD is available for IGR J13109-5552.

In all but 2 AGN (B3 0309+411 and 3C 390.3), an intrinsic absorption component is strongly required by the data, at more than 90 confidence level for IGR J13109-5552 and S5 2116+81 and at more than 99 confidence level for the remaining objects. The Fe K line is detected in all the sources (99 confidence level), except in IGR J13109-5552 and S5 2116+81, but this may be due to XRT not being sensitive enough to detect the cold iron line around 6.4 keV. For QSO B0241+62, B3 0309+411 and IGR J13109-5552 no previous work is available and thus the analysis presented here is the first broad-band study for those 3 AGN.

sources.summary: The review describes IGR J13109-5552 as an unclear GeV INTEGRAL AGN, optically Seyfert 1 and radio-bright, with possible extended jet-related structure needing follow-up.

quotes.quote: Among INTEGRAL AGN with GeV emission, there are a couple of sources of unclear class: IGR J18249-3243 and IGR J13109-5552. Optically they are both classified as Seyfert 1. They are both bright in the radio waveband and, although repeatedly observed, are poorly studied. Their structure seems to be extended but a dedicated observational campaign is necessary to confirm the presence of jet and related features. The broad band ( XMM plus IBIS/BAT data) properties of both objects have been explored by 2014ApJ...782L..25M .

gmw_00006314.name: 1RXS J111741.0+254858
gmw_00006314.overview: 1RXS J111741.0+254858 is an active galactic nucleus classified as a BL Lacertae object and a blazar [arXiv:2312.14144]. Observations of the source reveal probable quasi-periodic oscillations of 3.7 and 4.6 days, alongside shorter periods of approximately 1.1 days [arXiv:2312.14144].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes two TESS observations of 1RXS J111741.0+254858, reports probable 3.7 and 4.6 day QPOs, and models kink-instability periods near 1.1 days.

quotes.quote: Both lower panels belong to 1RXS J111741.0+254858. The Sec. \ 22 observation of Cycle 2 began on 21 February 2021 and continued for about 25.6 days; it is shown on the lower left panel of Fig. \ 1. The lower right panel shows the light curve of the Sec. \ 49 observation of Cycle 4 of 1RXS J111741.0+254858 starting on 28 February 2022 and continuing for about 24.8 days.
For 1RXS J111741.0+254858, we have analyzed the observations from Sec. \ 22 (Cycle 2) and Sec. \ 49 (Cycle 4) and found probable QPOs of 3.7 and 4.6 days respectively.
For the Cycle 2 and Cycle 4 observations of 1RXS J111741.0+254858, periods of 1.09 and 1.1 days are found, respectively.

gmw_00006315.name: J0000 0541
gmw_00006315.overview: J0000 0541 is a radio source with a compact, kiloparsec-scale core [arXiv:2006.01700]. It exhibits a steep radio spectrum and is associated with the 1.4 GHz counterpart FIRST J000040.2 054101 [arXiv:2006.01700].

sources.summary: The source has a compact kpc-scale core with relatively high radio power, steep spectrum, FIRST counterpart, and a nearby radio source lacking an optical counterpart or connecting diffuse emission.

quotes.quote: J0000 0541 This target exhibits a compact core at kpc-scale (Fig. a left panel) with a relatively large radio power but small black hole mass compared to the averages of this sample. The flux density, luminosity, and black hole mass are S_ int 1.16 mJy, L_ int 39.16, and (M_ BH / M_ ) 6.36 respectively. It has a steep spectrum with _ in-band 1.20. This source was also detected at 1.4 GHz associating with a counterpart of FIRST J000040.2 054101. It has a flux density of S_ 1.4 3.5 mJy, a luminosity of L_ 1.4 39.03, and a steep slope of _ 1.4-5.5 0.80.

gmw_00006316.name: J0350 1025
gmw_00006316.overview: J0350 1025 is an active galactic nucleus at a redshift of z = 0.128 [arXiv:2006.01700]. It is unresolved at the kiloparsec scale, exhibiting a very steep in-band spectrum and a low radio luminosity [arXiv:2006.01700].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J0350 1025 as the faintest sample source, unresolved at kpc scale, with low radio luminosity, above-average black-hole mass, and a very steep in-band spectrum.

quotes.quote: J0350 1025 This source is the faintest one in the sample with a flux density of S_ int 0.06 mJy at redshift z = 0.128. It is unresolved at kpc-scale (Fig. f right panel). The luminosity of L_ int 38.27 is below the average, while the black hole mass of (M_ BH / M_ ) 6.91 is above the average. It has a very steep spectrum with _ in-band 3.11. This object was not detected at 1.4 GHz.

gmw_00006317.name: J0436 1022
gmw_00006317.overview: J0436 1022 is a barred Seyfert 1 galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.03555 [arXiv:1707.01081]. It exhibits a spiral-like optical morphology [arXiv:2006.01700], with emission dominated by an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1707.01081] and an extended kpc-scale radio structure [arXiv:2006.01700].

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 618 as a spiral-looking NLS1 whose kpc-scale radio morphology follows the optical host, with steep spectrum and radio measurements from VLA and NVSS.

quotes.quote: J0436 1022 (Mrk 618) Mrk 618 looks like a spiral galaxy on the optical image from the Pan-STARRS (Fig. bb left panel). The VLA map shows an extended morphology at kpc-scale (Fig. i right panel) with a flux density of S_ int 4.62 mJy. The southern extended structure is twice as bright as the northern one. We overlaid the radio contours on the optical image and found that the morphology of the extended structure in the radio map is coincident with that of the host galaxy in the optical image. The spectral slope is steep with _ in-band 0.60.

sources.summary: The paper describes HE 0433-1028 as a barred Seyfert 1 at z=0.03555, with both broad- and narrow-line classifications, AGN-dominated emission, and little prior host-morphology discussion.

quotes.quote: HE 0433-1028 is a barred Seyfert 1 galaxy Kewley2001 at a redshift of z = 0.03555 0.00001 Keel1996 . On the one hand, it can be classified as a broad-line Seyfert 1 (BLS1) based on the Balmer line widths of more than 3000 km s ^ 1 Wang2007,Mullaney2008 , and, on the other hand, it can be assigned to the group of narrow-line Seyfert 1 (NLS1) with regard to its [ OIII ] 5007/H line ratio Ryan2007,Mullaney2008 . The emission is dominated by the AGN component Yuan2010 . Up to now the host morphology and properties have not been a subject of discussion in the literature.

gmw_00006318.name: J0447 0508
gmw_00006318.overview: J0447 0508 is an active galactic nucleus with a compact, kiloparsec-scale core [arXiv:2006.01700]. This source exhibits a steep spectral slope and was not detected at 1.4 GHz [arXiv:2006.01700].

sources.summary: The paper reports a compact kpc-scale core for J0447 0508, with above-average luminosity, average black-hole mass, steep spectrum, and no 1.4 GHz detection.

quotes.quote: J0447 0508 This source has a compact core at kpc-scale (Fig. j right panel) with a flux density of S_ int 4.04 mJy. The luminosity is slightly higher than the average and the black hole mass is comparable to the average, having L_ int 39.56 and (M_ BH / M_ ) 6.85 respectively. The spectral slope is steep with _ in-band 0.83. It was not detected at 1.4 GHz.

gmw_00006319.name: J0850 0318
gmw_00006319.overview: J0850 0318 is an active galactic nucleus with a large black hole mass [arXiv:2006.01700]. At kiloparsec scales, the galaxy exhibits a slightly extended radio structure with a steep spectral index [arXiv:2006.01700].

sources.summary: The paper finds J0850 0318 slightly extended at kpc scale, with mean radio power, high sample black-hole mass, very steep C-configuration spectrum, and compact A-array detections.

quotes.quote: J0850 0318 This target shows a slightly extended structure at kpc-scale (Fig. o left panel) with a flux density of S_ int 0.43 mJy. Its radio power is at a mean level with L_ int 39.30. But the black hole mass is large in this sample with (M_ BH / M_ ) 7.17. It has a steep spectral index of _ in-band 2.15. We overlaid the radio contours on the optical image from the Pan-STARRS (Fig. bb right panel), and note that a nearby source to the west of the central target is associated with an optical counterpart of 2MASS J08502683 0318107.

gmw_00006320.name: J1615 0936
gmw_00006320.overview: J1615 0936 is a galaxy with a central radio core that is slightly extended at the kiloparsec scale [arXiv:2006.01700]. This core exhibits a steep radio spectrum, and a nearby diffuse component lies to its west without an optical counterpart [arXiv:2006.01700].

sources.summary: The paper reports J1615 0936 as a first-time radio detection with a slightly extended kpc-scale core, steep spectrum, and a nearby diffuse western component without optical counterpart.

quotes.quote: J1615 0936 This target is detected at radio frequencies for the first time, no previous detection with the 1.4 GHz surveys. The central core is slightly extended at kpc-scale (Fig. u left panel) with a flux density of S_ int 0.68 mJy. The luminosity and black hole mass are below and above the averages having L_ int 38.59 and (M_ BH / M_ ) 6.94 respectively. It has a steep radio spectrum with _ in-band 1.15. A nearby component (centered at R.A. 16:15:18.66 and Dec. 09:36:12.75) to the west of the central core, has a separation of 5.9 arcsec corresponding to a projected linear size of 7.7 kpc at z = 0.065.

gmw_00006321.name: SDSS J153150.41+242317.7
gmw_00006321.overview: SDSS J153150.41+242317.7 is a red mini-broad absorption line quasar located at a redshift of 2.284 [arXiv:1406.5920]. The quasar exhibits a highly reddened continuum with an estimated color excess of approximately 0.40 [arXiv:1406.5920].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J153150.41+242317.7 as a red mini-BAL quasar, measures z=2.284, estimates E(B-V) near 0.40, and finds it becomes the brightest quasar in the sample after extinction correction.

quotes.quote: SDSS J153150.41+242317.7 (also known as FIRST 15318+2423 and F2M1531+2423) was at firstly identify as a red QSO by glikmanetal07 from a sample of FIRST-2MASS candidates. They obtained NIR spectroscopy and detected broad H \ and \ emission lines with z \ 2.287. Our GTC spectrum is shown in Figure fig:qsored where the highly reddened continuum is obvious as well as the presence of , and deep absorptions at the wavelengths of broad lines such as \ and \ from which it can be classified as a mini-BAL. Taking into account all these lines we obtained a redshift of 2.284 .

The three fits yield essentially the same value for the color excess. In the first two cases, using the Pop. A B \ composites, we obtained E(B - V) = 0.41 0.01 \ while the FBQS template yields E(B - V) = 0.40, all cases with a high correlation coefficient ( 0.97). Taking into account the estimated extinction, FIRST15318 becomes the brightest quasar in our sample with M_B 26.3 .

gmw_00006322.name: LAE02
gmw_00006322.overview: LAE02 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha galaxy candidate that exhibits multiple components in broadband imaging [arXiv:1509.03996]. The galaxy displays extended emission in the southeast direction and has a possible redshift of z=3.123 based on its strongest spectral line [arXiv:1509.03996].

sources.summary: LAE02 has multiple broadband components, extended emission, and a FOCAS spectrum with lines whose possible redshift interpretations leave it flagged as a possible unconfirmed contaminant.

quotes.quote: LAE02 There are multiple components in the broadband images, but no HST data is available for higher resolution. ( ) is extended in the South-East direction. In the FOCAS spectrum (Figure fig:spectra ) taken at the position of ( ) emission, the strongest line is detected at _ obs =5021 . If this is then z=3.123 .

gmw_00006323.name: LAE03
gmw_00006323.overview: LAE03 is a candidate Lyman-alpha galaxy with a primary spectral emission line observed at 4969.6 Angstroms [arXiv:1509.03996]. This major line suggests a high-redshift interpretation of z=3.088, although a second unexplained emission line at 5583.9 Angstroms makes it a possible unconfirmed contaminant [arXiv:1509.03996].

sources.summary: LAE03 shows a primary spectral line consistent with a high-redshift interpretation, a second unexplained line, and is flagged as a possible unconfirmed contaminant.

quotes.quote: LAE03 The major line in the spectrum at the ( ) emission position at _ obs =4969.6 gives z=3.088 if it is (Figure fig:spectra ). There is a second line at _ obs =5583.9 , which implies _ 0 =1365.9 . If the strongest line is instead [ OII ] , then z=0.333 and the second line would be at _ 0 =4186 . No major lines are expected at any of these positions.

gmw_00006324.name: LAE05
gmw_00006324.overview: LAE05 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha galaxy candidate with a spectrum that indicates a potential redshift of z=3.096 [arXiv:1509.03996]. However, because of an uncertain secondary emission line that may be sky noise, it remains an unconfirmed contaminant candidate [arXiv:1509.03996].

sources.summary: LAE05 has a spectrum with a possible z=3.096 primary line, an additional uncertain line near a sky line, and is flagged as a possible unconfirmed contaminant.

quotes.quote: LAE05 The primary emission line in the spectrum taken at ( ) position indicates z=3.096 if it is (Figure fig:spectra ). Similarly to LAE03, there is another line at _ obs =7480 , giving _ 0 =1826 at this redshift. If the primary line is instead [ OII ] , then z=0.336 and the secondary line is at _ 0 =5598 . No major lines are expected at any of these positions. The secondary line is close to a sky line and may be simply noise.

gmw_00006325.name: LAE08
gmw_00006325.overview: LAE08 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha galaxy that corresponds to object h from previous observations [arXiv:1509.03996]. Its emission and continuum components are spatially distinct, suggesting the galaxy may be contaminated by a foreground object [arXiv:1509.03996].

sources.summary: LAE08 is identified with object h from an earlier study; its emission and continuum components appear spatially distinct, so the paper flags it as possibly foreground contaminated.

quotes.quote: LAE08 This is object 'h' in 2011MNRAS.411.2336I . There are two distinct objects in the image. The emission is associated with the object to the South-West, as seen also from the HST F814W image. The ( ) emission seems more spatially coincident with the object to the North-East. Since the and sources are spatially different, this may be contaminated by a foreground object and we flag it as such.

gmw_00006326.name: LAE09
gmw_00006326.overview: LAE09 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha galaxy candidate [arXiv:1509.03996]. However, spectroscopic observations failed to detect any emission lines, leading to its classification as a possibly unconfirmed contaminant [arXiv:1509.03996].

sources.summary: LAE09, associated with LAE038 in a prior paper, lacks detected emission in the authors’ FOCAS spectrum and is flagged as a possible unconfirmed contaminant.

quotes.quote: LAE09 This object is a candidate from LAE038 2013ApJ...765...47N . We did not detect any emission in our FOCAS 2008 spectrum. The spectrum of the source spatially associated with the emission has no emission line and the peak of emission ( ) is offset by 0.66 , where there is a corresponding substructure seen in the HST image (Figure fig:hst ). We therefore flag this object as a possibly but unconfirmed contaminated object.

gmw_00006327.name: LAE12
gmw_00006327.overview: LAE12 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha galaxy that exhibits extended emission [arXiv:1509.03996]. Hubble Space Telescope imaging reveals a faint, edge-on continuum source at its position, though a large centroid offset suggests possible contamination [arXiv:1509.03996].

sources.summary: LAE12 has extended emission, a faint edge-on continuum source in HST imaging, and a large centroid offset that leads the paper to flag possible contamination.

quotes.quote: LAE12 The emission in our data is extended and appears to spatially cover both the UV and positions. HST data in both F625W and F160W reveal a faint continuum ``edge-on'' source at the position of ( ). The offset between the centroid detections of and is large ( 1.3 ) and we therefore flag this object as a possibly but unconfirmed contaminated object.

gmw_00006328.name: LAE14
gmw_00006328.overview: LAE14 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha galaxy candidate [arXiv:1509.03996]. Its rest-frame ultraviolet emission and Lyman-alpha emission are spatially aligned, and its spectral line is clearly visible [arXiv:1509.03996].

sources.summary: LAE14 is retained as a candidate because its rest-frame UV and emission are aligned, its spectral line is clear, and the authors find no contrary evidence.

quotes.quote: LAE14 This is object '' in 2011MNRAS.411.2336I . Rest-frame UV ( band) and ( ) are spatially aligned, and although there is a small ( 0.8 ) offset between and , the line is clearly visible in the spectrum, which is otherwise clean. We retain this object as a candidate due to lack of evidence to the contrary.

gmw_00006329.name: LAE16
gmw_00006329.overview: LAE16 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha galaxy candidate [arXiv:1509.03996]. The object exhibits extended emission and shows a relatively small offset of 0.59 arcseconds between its emission components [arXiv:1509.03996].

sources.summary: LAE16 is retained as viable because the offset is small, the emission appears extended, and the broadband images show no apparent offset.

quotes.quote: LAE16 The offset between and is relatively small, 0.59 . appears to be extended and there is no apparent offset from the broadband images. We keep this object as a viable candidate.

gmw_00006330.name: LAE17
gmw_00006330.overview: LAE17 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha galaxy [arXiv:1509.03996]. Hubble Space Telescope imaging reveals a counterpart to the galaxy's emission, which displays a small offset from broadband images [arXiv:1509.03996].

sources.summary: LAE17 is kept as viable because HST imaging shows a counterpart to the emission and the offset from broadband images is small.

quotes.quote: LAE17 The HST F814W image shows a counterpart to the emission. The offset between and the broadband images is, however, small. We keep this object as a viable candidate.

gmw_00006331.name: LBG02
gmw_00006331.overview: LBG02 is a high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy candidate [arXiv:1509.03996]. Its emission is associated with a northern substructure, and it exhibits positive surrounding signal despite a negative continuum subtraction at its reference position [arXiv:1509.03996].

sources.summary: LBG02 has emission associated with a northern substructure, positive surrounding signal despite negative continuum subtraction at the reference position, and is treated as viable.

quotes.quote: LBG02 The emission is associated with a substructure (with a 0.8 separation) to the North. The continuum subtracted is negative at the reference position but there is positive signal surrounding the position, including the location of the emission. This is a viable candidate.

gmw_00006332.name: LBG04
gmw_00006332.overview: LBG04 is a high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy [arXiv:2003.03905] with a systemic redshift of 3.3152 [arXiv:2003.03905]. It is a candidate Lyman continuum emitter [arXiv:2003.03905] that features a single small Lyman continuum clump offset by 0.8 arcseconds from its rest-frame ultraviolet continuum peak [arXiv:2003.03905, arXiv:1509.03996].

sources.summary: The paper describes LCG-2 as a lower-escape-fraction LyC candidate, confirms its emission-line redshift, and notes possible but unconfirmed faint AGN signatures.

quotes.quote: LCG-2 is also reported by Mic17MNRAS.465..316M as ``LBG04'' in their paper. The LyC image reveals a single small LyC clump, with a spatial offset of 0.8^ from the peak of the rest-frame UV continuum see figure 2 in Mic17MNRAS.465..316M . In this case, we set the slitlet to simultaneously cover the peak of the UV continuum and the LyC clump. The spectroscopic redshift is measured by Ste03ApJ...592..728S . In Ste03ApJ...592..728S , LCG-2 is referred to as ``SSA22b-oD8'' and shows z_ spec, Ly = 3.323 and z_ spec, abs = 3.311 .

For LCG-2, we detect the three emission lines ( [ O iii ] 4959, 5007 and H ). The systemic redshift estimated from [ O iii ] 5007 is z_ spec, sys = 3.3152 , which is consistent with the redshifts reported by Ste03ApJ...592..728S and Mic17MNRAS.465..316M since z_ spec, sys typically lies somewhere between z_ spec, Ly and z_ spec, abs e.g. Ste10ApJ...717..289S . Since we do not find any other suspicious emission lines, there is no evidence that the LyC photons detected in the Subaru/ NB359 filter stems from a low- z interloper.

sources.summary: LBG04 has an established z=3.31 redshift, an offset and marginal detection, and is nevertheless retained as a viable candidate.

quotes.quote: LBG04 The redshift for this object ( z=3.31 ) was established by 2003ApJ...592..728S . The detection is offset from the broadband by 0.8 and it is marginal even for measurements at the offset position ( 3.1 ). This object is retained as a viable candidate.

gmw_00006333.name: LBG05
gmw_00006333.overview: LBG05 is a high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy candidate with a confirmed redshift of z=3.102 [arXiv:1509.03996]. Its ultraviolet peak aligns well with substructures detected in both narrowband and Hubble Space Telescope F336W imaging [arXiv:1509.03996].

sources.summary: LBG05 has literature and FOCAS spectral evidence around z=3.1, aligned HST and narrowband structure, but is flagged as a possible unconfirmed contaminant after another study.

quotes.quote: LBG05 This object is from 2013ApJ...765...47N , whose optical spectrum shows a line at z=3.1 . They mention (but do not show) a near-IR spectrum with a possible [ OIII ] emission at z=2.88 , at 0.7 separation. This object was consequently removed from their candidate list. Our FOCAS 2008 spectrum, although noisy, confirms the z=3.102 of the emission and shows no emission at for an assumed redshift of z=2.88 , which is why we retain this object as a candidate. The available F336W HST image shows an object well aligned with the substructure we see in our narrowband image, and there is no offset between the UV peak and F336W or the narrowband image.

gmw_00006334.name: LBG07
gmw_00006334.overview: LBG07 is a high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy with a confirmed redshift of z=3.09 [arXiv:1509.03996]. Hubble Space Telescope imaging reveals a diffuse counterpart to the emission, although a large offset of 1.3 arcseconds between the ultraviolet and Ly-alpha emission makes it a possibly contaminated source [arXiv:1509.03996].

sources.summary: LBG07 has poor LRIS spectrum quality but confirmed redshift from earlier spectroscopy, an HST counterpart, and a large UV-emission offset leading to possible-contaminant status.

quotes.quote: LBG07 Our LRIS 2010 spectrum for this object is of poor quality and heavily affected by a nearby star, and no line was identified. However, this object is placed at z=3.09 from spectroscopy by both 2003ApJ...592..728S and 2013ApJ...765...47N , so we consider its redshift confirmed. The HST F814W shows a diffuse counterpart to the emission, however we measure a large offset ( 1.3 ) between UV and , and flag this object as a possibly but unconfirmed contaminated one.

gmw_00006335.name: SBG 249
gmw_00006335.overview: SBG 249 is a tide-loud close galaxy pair consisting of NGC 1738 and NGC 1739 [arXiv:astro-ph/0703552]. The system features partially overlapped, independently rotating disks that exhibit opposite rotation directions and profiles strongly affected by mutual tidal influence [arXiv:astro-ph/0703552].

sources.summary: The paper presents SBG 249 as a tide-loud close pair with overlapping disks, asymmetric component rotation curves, independently rotating disks, and a line-of-sight velocity difference consistent with perigalacticon.

quotes.quote: A counterexample to UGC 3995, namely, a ``tide-loud'' close pair, is SBG 249 (Soares et al. 1995). Long-slit spectroscopy has been performed and analyzed by Carvalho Soares (2007, in preparation). The galaxies are NGC 1738 (ESO-LV 5520490) and NGC 1739 (ESO-LV 5520500). They have partially overlapped disks. Two spectra were obtained with the Double Spectrograph instrument mounted at the Palomar 5 m telescope, on 1998 February 27--28.

It is clear that the rotation profiles are strongly affected by their mutual tidal influence. On the other hand, the spectrum of the overlapped portion of the system is clearly disentangled showing that both disks rotate quite independently and, as can be seen in Fig. sbg249 , in opposite directions. This also rules out classification as a CP I, in spite of the disturbed morphological appearance of the pair.

gmw_00006336.name: NGC 7361
gmw_00006336.overview: NGC 7361 is a galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.0043 [arXiv:1709.05085]. It has been directly detected in neutral hydrogen observations, with its integrated spectrum showing strong agreement with archival HIPASS data [arXiv:1709.05085].

sources.summary: The paper reports a direct H I detection of NGC 7361 in the GAMA G23 field and finds its integrated MPIPAF spectrum agrees well with archival HIPASS data.

quotes.quote: We have two direct H i detections of HIPASS detected galaxies, NGC 7361 and ESO 469-G015, at z=0.0043 and z=0.0055 (Figure fig:g23_specs panels (a) and (b), respectively). We found these direct detections through visual inspection of the H i spectra extracted from the G23 field based on optical identifications from NED. Unlike the targeted HIPASS galaxy observations, we can compare the total integrated flux for these two galaxies as they are completed covered by the drift scan. We compare our MPIPAF spectra with the HIPASS spectra integrated over the same area from the archival data cubes ( 20 20 arcmin) for these two galaxies in Figure fig:g23_specs (blue and brown lines, respectively). The integrated MPIPAF spectrum of NGC 7361 shows very good agreement with the HIPASS data both in spectral shape and flux density.

gmw_00006337.name: ESO 469-G015
gmw_00006337.overview: ESO 469-G015 is a nearby, low-mass edge-on disk galaxy [arXiv:2201.00566] that has been directly detected in neutral hydrogen [arXiv:1709.05085]. The galaxy contains extraplanar diffuse ionized gas and extraplanar H II regions, which are associated with a strong rotation velocity lag [arXiv:2201.00566].

sources.summary: The paper studies ESO 469-15 with MUSE and H-alpha imaging, finding strong eDIG/thin-disk correlation and unusually strong rotation lag tied to extraplanar H II regions.

quotes.quote: We investigate the origin of the extraplanar diffuse ionized gas (eDIG) and its predominant ionization mechanisms in five nearby (17--46 Mpc) low-mass ( 10^9 - 10^ 10 M_ ) edge-on disk galaxies: ESO 157-49, ESO 469-15, ESO 544-27, IC 217, and IC 1553. We acquired Multi Unit Spectroscopic Explorer (MUSE) integral field spectroscopy and deep narrowband H imaging of our sample galaxies. To investigate the connection between in-plane star formation and eDIG, we measure the star formation rates (SFRs) and perform a photometric analysis of our narrowband H imaging. Using our MUSE data, we investigate the origin of eDIG via kinematics, specifically the rotation velocity lags. We also construct standard diagnostic diagrams and emission-line maps (EW(H ), [ N   ii ] /H , [ S   ii ] //H , [ O   iii ] /H ) and search for regions consistent with ionization by hot low-mass evolved stars (HOLMES) and shocks.

ESO 469-15 on the contrary, has significantly stronger lag at all radii compared to the simulation model. This results also in a steeper radial lag gradient in the central parts of the galaxy. This gradient is likely not related to radial inflow but rather to the extraplanar H  ii regions of ESO 469-15, which seem to have greater lag than the more diffuse extraplanar gas of the other galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper reports a direct H I detection of ESO 469-G015 in the GAMA G23 field; its MPIPAF spectrum has similar shape but slightly lower flux than archival HIPASS, within uncertainties.

quotes.quote: We have two direct H i detections of HIPASS detected galaxies, NGC 7361 and ESO 469-G015, at z=0.0043 and z=0.0055 (Figure fig:g23_specs panels (a) and (b), respectively). We found these direct detections through visual inspection of the H i spectra extracted from the G23 field based on optical identifications from NED. Unlike the targeted HIPASS galaxy observations, we can compare the total integrated flux for these two galaxies as they are completed covered by the drift scan. We compare our MPIPAF spectra with the HIPASS spectra integrated over the same area from the archival data cubes ( 20 20 arcmin) for these two galaxies in Figure fig:g23_specs (blue and brown lines, respectively).

While the integrated MPIPAF spectrum of ESO 469-G015 has a similar spectral shape, it has a slightly lower flux density. Nevertheless, both spectra agree within the combined uncertainties (Table table:g23_fluxes ).

gmw_00006338.name: Mrk 1486
gmw_00006338.overview: Mrk 1486 is a highly inclined, edge-on disk galaxy [arXiv:2108.13211, arXiv:1511.05406] with a low-metallicity interstellar medium [arXiv:2308.06918] and a high star formation rate [arXiv:2308.06918]. This starburst activity drives a metal-enriched, non-spherical bipolar outflow along its minor axis [arXiv:2108.13211, arXiv:2308.06918].

sources.summary: The paper maps Mrk 1486 with direct-method metallicities, finding metal-poor inflow along the disk, metal-enriched outflow along the minor axis, and an edge-on starburst morphology.

quotes.quote: We present electron temperature ( T_e ) maps for the edge-on system Mrk 1486, affording ``direct-method'' gas-phase metallicity measurements across 5. ^ 8 (4.1 kpc) along the minor axis and 9. ^ 9 (6.9 kpc) along the major axis. These maps, enabled by strong detections of the 4363 auroral emission line across a large spatial extent of Mrk 1486, reveal a clear negative minor axis T_e gradient in which temperature decreases with increasing distance from the disk plane. We find that the lowest metallicity spaxels lie near the extremes of the major axis, while the highest metallicity spaxels lie at large spatial offsets along the minor axis. This is consistent with a picture in which low metallicity inflows dilute the metallicity at the edges of the major axis of the disk, while star formation drives metal-enriched outflows along the minor axis. We find that the outflow metallicity in Mrk 1486 is 0.20 dex (1.6 times) higher than the average ISM metallicity, and more than 0.80 dex (6.3 times) higher than metal-poor inflowing gas, which we observe to be below 5  Z_ .

Mrk 1486 is a star-forming galaxy at z=0.033841 ( band magnitude m_r = 16.81 ) with stellar mass log( M_*/M_ ) = 9.3 0.2 and star-formation rate SFR = 3.6 0.7  M_ yr ^ 1 Chisholm18 . In HST /WFC3 imaging, Mrk 1486 has morphology consistent with a disk-like galaxy that is oriented almost directly edge-on (Figure  fig:1 ). Narrow-band imaging shows extended H emission along the minor axis and previous studies have shown that this is consistent with bipolar outflows Duval16 .

sources.summary: The paper uses Mrk 1486 as an edge-on starburst case study, measuring asymmetric ionized outflows, opening angles, mass outflow rates, and a non-spherical, non-purely-biconical wind geometry.

quotes.quote: Mrk 1486 is an edge-on disk, with inclination 85^ Chisholm2015 . It has a redshift of z=0.03383 and stellar mass (M_ /M_ )=9.3 0.2 . It is a 5x outlier above the star-formation rate main-sequence, similar to other galaxies with observed strong star-formation driven outflows, with star formation rate SFR =3.6 0.7 M _ yr ^ 1 Chisholm2018b . Mrk 1486 has a low ISM metallicity of 12 + ( O/H )=7.8 Ostlin2014 . Mrk 1486 also hosts a bipolar outflow Duval2016 , visible as extended filamentary structures in HST H imaging (fig. fig:three_colour ).
These properties make Mrk 1486 an ideal target to study basic outflow properties.

We are thus able to determine the 3-dimensional shape of the outflow in Mrk 1486, and find it to be neither spherical nor purely biconical. A plausible cause of both the wide-base and disk-plane outflows is the distribution of star formation within Mrk 1486.

sources.summary: The paper uses Mrk 1486 as a direct-method outflow-metallicity comparison, noting a minor-axis profile reaching 1.6 kpc and metallicity 1.6 times the ISM.

quotes.quote: More recently, pioneered a self-consistent study of the metal content throughout the baryon cycle using direct-method abundances in the disk, inflow, and outflow of Mrk 1486. They observed an Z_ out /Z_ ISM 1.6 . This emission line metal ratio is only half that of the absorption-line value from for the same galaxy.

The only other direct-method outflow metallicity profile was observed by . They measured the metallicity profile along the minor axis of Mrk 1486 up to 1.6 kpc away from the midplane. They found that the metallicity at the larger distance was 1.6 times Z_ ISM . Mrk 1486 has metallicity of 0.05-0.1 Z _ .

sources.summary: The paper describes LARS 5 as a compact edge-on disk with Ly absorption in the disk, an extended halo, filamentary outflow-like structures, and non-disk-like kinematics.

quotes.quote: LARS 5 ( Mrk 1486 ) In the HST images LARS 5 appears as a small (i.e. projected diameter d 5 kpc) highly inclined edge-on disk. Within the apparent disk Ly appears in absorption but the galaxy shows an extended halo, which is azimuthally symmetric at low surface brightness isophotes, while brighter isophotes resemble more the elongated H shape. Most prominent in the H image are filamentary ``fingers'' extending below and above the disk, reminiscent of an outflowing wind. Although the profile of our single-dish H i observations shows multiple peaks close to the noise level that might correspond to the peaks of a faint double horn profile Pardy2014 , our H kinematics appear incompatible with a typical disk scenario: Firstly, our measured v_ shear = 37 km s ^ 1 indicates a very low rotation curve amplitude - untypical even for a small disk e.g. in the largest H disk sample of for d 5 kpc the maximum rotation curve velocity is on average 95 km s ^ 1 Epinat2010 .

sources.summary: LARS05 is discussed using HST optical data, with relatively bright contours and radial colors indicating well-mixed stellar populations across the visible galaxy.

quotes.quote: LARS05 The two contours in panel D in Fig. fig:lars05 are at 23.4 and 24.7 mag/arcsec ^2 . All optical data for this galaxy are HST data, resampled to the resolution of the K band. For this reason, the faintest contour we could obtain is relatively bright and does not reveal any faint underlying structure. In the SEMs diagrams, the radial color profiles cluster around the same region, consistent with a mixed stellar populations model with a young mass fraction of 10^ 2 . The figure suggests that the entire visible area of LARS05 in the optical data consists of well-mixed stellar populations, since no significant population change can be observed and the radial profile points cluster around similar values.

sources.summary: LARS 05/Mrk 1486 is presented as a blue, highly inclined edge-on disk where central absorption gives way to rapidly rising emission and a symmetric halo out to about 4 kpc.

quotes.quote: LARS 05 Mrk 1486 is blue highly inclined (edge-on) disk galaxy with SFR of around 2 . In the very central regions -- along the plane of the disk -- \ absorption of the stellar continuum is quite strong, and cancels much of the emission, resulting in a narrow absorbing band. However, this gives way to emission at small projected distances and \ luminosity and EW rise rapidly; EW >500 \ is reached at a projected distance of 2.5 kpc from the disk. The large-scale morphology is one of an azimuthally symmetric and featureless halo of emission, although the aperture growth-curve in \ appears to flatten at a radius of 4 kpc which would indicate that we are able to capture most of the \ emission.

sources.summary: The paper describes LARS 5 as having a broad but shallow LIS metal profile with two likely subsystems, possible incomplete opacity in Si II 1304, and weak fluorescent emission.

quotes.quote: The average LIS metal profile of LARS 5 is broad, but relatively shallow, and shows a notable pair of minima at 90 km s ^ 1 and 235 km s ^ 1 , probably representing at least two distinct subsystems. Si ii 1304 is markedly shallower than the rest of the lines, possibly indicating that the medium is not completely opaque at this wavelength. However, the best fits at line center still agree very well with the hypothesis of full opacity and part covering, and the f_C(v) profile coincides remarkably well with the average line profile. Fluorescent emission at 1194, 1305 is present but is the weakest of the galaxies so far mentioned.

sources.summary: The paper cites Mrk 1486 as an edge-on disk galaxy with biconical outflows whose minor-axis winds are metal enriched, though enrichment estimates differ by about a factor of two.

quotes.quote: Cameron+2021 circumvent uncertainties related to ionization by directly estimating the electron temperature from auroral lines in the winds of Mrk 1486 , an edge-on disc galaxy with biconical outflows Duval+2016 . They find that outflows along the minor axis of the galaxy are enriched with metals compared to the ISM of the disc. It is worthwhile to note that though both and conclude that the outflows in Mrk 1486 are metal enriched, their estimates of the degree of enrichment differ by a factor of two, likely indicative of the level of observational uncertainty.

gmw_00006339.name: NGC 2770B
gmw_00006339.overview: NGC 2770B is a dwarf companion galaxy located to the west of NGC 2770 [arXiv:2008.08091, arXiv:1011.2521]. The two galaxies are actively interacting, connected by a clear bridge of material [arXiv:2008.08091], and NGC 2770B exhibits a very high specific star formation rate and very low metallicity [arXiv:1011.2521].

sources.summary: The paper identifies B as NGC 2770’s western companion, gives its alternate designation, finds discrepant velocity relative to the main disk, and confirms an interacting bridge.

quotes.quote: A companion galaxy called B in fynbo08gcn , soderberg08 , and thone09 is detected in 3 (24 kpc) to the west from the centre of . This galaxy was first reported by garciaruiz02 , as KUG 0906+333A, based on the WHISP data we are using. Its velocity is clearly inconsistent with the rotation of the main disc of , having a negative velocity with respect to the systemic velocity, whereas the nearby north-western tip of the galaxy has a positive velocity. B is connected to the north-western tip of by a clear bridge.
The bridge between and B is definitive proof that they are interacting.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 2770B as a dwarf companion of NGC 2770 with high specific star formation, very low metallicity, and no detected supernovae.

quotes.quote: NGC 2770 does have a dwarf companion, NGC 2770B, and based upon recent work on the local group of galaxies e.g. mcconnachie2009nature , it is clear that collisions with dwarf galaxies are ongoing into the present epoch. However, the star formation rate of NGC 2770B appears to be large; in fact, the specific star formation rate of NGC 2770B is very high and it is one of the most metal poor galaxies ever detected, indicating a very young stellar population thone2009apj . Although the specific star formation rate of NGC 2770B is larger than for NGC 2770, there have not been any SNe detected in NGC 2770B, which can be explained by the fact that we are just taking an instantaneous snapshot compared to the 10^7-10^8 yr timescale at which SNe trail star formation.

gmw_00006340.name: JO20
gmw_00006340.overview: JO20 is an isolated galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.1471 [arXiv:2104.02089]. It is undergoing a major 1:1 merger, featuring two bright nuclei of similar mass and a clear tail extending toward the north [arXiv:2104.02089].

sources.summary: The paper presents JO20 as an isolated, distant GASP galaxy undergoing a clear major merger, with twin nuclei, a northern tail, disturbed gas, and merger-remnant kinematics.

quotes.quote: In this section we will focus on JO20, which is a very clear case of a major 1:1 merger and use this galaxy to outline the typical features of galaxies undergoing mergers. JO20 is found in the field of view of the cluster Abell 147 for which there is no spectroscopic coverage available. Indeed, JO20 had no redshift before MUSE observations and it is the farthest away object in our sample (z=0.1471). It is quite isolated in the universe: the closest galaxy at similar measured redshift is at 18 .

Figure fig:merger shows the rgb image, the gas and stellar kinematics and their velocity dispersions, the BPT, the ionized gas metallicity, In this case metallicities have been derived neglecting the information from the BPT classification. the mass density and luminosity weighted age maps for JO20. The rgb image highlights the presence of a clear tail extending towards North. Two bright nuclei are visible and these are most likely the remnants of the two merging galaxies. These have similar brightness and size, suggesting the two objects had similar mass.

gmw_00006341.name: JO134
gmw_00006341.overview: JO134 is a galaxy with a highly irregular morphology, featuring a low-luminosity central region, bright blue knots to the northeast, and a low-luminosity southern tail [arXiv:2104.02089]. Its chaotic stellar kinematics and structure suggest it is simultaneously experiencing ram pressure stripping and a minor merger [arXiv:2104.02089].

sources.summary: The paper describes JO134 as the field sample’s clear case of coexisting mechanisms, with irregular morphology, blue northern knots, a southern tail, chaotic stellar kinematics, and likely ram pressure plus minor merger effects.

quotes.quote: JO134: a case of multiple processes To conclude this overview, we focus on a galaxy that might be simultaneously affected by two mechanisms. This is actually the only clear example of coexistence of two mechanisms in the field sample, suggesting that typically the presence of a dominant mechanism washes out the effect of the secondary one but see for an example in GASP clusters Fritz2017 . Figure fig:mul shows the rgb image, the flux, the and stellar kinematics, the metallicity, the luminosity weighted age maps and star formation histories maps in 4 age bins for JO134, while Fig. fig:JO134_env shows its environment. The rgb image unveils a low luminosity central part, and a very clear bright region towards North East, composed by many blue knots.

Overall the galaxy morphology is very irregular, especially in the North-West side of the galaxy. The maps shows very bright regions in the Northern part, and a clear low luminosity tail towards the South is visible. The stellar kinematics is much more chaotic and no regular rotation is detected. The most probable scenario that could explain the characteristics of JO134 is that on one side the galaxy is falling towards a group and therefore feeling ram pressure stripping that is producing the bow shock in the Northern part and the tail developing on the opposite side, and on the other hand it is also undergoing a minor merger event.

gmw_00006342.name: P12823
gmw_00006342.overview: P12823 is an inclined spiral galaxy that is part of a binary system with a companion galaxy located 30 arcseconds away [arXiv:2104.02089]. The galaxy exhibits disk distortions, shredded gas facing its companion, and disturbed kinematics on the companion-facing side [arXiv:2104.02089].

sources.summary: The paper identifies P12823 as an inclined spiral in a binary interaction, with disk distortions, shredded gas toward its companion, clouds between the galaxies, and disturbed kinematics on the companion-facing side.

quotes.quote: Galaxy-Galaxy Interactions P12823 Figure fig:interacting_a shows that P12823 is a spiral galaxy with quite high inclination ( i=65^o ). The galaxy is part of binary system Tempel2014_g and its companion is visible in the rgb, 30 \ away from P12823. This galaxy P12813, Calvi2011TheGalaxies is at z= 0.05023 and no stellar mass estimate is available from the literature; we therefore can not compute the strength of the tidal interaction. The rgb image highlights some distortions in the external parts of the disk.

Such distortions are more evident from the flux map, which shows that the portion of the disk facing the companion is shredded. Some clouds are also visible in between the two objects, suggesting that stripped gas may have triggered the formation of new stars. Both the gas and the stellar kinematics are overall regular ( A_v =0.15 and A_g =0.17) , showing significant distortions only on the side closer to the companion, where the stellar velocity dispersion is also much higher. This might suggest that the galaxies are approaching their first pericenter passage.

gmw_00006343.name: JO190
gmw_00006343.overview: JO190 is an edge-on galaxy located within a local overdensity [arXiv:2104.02089]. It is classified as an old minor merger, featuring a long, shell-shaped extraplanar tail with bright blue knots and a possible remnant clump [arXiv:2104.02089].

sources.summary: The paper classifies JO190 as an old minor merger, citing its overdense environment, peculiar edge-on morphology, extraplanar shell-like tail, blue knots, possible remnant clump, and settled gas and stellar rotation.

quotes.quote: Mergers JO190 JO190 is the closest galaxy in the sample, at z= 0.013218. The galaxy is part of an overdensity: within 1 degree from JO190, about 40 galaxies have similar redshift (0.013 <z< 0.015). The galaxy has been included in the merger category even though it does present a rather regular stellar kinematics ( A_v = 0.3 ) . The rgb image in Fig. fig:merger_JO190 shows the peculiar morphology of JO190: the main body of the galaxy is in the northern part and the galaxy is quite edge on.

Then a long extraplanar tail extends towards the South, with a shell shape typical of tidal interactions e.g., Peirani2010 . Many bright blue knots are visible in the image: these might correspond to star-forming regions that could eventually end up as tidal dwarf galaxies as in Vulcani2017c . A redder clump in the tail is particularly outstanding and it could be the remnant of a minor merger, being at the same redshift of JO190. To conclude, JO190 is most likely an example of an old minor merger: both the stars and gas have had the time to settle down and again acquire a rather regular rotation.

gmw_00006344.name: P3984
gmw_00006344.overview: P3984 is an isolated post-merger galaxy resulting from a gas-rich dwarf-dwarf merger [arXiv:2104.02089]. It features a bent, stream-like morphology with a blue compact core, tattered gas clouds, and chaotic stellar kinematics that contrast with its gas velocity field [arXiv:2104.02089].

sources.summary: The paper presents P3984 as an isolated post gas-rich dwarf-dwarf merger, with bent stream-like morphology, a blue compact core, tattered gas, chaotic stellar kinematics, and contrasting gas and stellar behavior.

quotes.quote: P3984 P3984 is most likely a case of post gas rich dwarf–dwarf merger. The rgb image shown in Fig. fig:merger_P3984 highlights the presence of a bent shape, similar to a stream, with a very blue compact core. The latter is usually used as evidence for the formation via dwarf–dwarf merging Bekki2008 . The larger extent of the current galaxy compared to the original is clearly visible, indication that some event puffed up the galaxy disk.

The flux map shows a tattered distribution, especially in the western part of the galaxy, where a sort of tidal is evident. The gas velocity field indicates that these clouds do belong to the galaxy, as they have velocities similar to that of the galaxy. In contrast, the stellar kinematics is chaotic and shows little rotation ( A_s =0.39) . The different kinematics between stars and gas is another indication that the galaxy is the remnant of a dwarf-dwarf merger BekkiChiba2008 .

gmw_00006345.name: P877
gmw_00006345.overview: P877 is a candidate galaxy merger that exhibits a complex morphology, including a southern horn-like protuberance and several detached gas clouds in its surroundings [arXiv:2104.02089]. The galaxy displays highly asymmetric ionized gas kinematics with minimal rotation, a double-peaked stellar velocity dispersion, and a few young star-forming regions likely triggered by the merger [arXiv:2104.02089].

sources.summary: The paper treats P877 as a probable but uncertain merger, noting detached gas clouds, a horn-like protuberance, highly asymmetric gas kinematics, double-peaked stellar dispersion, and young regions likely triggered by the event.

quotes.quote: P877 Among the merging candidates, P877 is the least certain. Nonetheless, the flux map unveils a more complicated morphology. Many detached clouds are detected in the disk surroundings. A horn extending from the southern part of the galaxy towards East is also detected.

The ionized gas kinematics is extremely asymmetric ( A_g =0.98) and has very little rotation ( | (v)|=25 km/s, A_v=0.31 ). The stellar velocity dispersion map shows a a double-peaked distribution, which can be explained in terms of edge-on gas rings due to 1:1 merger Jesseit2007 or due to edge-on barred disks Bureau2004, Chung2004 . Only a few young regions are evident (LWA <10^ 7.5 yr) and these might correspond to star-forming regions ignited by the merger. To conclude, there is no clear smoking gun for mergers, even though it is the most probable process.

gmw_00006346.name: P63947
gmw_00006346.overview: P63947 is a minor merger candidate that exhibits a highly lopsided structure and a tidal tail encircling its eastern side [arXiv:2104.02089]. The galaxy features a clumpy disk, distorted stellar and ionized-gas kinematics, and a flat metallicity distribution indicative of mixed gas [arXiv:2104.02089].

sources.summary: The paper describes P63947 as a minor merger candidate, with strong lopsidedness, a tidal tail, clumpy gas, distorted stellar and ionized-gas kinematics, mixed metallicity, and young southern material.

quotes.quote: P63947 P63947 is another minor merger candidate. The rgb image in Fig. fig:merger_P63947 shows a strong lopsidedness ( A_o=70 ) and a tidal tail encircling the body in the eastern side of the galaxy. This is a clear feature of interaction/merger Mihos1993 . The asymmetry is even more visible in the flux map, which also reveals a very clumpy disk.

The ionized gas presents very little and distorted rotation: the maximum relative velocity of the disk is of only 25 km/s and the locus of zero velocity is chaotic ( A_g=0.61 ) . The velocity field of the stellar component shows the rotation is distorted along the tidal tail ( A_v=0.45 ) , where the velocity dispersion is also higher. P63947 has a quite flat metallicity distribution, indicating that gas of different metallicity might have been mixed during the merger and gradients have been destroyed Kobayashi2004 . Given that we have no clear evidence for the merged companion, we label this galaxy as a minor merger candidate.

gmw_00006347.name: P20159
gmw_00006347.overview: P20159 is a highly inclined galaxy located in a small three-member group [arXiv:2104.02089]. It features an elongated south-western tail containing young stars born from stripped gas, likely caused by ram pressure stripping [arXiv:2104.02089].

sources.summary: The paper presents P20159 as a possible group ram-pressure case, with a south-western elongated tail, coherent but asymmetric gas and stellar rotation, young stars in the tail, and a peculiar metallicity distribution.

quotes.quote: Ram pressure stripping P20159 P20159 is a (M_ /M_ ) =9.6 galaxy found in a small 3 member group Tempel2014_g, Saulder2016 . Taking all these values with caution, we can estimate that P20159 is at 2.2 R_ vir , and has a velocity difference from the group center of 318 km/s, corresponding to 3.7 . The rgb image of P20159 in Fig. fig:rps_P20159 highlights the high inclination of the galaxy ( i=80^o ) and shows the presence of an elongated tail extending towards South-West. The tail is also clearly visible in and the BPT map suggests that in that region star formation is not the dominant ionizing mechanism.

Gas and stars rotate coherently, with the gas showing a higher asymmetry than the stars ( A_g =0.41 , A_v=0.18 ) . Stars are present in the tail, but as suggested by the analysis of the SFH they are mostly young, born out of the stripped gas. The SFH maps also show that the galaxy had an enhancement of the star formation in the core for t<6 10^8 yr. The galaxy has a peculiar metallicity distribution: the metallicity profile of the leading part of the galaxy is almost flat, while the trailing part shows a quite steep metallicity gradient.

gmw_00006348.name: P59597
gmw_00006348.overview: P59597 is a highly inclined galaxy located near a large group and a binary system [arXiv:2104.02089]. It is a confirmed case of group-induced ram pressure stripping, featuring a trail of bright knots, asymmetric gas kinematics, and a recent central starburst [arXiv:2104.02089].

sources.summary: The paper classifies P59597 as a certain group-induced ram-pressure case, citing its nearby group, trail of bright knots, extended gas structures, asymmetric gas kinematics, recent central burst, and low-metallicity tail.

quotes.quote: P59597 P59597 is a (M_ /M_ ) =9.7 galaxy found in proximity of a large group and close to a binary system. However, Fig. fig:rps_env shows that the automatic approach might have broken the same structure into many smaller systems and P59597 could actually be part of this larger group. P59597 is seen with a quite high inclination ( i=70^o ). The rgb image in Fig. fig:rps_P59597 shows a trail of bright knots extending towards the North-West.

As highlighted by the contours, the main body of the galaxy is located in the southern part of the galaxy, the upper part could be a spiral arm opening up. The gas kinematic is stretched towards South and asymmetric. The SFH maps indicate the galaxy had a central burst in the recent epochs. To conclude, this galaxy can be considered a certain case of ram pressure stripping induced by the hosting group.

gmw_00006349.name: P63692
gmw_00006349.overview: P63692 is a low-mass dwarf galaxy that is likely experiencing cosmic-web stripping [arXiv:2104.02089]. It features a lopsided, tail-like gas emission with a bright clump, an asymmetric gas velocity field, and low metallicity [arXiv:2104.02089].

sources.summary: The paper describes P63692 as GASP’s least massive galaxy and a likely cosmic-web stripping case, with lopsided tail-like emission, clumps, asymmetric gas velocity, low metallicity, and nearby group geometry.

quotes.quote: Cosmic web stripping P63692 P63692 is the least massive galaxy in GASP, with a mass of 10^ 8.9 . The rgb in Fig. fig:cws_a does not highlight a clear central structure but highlights a lopsidedness towards the East that resembles a gas tail and whose presence is supported by the analysis of the extent (Fig. fig:tail ) . The same lopsidedness and tail are confirmed in the flux map, where a bright clump is detected. The gas velocity field is asymmetric ( A_g=0.35 ) , spanning a range -80 <v_ gas [ km/s ] < 30.

The galaxy has very low metallicity ( 12+ O/H 8 ), but in the very end of the tail, where it reaches 12+ O/H 8.6 . The SFH maps shows that the galaxy acquired the current extent only for t<5.7 10^8 yr. P63692 is not connected to any bound structure, so ram pressure stripping might not be advocated. The galaxy is therefore most likely experiencing cosmic web stripping, even though we can state it clearly, mainly due to the high uncertainties in the stellar velocity field.

gmw_00006350.name: P96244
gmw_00006350.overview: P96244 is a spiral galaxy that features unwinding arms and a clear tail extending toward the south [arXiv:2104.02089]. This tail, which contains small detached gas clouds, is a signature of ram pressure stripping [arXiv:2104.02089].

sources.summary: The paper uses P96244 as the best ram-pressure candidate, describing a spiral with a clear southern tail, unwinding arms, detached gas clouds, and tail asymmetry exceeding undisturbed control galaxies.

quotes.quote: The best candidate is P96244 and will be used to outline the main features of galaxies falling in this class. Its main properties are shown in Fig. fig:rps : the rgb image, the BPT, flux, gas, and stellar kinematics, metallicity and the SFH maps. As the hosting environment is a key feature for understanding if ram pressure stripping can be advocated as the main mechanism, Fig. fig:rps_env shows the surrounding region of P96244. Its rgb image shows that P96244 is a spiral galaxy with a clear tail extending towards South.

The signatures of unwinding arms (i.e. dislodged material that appears to retain the original structure of the spiral arms) are also observed, a feature that can be induced by ram pressure stripping Bellhouse2020 . The tail is even more visible in the flux map, where small detached clouds are also detected. The presence of the tail for this and for all the other ram pressure and cosmic web stripping candidates has been confirmed by comparing the extent of along two opposite directions with the extent of in undisturbed galaxies (see Sec. sec:results2 for details on the control sample). In contrast, stripping candidates typically exceed this value, allowing us to conclude that they are characterized by a tail.

gmw_00006351.name: SDSS J0100+1818
gmw_00006351.overview: SDSS J0100+1818 is a massive early-type elliptical galaxy located at a spectroscopic redshift of z=0.581 [arXiv:2211.16528]. It acts as a rare strong gravitational lens on two background sources and is a candidate fossil system [arXiv:2211.16528].

sources.summary: The paper models SDSS J0100+1818 as a rare ultra-massive elliptical strong lens, measuring its stellar mass, velocity dispersion, total mass profile, and possible overdense environment.

quotes.quote: ForsterSchreiber2009, Canameras2017b, Cava2018, Iani2021 In this paper, we measure the total and baryonic mass distributions of the deflector SDSS J010049.18+181827.7 (hereafter, SDSS J0100+1818), included in the Cambridge And Sloan Survey Of Wide ARcs in the skY (CASSOWARY) survey Belokurov2009, Stark2013 The ID of this system has changed in the CASSOWARY survey in the past years. The exceptionally large Einstein radius of 42 kpc , together with the results from our follow-up observations with the Nordic Optical Telescope (NOT), the Very Large Telescope (VLT) and the Hubble Space Telescope ( HST ), presented in Section sec:data , suggest that the SDSS J0100+1818 deflector is an uncommon strong lens and a candidate fossil system at intermediate redshift Johnson2018 . VLT/X-Shooter spectroscopy SDSS J0100+1818 was observed with VLT/X-Shooter Vernet2011 (program 091.A-0852, PI: Christensen), in order to measure the lens and source spectroscopic redshifts, and to infer the lens stellar velocity dispersion. SDSS J0100+1818, (RA, dec )=( 01:00:49.18, +18:18:27.79), was introduced in a late version of the CASSOWARY catalog, and was not included in the spectroscopic confirmation program from Stark2013 .

This results in a stellar velocity dispersion value of (451 ± 37) km s ^ 1 , confirming that the main lens galaxy of SDSS J0100+1818 is among the rarest, most massive elliptical galaxies Loeb2003 . In this paper, we have studied SDSS J0100+1818, a strong lensing system consisting of a massive early-type galaxy, with a spectroscopic redshift of z=0.581 , which acts as a gravitational lens on two background sources, AB and C. We have measured that = (451 37) km s ^ 1 , which is consistent with the very large values of the galaxy stellar mass and of the mean distance between the observed multiple images, confirming that SDSS J0100+1818 is among the rarest, most massive elliptical galaxies Loeb2003 .

gmw_00006352.name: VDESJ0246-5219
gmw_00006352.overview: VDESJ0246-5219 is a high-redshift quasar [arXiv:1901.07456]. Based on the onset of the Lyman-alpha forest, the object has a derived redshift of approximately 6.90 [arXiv:1901.07456].

sources.summary: The paper identifies VDESJ0246-5219 as one of three newly confirmed DES+VHS z>6.5 quasars and derives a Ly-forest redshift near 6.90.

quotes.quote: Our photometric selection method is shown to be highly efficient in identifying clean samples of high-redshift quasars leading to spectroscopic confirmation of three new quasars - VDESJ \ 0244 5008 ( z=6.724 ), VDESJ \ 0020 3653 ( z=6.834 ) and VDESJ \ 0246 5219 ( z=6.90 ) - which were selected as the highest priority candidates in the survey data without any need for additional follow-up observations. The new quasars span the full range in luminosity covered by other z>6.5 quasar samples (J _ AB =20.2 to 21.3 ; M _ 1450 =-25.6 to 26.6 ).

The two candidates - VDESJ0246-5219 This quasar was recently independently discovered by Yang2018 and VDESJ0020-3653 - identified from the DES Y3 data, were observed using the European Southern Observatory's 3.6m New Technology Telescope (NTT). Observations were taken during December 2016 and November 2017 as part of programmes 098.A-0439 and 0100.A-0346 respectively.

The reduced spectra can be seen in the top two panels of Fig. optSpecs and confirms the identity of both candidates as high-redshift quasars. Based on the onset of the Ly forest we derive redshifts of 6.90 0.02 and 6.86 0.01 for VDESJ0246-5219 and VDESJ0020-3653 respectively.

gmw_00006353.name: HE 0238 1904
gmw_00006353.overview: HE 0238 1904 is a luminous, radio-quiet quasar located at a redshift of approximately 0.63 [arXiv:2302.00300, arXiv:2309.00053] in an unusually overdense environment [arXiv:2309.00053]. The system features a giant ionized nebula [arXiv:2309.00053] and an active galactic nucleus-driven superbubble that hosts a large-scale, one-sided outflow [arXiv:2302.00300].

sources.summary: The paper presents VLT/MUSE observations of HE 0238 1904, identifying a 55 kpc AGN-driven superbubble and arguing its large-scale feedback is weak.

quotes.quote: We report on the discovery of a rare case of spatially and kinematically resolved galactic scale outflow at intermediate redshift, based on VLT/MUSE optical integral field spectroscopic observation of the quasar HE 0238 1904. This classical non-broad absorption line (non-BAL) quasar at z=0.631 remains underexplored in its optical emission lines, though its UV absorption lines are well-studied. We identify a superbubble driven by HE 0238 1904 from the emission line morphology, line ratio diagnostics and kinematics, showing a one-sided outflow reaching a projected distance of R 55 kpc from the nucleus.

The bulk of the ionized gas, with a characteristic mass M 10^ 8 M_ , is blueshifted by v 700 km s ^ 1 with respect to the quasar systemic velocity. The outflows detected using absorption line and the emission line are likely stratified components of different spatial scale and velocity in the ionized phase outflow. Although feedback in HE 0238 1904 is taking place on kpc scale, the kinetic power of the outflow at 55 kpc ( 0.1 L_ bol ) implies that it is inadequate to regulate effectively the evolution of the host galaxy at this large scale.

sources.summary: The paper includes HE0238 1904 as a newly analyzed MUSEQuBES QSO field and finds its oxygen-line VSF slopes consistent with Kolmogorov turbulence.

quotes.quote: Out of the seven QSO fields, the measurements for four fields (PKS0454 22, J0454 6116, J2135 5316, and TXS0206 048) were presented in Chen2023 . The three newly included fields (PKS0405 123, HE0238 1904, and PKS0552 640) are all part of the MUSE Quasar-field Blind Emitters Survey (MUSEQuBES) program, and we use the MUSE-DEEP datacubes directly downloaded from the ESO phase-3 archive with program IDs 097.A-0089(A) and 094.A-0131(B) PI: J. Schaye; Muzahid2020 .

Nebulae with _2 consistent with 2/3: the VSF measurements for PKS0405 123 E, HE0238 1904, PKS0552 640 and TXS0206 048 lead to a constrained 2nd-order slope in agreement with the value 2/3. For HE0238 1904 and PKS0552 640, the measurements for both and within the 16 ^ th - 84^ th percentiles are consistent with the Kolmogorov slope. For TXS0206 048, only measurements with are available and the result is consistent with _2=2/3 .

sources.summary: HE 0238 1904 is analyzed as a luminous radio-quiet quasar at z=0.6282 in an overdense environment with a giant ionized nebula and likely merging groups.

quotes.quote: We present the first comprehensive study of a giant, 70 kpc-scale nebula around a radio-quiet quasar at z<1 . The analysis is based on deep integral field spectroscopy with MUSE of the field of HE 0238 1904, a luminous quasar at z=0.6282 . The nebula emits strongly in [ O II ] , H , and [ O III ] , and the quasar resides in an unusually overdense environment for a radio-quiet system. The environment likely consists of two groups which may be merging, and in total have an estimated dynamical mass of M_ dyn 4 10^ 13 to 10^ 14 \ M_ .

gmw_00006354.name: NGC 4881
gmw_00006354.overview: NGC 4881 is an elliptical galaxy located in the Coma Cluster [arXiv:1509.08381, arXiv:1203.2578]. It is characterized by a nearly circular shape, isolation from other galaxies, and a negative color gradient [arXiv:1509.08381, arXiv:1203.2578].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4881 as a surface-photometry template example, noting its Coma location, circular isolated appearance, and measured color gradient in the J-band profile comparison.

quotes.quote: An example of a good fit is shown in Figure ngc4881 , a comparison of the V and J surface brightness profiles and templates for NGC 4881. NGC 4881 is located in the Coma cluster and is a common test galaxy for surface photometry due to its nearly perfectly circular shape and isolation from other galaxies and bright stars. Shown in Figure ngc4881 is a comparison between Johnson V templates from Schombert (1986) and the current set of templates for 2MASS J . The original V templates were based on photographic and early CCD imaging, but display the same slope and scaling relations as the newer J templates (when color gradients are taken into consideration). The NGC 4881 data displays a color gradient of (V-J)/ log \ = 0.15 , which is near the mean value for bright ellipticals (La Barbera 2012).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4881 to test repeatability, comparing V and J surface-brightness profiles and noting its nearly circular elliptical shape and color-gradient differences.

quotes.quote: One such comparison is shown in Figure 4 where we overlay the V and J surface brightness profiles for the Coma elliptical NGC 4881 (the V data has been corrected for V-J =2.57, taken from NED). NGC 4881 is a popular surface brightness target as its elliptical shape is close to a perfect circle and its profile is ^ 1/4 over a large range of surface brightness. The close correspondence between the two profiles is remarkable since the V data is taken from PDS scans of Palomar Schmidt photographic material over 20 years ago. There is a slight difference in slope for the two profiles, however, due to the blue nature of this slope, this is more than likely the result of a small metallicity gradient (i.e., negative V-J slope).

gmw_00006355.name: B2 0722+30
gmw_00006355.overview: B2 0722+30 is a highly inclined late-type disk galaxy [arXiv:1003.2835, arXiv:0908.4391] classified as a Seyfert 2 [arXiv:1504.02772]. It hosts a classical radio source with symmetric jets [arXiv:1003.2835, arXiv:1504.02772] and exists in a rich environment of interacting, gas-rich companion galaxies [arXiv:1003.2835, arXiv:astro-ph/0508438].

sources.summary: The appendix treats B2 0722+30 as the sample's only late-type galaxy, with H I following an edge-on stellar disc, interacting gas-rich neighbors, and an unusually classical radio source.

quotes.quote: B2 0722+30 is the only late-type galaxy in our sample. The regularly rotating \ emission follows the edge-on stellar disc. Part of the \ disc is seen in absorption and not taken into account in the total intensity image of Fig. fig:HIsample and \ mass estimate in Table tab:hiradiogalaxies . B2 0722+30 has an rich environment, with gas-rich galaxies that are in ongoing interaction see emo09 .

The radio source reaches beyond the optical boundary of the host galaxy and has an \ morphology fan86 . It is extremely rare for disc galaxies to host a classical radio source see ver01,kee06,emo08_NGC612,emo09 , hence we warn the reader that B2 0722+30 should be regarded as a special case in our sample.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 0722+30 with a highly inclined spiral galaxy, notes unusual symmetric radio features away from the disk, and reports no VLBA detection.

quotes.quote: 0722+30 This radio source at z=0.0188 is the only one identified with a spiral galaxy (see ). It is a highly inclined galaxy with a strong absorption associated with the disk and a bulge-like component. The radio emission is quite peculiar showing two symmetric jet-like features at an angle of about 45 ^ from the disk (see ). New high resolution VLA images will be discussed in . The source is undetected in our VLBA image, with a S_ peak <0.3 mJy/beam at 5 GHz.

sources.summary: J0725+2957 is identified as a Seyfert 2 in a disk/S0 galaxy, with strong optical absorption and symmetric radio jets inclined to the Galactic disk.

quotes.quote: J0725+2957 This is a Seyfert 2 galaxy with strong absorption lines in the optical spectrum. It is associated with a disk galaxy, particularly an S0, the only source in the B2 sample of this kind. The radio emission originates in two symmetric jets that form an angle of 45 ^ with the Galactic disk .

sources.summary: The paper reports modest neutral hydrogen associated with B2 0722+30, mostly in a disk, with additional blobs toward a companion and a surrounding interacting-galaxy environment.

quotes.quote: B2 1322+36 and B2 0722+30 show only modest amounts of \ ( 10 ^ 8 M _ ) associated with the radio galaxy. Although for B2 0722+30 most of this \ is again located in a disk, in both these galaxies we detect \ ``blobs'' in the direction of a nearby companion galaxy, possibly tracing a bridge-like structure. From Figure fig:hi it is also clear that in particular B2 0722+30 and NGC 3894 are located in an rich environment of nearby interacting galaxies.

gmw_00006356.name: SDSS J1339+0441
gmw_00006356.overview: SDSS J1339+0441 is a high-redshift quasar [arXiv:1404.3760]. Its rest-frame optical emission exhibits a multi-peaked structure, while its oxygen emission line shows a complex velocity structure that may indicate outflows, a dual nucleus, or an assembling host galaxy [arXiv:1404.3760].

sources.summary: The paper presents SDSS J1339+0441 as a high-redshift quasar with multi-peaked rest-frame optical emission, complex velocity structure, and possible outflow, dual-nucleus, or assembling-host interpretations.

quotes.quote: We specifically obtained NIR spectra for two of these sources (SDSS J1339+0441 and SDSS J1444 0013, the latter subsequently fell out of the main sample in Paper I due to revised C 4 line width measurements). Here we compare the C 4 and \ line profiles (Figure fig:onedclump ). In Figure fig:twodclump we show the two-dimensional, flat-fielded and sky-subtracted FIRE spectrum of SDSS J1339+0441, which clearly has a multi-peaked structure in both the 4959 and 5007 lines.

In SDSS J1339+0441 the \ line has a complicated velocity structure with multiple kinematic components, although less distinct than that in C 4 . This profile could be due to outflow kinematics greeneho2005gas,greeneetal2011,liuetal2013a,liuetal2013b or to narrow line regions around distinct black holes e.g., djorgovskietal2007,barthetal2008,comerfordetal2009, liuetal2010a,liuetal2011 , as found in low-redshift objects. Another intriguing possibility specific to high-redshift objects is that the quasar host galaxy is being formed from multiple components which are illuminated by the main quasar e.g., elmegreenetal2008,forsterschreiberetal2011 .

gmw_00006357.name: SDSS J0958+0135
gmw_00006357.overview: SDSS J0958+0135 is a COSMOS quasar characterized by a very red spectral energy distribution [arXiv:1404.3760]. The galaxy exhibits large inferred dust extinction, suppressed ultraviolet luminosity, and detected line emission [arXiv:1404.3760].

sources.summary: The paper highlights SDSS J0958+0135 as a COSMOS quasar with discrepant near-infrared photometry, detected line emission, a very red SED, large inferred extinction, and suppressed UV luminosity.

quotes.quote: SDSS J0958+0135 was observed with COSMOS scovilleetal2007 and so has additional data (Paper I). The UKIDSS magnitude of H=20.2 mag (AB) agrees with the UKIRT value of H=20.0 mag quoted in Paper I ilbertetal2009 , but is two magnitudes brighter than our H=21.97 mag. Such a large discrepancy is difficult to attribute to slit losses alone. We do detect line emission from this object, so it was at least partially in the slit. The apparent SED is quite red; it has SDSS z = 21.9 mag.
The two largest extinction values belong to SDSS J1330+0146 and SDSS J0958+0135 the latter is in COSMOS; Paper I scovilleetal2007 .

Furthermore, we see evidence that the UV luminosities are somewhat suppressed, in the broad-band SED of SDSS J0958+0135 presented in Paper I and also in the low ratio of UV to WISE luminosity (Figure fig:wiselum ). Thus, the continuum measurements support a moderate-obscuration scenario.

gmw_00006358.name: LEDA 074886
gmw_00006358.overview: LEDA 074886 is a rare rectangular dwarf galaxy located at a distance of 21 Mpc [arXiv:1203.3608]. It resides within the hot gas halo of the massive galaxy NGC 1407 and contains an embedded, edge-on stellar disk [arXiv:1203.3608].

sources.summary: The paper presents LEDA 074886 as a rare rectangular dwarf galaxy with an embedded edge-on disk, boxy outer isophotes, and a likely wet-plus-dry merger origin.

quotes.quote: At a distance of 21 Mpc, the dwarf galaxy LEDA 074886 has an absolute R band magnitude of 17.3 mag. Adding to this galaxy's intrigue is the presence of an embedded, edge-on stellar disk (of extent 2R_ e,disk =12 =1.2 kpc) for which Forbes et al. \ reported v_ rot / 1.4 . We speculate that this galaxy may be the remnant of two (nearly edge-one) merged disk galaxies in which the initial gas was driven inward and subsequently formed the inner disk, while the stars at larger radii effectively experienced a dissipationless merger event resulting in this `emerald cut galaxy' having very boxy isophotes with a_4/a =-0.05 to 0.08 from 3 to 5 kpc.

We have discovered an exceedingly rare example of a rectangular-shaped galaxy, which may be unique for its luminosity. LEDA 074886 ( = 03h 40m 43.2s, = -18d 38m 43s: J2000) resides within the hot gas halo of the massive, spherical galaxy NGC 1407 (E0), with a projected separation of 50 kpc from this giant galaxy's core. LEDA 074886, which we affectionately call the ``emerald cut galaxy'' given its striking resemblance to an emerald cut diamond, is the 25 ^ th brightest galaxy within the NGC 1407 Group of over 250 galaxies (Trentham, Tully, Mahdavi 2006). At a distance of 21 Mpc (Spolaor et al. \ 2008), LEDA 074886 has an apparent (and absolute) R-band brightness of 14.3 (and -17.3) mag (Trentham et al. \ 2006), roughly corresponding to a stellar mass of 10^9 M_ (see Forbes et al. \ 2011, their section 9.1). The associated spatial scale is such that 1 = 101 pc.

gmw_00006359.name: UVISTA-Y2
gmw_00006359.overview: UVISTA-Y2 is a high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy located in the COSMOS field [arXiv:2203.03657]. It has a spectroscopically confirmed redshift of z=7.677 and exhibits Lyman-alpha emission [arXiv:2203.03657].
