sources.summary: The paper reports Lyman-alpha and ionized-carbon emission for UVISTA-Y2/REBELS-36 at z=7.677, deriving UV star formation, low infrared star formation, offsets, and gas constraints.

quotes.quote: Here we report the detection of \ emission from archival data of a Lyman-Break Galaxy (LBG) at the extreme bright end of the luminosity function with confirmed \ emission at z=7.677 in the COSMOS field, dubbed \ hereafter. This object has been indicated as a high-redshift photometric candidate (UVISTA-Y2 with z_ phot =8.21^ +0.49 _ 0.50 , stefanon_2017, stefanon_2019 ), later followed up with the Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA) in a pilot schrouws_2021 and then in the full Reionization Era Bright Emission Line Survey (REBELS-36 with z_ phot =7.88^ +0.58 _ 0.20 , bouwens_2021 ).

The target was observed for 3.5 hours on December 18 ^ th 2015, with an average seeing of FWHM = 0 89 . We applied the distributed pipeline for the data reduction followed by an optimal extraction of the 1D spectrum horne_1986 . We corrected for the minor telescope drifting and derived the flux calibration based on a star observed within the same mask and in the same conditions as . Finally, we anchored the photometry to the latest UltraVISTA DR4 Y band flux as part of the COSMOS2020 catalog weaver_2022 to correct for further flux losses. We computed a reduced ^2=1.0 in regions of pure background in the 2D frame, confirming the reliability of the noise estimate.

gmw_00006360.name: CSWA 31
gmw_00006360.overview: CSWA 31 is a complex gravitational lens system where a group-group lens acts on a background source [arXiv:1010.5298]. The brightest group galaxy is an ultra-massive, non-rotating early-type galaxy at a redshift of 0.683 [arXiv:2203.13759].

sources.summary: The paper presents the first high-resolution reconstruction of CSWA 31, describing it as a complex group-group gravitational lens whose source is modeled as five large merging spiral galaxies.

quotes.quote: We present the first high-resolution images of CSWA 31, a gravitational lens system observed as part of the SLUGS (Sloan Lenses Unravelled by Gemini Studies) program. These systems exhibit complex image structure with the potential to strongly constrain the mass distribution of the massive lens galaxies, as well as the complex morphology of the sources.

CSWA 31 is a complex lens with at least seven identifiable images and a very large image separation ( 12'')(Figure cswa31_full ). The lens is a galaxy group, with a dominant central galaxy acting as the primary lens. We obtained g,, and i-band images for CSWA31 on February 21, 2009, using the GMOS instrument on the Gemini South telescope.

We presented the first reconstruction of the complex SLUGS lens CSWA 31. We found that the observed lensing configuration is best explained by five source components, lensed into the elaborate image structure by the complex group lens. Although the source redshift is unknown, it is plausible that the physical sizes of the sources are 10 kpc and hence we are observing the merger of five large spiral galaxies.

sources.summary: The BGG is the ultra-massive early-type main lens galaxy in CSWA 31; the paper measures high velocity dispersion, models its light and mass, and finds it dominates projected mass within 20 kpc.

quotes.quote: The main lens galaxy of CSWA 31 is located in the group center and will be referred to as the brightest group galaxy (BGG). This is an early-type galaxy at z=0.683 with a very high stellar mass of about 3 10^ 12 M _ and an aperture-averaged stellar velocity dispersion of 450 80 km s ^ 1 from SDSS grillo13 , which suggests it is among the rarest ultra-massive elliptical galaxies at these redshifts with comoving number densities 10^ 8 Mpc ^ 3 , loeb03 . The BGG is surrounded by group members and by giant lensed arcs formed by a face-on spiral at z=1.487 .

The BGG does not show significant rotation, as also found for the majority of massive early-types in the local Universe e.g., emsellem07 , and varies between 380 and 500 km s ^ 1 . The V_ rms map shows higher values along the minor axis of the BGG (y' direction) and lower values along the major axis (x' direction) with significant scatter.

Finally, from the best-fit stellar kinematics, we obtain a luminosity-weighted average velocity dispersion of the BGG of = 430 29 km s ^ 1 , consistent with the value = 450 80 km s ^ 1 previously measured by grillo13 from lower S/N SDSS spectroscopy. The elevated confirms that the BGG is an ultra-massive early-type galaxy loeb03 .

gmw_00006361.name: Maffei 1
gmw_00006361.overview: Maffei 1 is a giant elliptical galaxy located close to the Galactic plane in the Zone of Avoidance [arXiv:1404.2987, arXiv:2403.06187]. It is situated in the Local Sheet at a distance of approximately 3.43 Mpc and hosts an old, metal-rich stellar population [arXiv:1404.2987, arXiv:2403.06187].

sources.summary: The paper describes Maffei 1 as a heavily extinguished giant elliptical with old metal-rich stars, estimates its reddening from M31-bulge templates, and derives a 3.43 Mpc TRGB distance.

quotes.quote: Maffei 1 Maffei 1 is a giant elliptical galaxy located close to the Galactic plane ( b = -0.^ 55 ). The severe extinction makes it appear dim and small at visual wavelength ( V = 11.14 ), but if the extinction were removed, Maffei 1 would be among the brightest galaxies in the sky fin03 ). The infrared-bright AGB stars in Maffei 1 are found to be similar to those in other nearby elliptical galaxies such as NGC 5128 and M32, as well as the bulge of M31 dav02 , which suggests that Maffei 1 has an old, metal-rich population. In Figure fig:p12877 we see that Maffei 1 lacks a populated upper main sequence, hence the approach applied on IC 342 for estimating the reddening is not applicable here. Instead, we estimate the reddening by comparing the F110W - F160W TRGB color of Maffei 1 to the bulge of M31, which we assume has a similar stellar content but with less reddening.
Given the assumed extinction, the distance modulus and distance towards Maffei 1 from the F110W TRGB are 27.68^ +0.20 _ 0.17 and 3.43^ +0.32 _ 0.23 Mpc, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper treats Maffei 1 as a distance-sensitive Zone-of-Avoidance giant elliptical whose K-band weight can dominate the recovered Council of Giants ring.

quotes.quote: As Neuzil2020 also points out, the distances to Maffei 1 and Maffei 2 are particularly sensitive since they are in the `Zone of Avoidance' of our galaxy and hence subject to redening.

While not explicitly shown here, we find that in both cases the Council of Giants ring (i.e. the lower panel of Fig. fig:map ) shifts to include both Maffei 1 and 2 (excluding IC342, M81/2, M94, and M64). This same ring -- which obviously has a much lower isotropy level -- is also found for the lower luminosity cuts (i.e. the upper panel of Fig. fig:map ) when using linear K-band weights, indicating that Maffei 1 clearly dominates the sample when taken its K-band luminosity (and hence stellar mass) linearly into account. Finally, using the logarithm of the K-band luminosity as a weight, the so-called satellite ring is not affected.

This is primarily driven by Maffei 1, a giant elliptical galaxy in the Zone of Avoidance whose distance is not the best established one: when basically forcing HINORA to include it by using linear K-band weights, other lower-mass galaxies are removed from the non-weighted ring. We believe that, given the special situation for Maffei 1 (and 2), the results shown here in Fig. fig:map should be the ones to interpret, which is also why we defer from showing the results for the different weighing schemes. Note that both Maffei 1 and 2 do lie in the Local Sheet, which is why HINORA picks them up as inliers in the rings found by it.

gmw_00006362.name: G1
aliases_external: Labbé et al. red massive candidate 35300
gmw_00006362.overview: G1 is a massive galaxy observed at a high redshift of approximately 9.1 [arXiv:2306.05364]. It has an estimated stellar mass of at least 2.5 billion solar masses [arXiv:2306.05364].

sources.summary: The paper treats G1 as a massive high-redshift JWST object at z 9.1 and uses its mass, redshift, and number density to constrain primordial-black-hole seed scenarios.

quotes.quote: Here, we consider two most massive high- z objects, Galaxies 35300 and 38094 (G1 and G2 for short hereafter), with their stellar masses M_ and redshifts z as M_ & 2.5 10^ 10 M_ , z 9.1 (G1) , M_ & 7.8 10^ 10 M_ , z 7.5 (G2) . On the one hand, from eq. ( Psdomz ), the seed effect is likely to dominate if f_ PBH (1+z)a_ eq . Taking into account the redshifts of G1 and G2, we approximately obtain the dominant region for the seed effect as f_ PBH 3 10^ 3 . This result is generally consistent with the constraints from the X-ray binaries , the infall of PBHs into the Galactic center by dynamical friction , and the large-scale structure statistics . On the other hand, due to the nonlinear dynamics around the PBH-seeded halo , the Lyman- forest constraint can be weakened on small scales, where each halo contains less than one PBH on average. Altogether, these factors necessitate further analysis on the seed effect. From eq. ( Psz ), the mass of the observed galaxies seeded by the PBHs growing in isolation is M_ halo M_ PBH (1+z)a_ eq . As the JWST data in ref. have been updated, we derive two idealized conditions following the methods in ref. . First, the cosmic comoving number density of PBHs is n _ PBH = 3H_0^2( _ m _ b ) 8 G f_ PBH M_ PBH 3.25 10^ 10 f_ PBH M_ PBH M_ Mpc ^ 3 , where H_0=67.36 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 is the Hubble constant . Obviously, n _ PBH must be larger than the comoving number densities n_ G of G1 and G2.

The blue region is only valid for G1, but the brown region can explain G1 and G2 together. If 0.4 , P _ 11 and P _ 12 cannot explain G1, P _ 13 and P _ 14 cannot explain G2, while P _ 15 - P _ 19 are valid for both G1 and G2. Furthermore, when 0.1 , which is much more reasonable, the inflation model can still explain the JWST observations. It should be noted that P _ 17 is beyond the blue and brown regions. However, it still has the potential to explain the JWST observations because the solid red line only corresponds to the maximum comoving number densities of G1 and G2 with the overestimated star formation efficiency =1 .

gmw_00006363.name: G2
aliases_external: Labbé et al. red massive candidate 38094
gmw_00006363.overview: G2 is a massive high-redshift galaxy observed at a redshift of approximately 7.5 [arXiv:2306.05364]. It has an estimated stellar mass of at least 7.8 billion solar masses [arXiv:2306.05364].

sources.summary: The paper treats G2 as a massive high-redshift JWST object at z 7.5 and uses its mass, redshift, and number density to test primordial-black-hole seed models.

quotes.quote: Here, we consider two most massive high- z objects, Galaxies 35300 and 38094 (G1 and G2 for short hereafter), with their stellar masses M_ and redshifts z as M_ & 2.5 10^ 10 M_ , z 9.1 (G1) , M_ & 7.8 10^ 10 M_ , z 7.5 (G2) . On the one hand, from eq. ( Psdomz ), the seed effect is likely to dominate if f_ PBH (1+z)a_ eq . Taking into account the redshifts of G1 and G2, we approximately obtain the dominant region for the seed effect as f_ PBH 3 10^ 3 . This result is generally consistent with the constraints from the X-ray binaries , the infall of PBHs into the Galactic center by dynamical friction , and the large-scale structure statistics . On the other hand, due to the nonlinear dynamics around the PBH-seeded halo , the Lyman- forest constraint can be weakened on small scales, where each halo contains less than one PBH on average. Altogether, these factors necessitate further analysis on the seed effect. From eq. ( Psz ), the mass of the observed galaxies seeded by the PBHs growing in isolation is M_ halo M_ PBH (1+z)a_ eq . As the JWST data in ref. have been updated, we derive two idealized conditions following the methods in ref. . First, the cosmic comoving number density of PBHs is n _ PBH = 3H_0^2( _ m _ b ) 8 G f_ PBH M_ PBH 3.25 10^ 10 f_ PBH M_ PBH M_ Mpc ^ 3 , where H_0=67.36 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 is the Hubble constant . Obviously, n _ PBH must be larger than the comoving number densities n_ G of G1 and G2.

The blue region is only valid for G1, but the brown region can explain G1 and G2 together. If 0.4 , P _ 11 and P _ 12 cannot explain G1, P _ 13 and P _ 14 cannot explain G2, while P _ 15 - P _ 19 are valid for both G1 and G2. Furthermore, when 0.1 , which is much more reasonable, the inflation model can still explain the JWST observations. It should be noted that P _ 17 is beyond the blue and brown regions. However, it still has the potential to explain the JWST observations because the solid red line only corresponds to the maximum comoving number densities of G1 and G2 with the overestimated star formation efficiency =1 .

gmw_00006364.name: UGC 6205
gmw_00006364.overview: UGC 6205 is a small spiral galaxy that hosts a rich population of H II regions [arXiv:1011.1013]. Observations of these regions reveal a steep oxygen abundance gradient of approximately 0.3 dex/kpc across its galactocentric radius [arXiv:1011.1013].

sources.summary: The paper finds UGC 6205 has the sample's richest H II-region set and a very steep oxygen abundance gradient, robust across abundance methods and line-ratio checks.

quotes.quote: UGC 6205 is the galaxy with the largest number of H ii regions detected in our sample: a total of 11 regions were studied in this investigation. The gradient determined with the semi empirical methods are shown in the first column of Table 1. Also, the values determined using the most and less metallic regions, as well as the most internal and external ones are presented in rows 6 and 7, respectively. Finally, the slope from the averaged distances is presented in row 8 (See below). If the three latter values of the gradients are similar to those obtained with the least-squared fitting might indicate the robustness of the determination. This is the situation for UGC 6205, with all the values of the gradients ranging from 0.2 to 0.4 dex/kpc. An average value of 0.31 dex/kpc with a dispersion of 0.03 will be considered. Another remarkable results presented in Table 1 is the steepness of the gradient, being larger than the values obtained for most of the galaxies studied so far (e.g. see Table 4 in Vila-Costas Edmunds 1992). Why? One might think that this galaxy shows very extreme abundance values, but the most external region, located at 3.1 kpc, has a metallicity of 7.7 dex while the most internal regions have metallicities of 8.7 dex. The difference is only of 1 dex, which is not so large. There are other spiral galaxies with such difference in their abundances, or larger, as M81 (Garnett Shields 1987). The point here is that M81 is very much larger than UGC 6205. So, UGC 6205 has the same difference in the abundance than other galaxies but the galactocentric distances of the H ii are smaller. Therefore, so large gradient. The next question to be addressed is which is the region responsible for such a steep gradient. One might think that those regions with low S/N are responsible for the slope because their abundance value might not be so reliable. This is not true for several reasons: firstly, the gradients obtained with the N2 and N3 methods, which included only those high S/N regions, give only slightly shallower gradients. Moreover, the values determined without the least-squared fitting are of the same order except when the gradient is determined from the most and less metallic regions which is of 1.1 dex/kpc, and it will be ignored because of its doubtful meaning. Another way to check the reliability of the steep gradient, is, as previously said, using an average abundance for different ranges of galactocentric distances: 0 0.6 kpc, 0.6 1.2 kpc, 1.2 1.8 kpc, 1.8 2.4 kpc, 2.4 3.0 kpc, and 3.0 3.600 kpc. As said, the variations of the abundance are smoothed and the low S/N regions will not be the responsible of the value of the gradient. These are shown in the eighth row of Table 1 and it is about 0.3 dex/kpc for this galaxy. Whatever the method is used for the gradient determination, will end up in a very high value. Then, it can be concluded that such value might be real. Figure fig1 shows the metallicity vs. the galactocentric radius, in kpc, for each of the five metallicity determinations. The errorbars in the metallicity and in the galactocentric distances are shown but the latter are smaller than the symbol. The solid line is the fitting using all the H ii regions. The fitting is, in general, very good with all the methods except for regions U05a1, U05c1, and U05b5 which have odd abundance values for their galactocentric distances. If those regions are not considered, the gradients do not change significantly. It can be noticed in figure fig1 that the gradient seems to change along the radius of the galaxy, with a plateau for radii up to 1.6 kpc, whatever method is used for the abundance determination. This is motivated mainly because the high metallicity of regions U05c1 and U05a1, which as stood in paper I, they might be something more than simply H ii regions. In any case, an internal ( < 1.4 kpc) and external gradients were determined without these three anomalous regions, and no differences were found. It can be concluded that the gradient in this small spiral galaxy is very steep, of about 0.3 dex/kpc, with no dependence on the metallicity method and no differences along the galactocentric distance.

The other gradient that can be determined is the [ NII ] /H ratio. The only galaxy for which is it meaningful is UGC 6205. The nitrogen gradient is also very steep in UGC 6205, of 0.44 dex/kpc. From both line ratios, it can be concluded that the oxygen gradients determined are real, especially for UGC 6205 for which both ratios give very steep gradients.

gmw_00006365.name: UGC 6377
gmw_00006365.overview: UGC 6377 is a spiral galaxy that contains several H II regions [arXiv:1011.1013]. The galaxy exhibits a steep oxygen abundance gradient determined from seven of these regions, excluding two anomalous candidates that may be planetary nebulae [arXiv:1011.1013].

sources.summary: The paper derives UGC 6377's oxygen abundance gradient from seven H II regions, excluding two anomalous regions, and concludes the gradient remains steep despite sampling and S/N caveats.

quotes.quote: UGC 6377 As mentioned in paper I, two of the nine H ii regions in this galaxy have abnormal abundances and they might be planetary nebulae instead of normal H ii regions. Therefore, they are not going to be considered in the determination of the gradients. We will use the other seven regions in order to obtain the gradient.

It can be seen that the R_ 23 , the P method and the average abundance gradients are very similar, from 0.15 to 0.20 dex/kpc. Also, those values are very similar to the slopes determined from the most external/internal regions, with values ranging from 0.16 to 0.13 dex/kpc. The slopes determined from the most/less metallic regions are larger, of the order of 0.3 dex/kpc. The main reason is that the less metallic region, U77c3, is not the most external one. The abundance differences in UGC 6377 are lower than for UGC 6205, only 0.8 dex, but the distances considered are also smaller, only 2.4 kpc. Therefore, the gradients are also quite large.

gmw_00006366.name: UGC 5296
gmw_00006366.overview: UGC 5296 is classified as a tiny dwarf spiral galaxy [arXiv:1011.1013]. The galaxy contains only five known H II regions, which has made confirming its large metallicity gradient difficult [arXiv:1011.1013].

sources.summary: The paper treats UGC 5296 as a tiny dwarf spiral with only four usable H II regions, adopting a large abundance gradient that needs better data for confirmation.

quotes.quote: UGC 5296 There are only four H ii regions for gradient determination in this tiny galaxy. One might think that no gradient can be determined with such a few number of data-points. But there are at least another three galaxies with values determined with only four metallicity determination. Nevertheless, the confidence of the gradient obtained is not as good as for the two galaxies previously studied.

The value of the gradient adopted for this galaxy is 0.4 dex/kpc, as stated in Table 1. The dispersion is about 0.1 . Such gradient is large but similar to the one of UGC 6205. From the results of the averaged distances determination discussed above, it can be concluded there is a real, and large, change in the abundances of this galaxy. Such differences cannot be explained by differences in the SFR (R eyes-P erez 2009). Finally, it is important to point out that a more accurate determination of the gradient will be very difficult, because there is only one more H ii region in the galaxy. In any case, more high quality data are needed in order to confirm these results.

gmw_00006367.name: UGC 5242
gmw_00006367.overview: UGC 5242 is classified as a barred dwarf spiral galaxy [arXiv:1011.1013]. It contains a small number of H II regions, which exhibit a shallow oxygen abundance gradient [arXiv:1011.1013].

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC 5242 as the sample's only barred dwarf spiral and finds a shallow oxygen gradient relative to the non-barred galaxies.

quotes.quote: UGC 5242 The number of H ii regions is also small in this barred galaxy, but for the same reasons as for UGC 5296, a gradient will be determined. Moreover, as being the only barred galaxy in our sample, it will be very interesting to know if the gradient is similar to the other barred late-type galaxies. The oxygen abundances can be determined for only five regions. Also, as for UGC 5296, there are two set of data for each region. Therefore, a total of eight different estimations of the gradient can be obtained for this galaxy.
In this case, the values of the gradient vary from 0.006 dex/kpc to 0.18 dex/kpc (see Table 1). These values a re slightly larger than those determined for other barred late-type galaxies.

gmw_00006368.overview: 00141 is a lensed, massive passive galaxy located at a high redshift of approximately z~2 [arXiv:1803.01852]. Under standard Padova isochrone models, its spectral energy distribution indicates a sub-solar metallicity, though this estimate varies depending on the chosen isochrone model [arXiv:1803.01852].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 00141 as a lensed massive passive z~2 galaxy whose SED prefers sub-solar metallicity under Padova models, with isochrone choice a key systematic.

quotes.quote: Both galaxies prefer sub-solar metallicities under the standard assumption of Padova isochrones: =-0.40_ 0.02 ^ +0.02 and 0.49_ 0.03 ^ +0.04 for 00141 and 00227, respectively, with estimates and uncertainties reflecting the 16 ^ th /50 ^ th /84 ^ th percentile values. While it is known that metallicities are effectively differentiated in the rest-frame NIR e.g., lee07 , our K_S /IRAC data do not provide the tightest constraints.

We tested all isochrones available in FSPS, and found that, while 00227's result is robust, Z_ * changes significantly for 00141, reaching a low of -0.6 PARSEC; bressan12 and a high of 0.10 0.07 MIST; choi16 . This is mostly caused by the different treatments of the asymptotic giant branch, which contributes significantly to the galaxy's luminosity at ages near 00141's of 500 Myr. Since we cannot determine the best isochrone with the current data set, we base our discussion on the result with the standard Padova isochrone to facilitate contextualizing our results with those in the literature.

gmw_00006369.overview: 00227 is a gravitationally lensed, massive passive galaxy at a redshift of approximately 2 [arXiv:1803.01852]. Its stellar population is dominated by an A-type spectrum with sub-solar metallicity, showing very weak Balmer infilling and emission that suggests a recent quenching [arXiv:1803.01852].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 00227 as a lensed massive passive z~2 galaxy with robustly low stellar metallicity and an A-type dominated spectrum.

quotes.quote: Both galaxies prefer sub-solar metallicities under the standard assumption of Padova isochrones: =-0.40_ 0.02 ^ +0.02 and 0.49_ 0.03 ^ +0.04 for 00141 and 00227, respectively, with estimates and uncertainties reflecting the 16 ^ th /50 ^ th /84 ^ th percentile values. While it is known that metallicities are effectively differentiated in the rest-frame NIR e.g., lee07 , our K_S /IRAC data do not provide the tightest constraints.

Also, both best-fit templates are dominated by 0.5 Gyr old stellar populations: 99 of 00141's light is in the a_2 template, while 00227's stellar population is younger, with 44 in a_1 and a_2 . Stellar masses are M_*=1.8 10^ 11 and 5.8 10^ 10 , . 00141 exhibits no significant emission, while 00227 shows very weak Balmer infilling (EWs of H 0.8 0.2 \ and H 1.8 0.2 ) and \ emission ( 3.4 0.7 ), suggesting the latter was indeed quenched more recently.

gmw_00006370.name: ESO 184-G063
gmw_00006370.overview: ESO 184-G063 is a small Sb-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:0808.3878]. It features a strong, asymmetric integral-sign optical warp, though its major-axis and off-axis rotation curves remain consistent through this warped region [arXiv:0808.3878].

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO 184-G063 as a small warped Sb galaxy whose major-axis and off-axis rotation curves remain consistent through the optical warp.

quotes.quote: ESO 184-G063 (Fig. fig_eso184 ) is a small ( M=-18.5 ) Sb-type galaxy at cz = 3207 km/s, and has a strong integral-sign warp, which is distinctly stronger on one side than the other.
Our off-axis spectrum extends out to 1.6 R _ 25 , clearly intersecting the tip of the optical warp.
Both the major axis and the offset rotation curves are consistent, and there is no sign of a sudden break in the rotational velocity coincident with the onset of the warp.

gmw_00006371.name: ESO 340-G026
gmw_00006371.overview: ESO 340-G026 is classified as an Sc-type spiral galaxy with low-surface-brightness tails at the edge of its disk [arXiv:0808.3878]. These tails show no warp, and the galaxy's rotation curve displays no signs of kinematic anomalies [arXiv:0808.3878].

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO 340-G026 as an Sc galaxy with low-surface-brightness tails but no significant warp or rotation-curve anomaly in the available spectra.

quotes.quote: ESO 340-G026 (Fig. fig_eso340 ) is an Sc-type galaxy at cz=5483 km/s with M=-19.5 .
There are low-surface-brightness tails at the edge of the disk, but they show no significant warp.
The rotation curve, again, shows no signs of kinematic anomalies.

gmw_00006372.name: ESO 473-G025
gmw_00006372.overview: ESO 473-G025 is an Sb-type spiral galaxy with an absolute magnitude of -20.3 [arXiv:0808.3878]. Acquisition images of the galaxy show at most a very subtle warp, and there are no signs of kinematic anomalies toward the edge of its disk [arXiv:0808.3878].

sources.summary: The paper treats ESO 473-G025 as the brightest sample galaxy, with at most a subtle warp, no edge kinematic anomaly, and a possible small satellite emission region.

quotes.quote: ESO 473-G025 (Fig. fig_eso473 ), the brightest object in our sample ( M=-20.3 ), is an Sb-type galaxy at cz=7110 km/s.
Acquisition images show what is at most a very subtle warp.
However, there are no signs of kinematic anomalies towards the edge of the disk.

gmw_00006373.name: MCG -01-10-035
gmw_00006373.overview: MCG -01-10-035 is a small Sc-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:0808.3878]. It features a very extended, low-surface-brightness tail that curves back, as well as a sudden drop in rotational velocity near the onset of this tidal feature [arXiv:0808.3878].

sources.summary: The paper describes MCG -01-10-035 as a small Sc galaxy with a low-surface-brightness tail, bent outer isophotes, and anomalous low-velocity gas near the tidal feature.

quotes.quote: MCG -01-10-035 (Fig. fig_mcg101 ) is a small ( M=-18.3 ) Sc-type galaxy at cz = 3788 km/s.
Our acquisition images reveal a very extended tail of low surface brightness extending as far as 2R_ 25 and then curving back.
Remarkably, at the onset of this feature, around R _ 25 , we see a sudden break in the rotation curve along the major axis in the sense of a jump to lower rotational velocities by 60-70 km/s.

gmw_00006374.name: UGC 12423
gmw_00006374.overview: UGC 12423 is an Sc-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:0808.3878]. It exhibits a strong and asymmetrical optical warp, though its rotation curves show no signs of kinematic anomalies [arXiv:0808.3878].

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC 12423 as an Sc galaxy with a strong asymmetric optical warp but no significant kinematic anomaly in its measured rotation curves.

quotes.quote: UGC 12423 (Fig. fig_ugc12423 ) is an Sc-type galaxy at cz = 4839 km/s with an absolute magnitude M=-19.4 .
It shows a strong and asymmetrical optical warp.
There are no signs for any kinematic anomalies in this rotation curve.

gmw_00006375.name: UGCA 23
gmw_00006375.overview: UGCA 23 is an Sd-type spiral galaxy with an absolute magnitude of -19 [arXiv:0808.3878]. It exhibits lumpy emission, slightly bent isophotes on one side, and a warp feature that displays disk-like kinematics [arXiv:0808.3878].

sources.summary: The paper presents UGCA 23 as an Sd galaxy with lumpy emission and bent isophotes whose offset spectrum samples disk-like kinematics in the warp feature.

quotes.quote: UGCA 23 (Fig. fig_ugca23 ) is an M=-19 Sd-type galaxy at cz=3864 km/s.
Acquisition images show lumpy emission and slightly bent isophotes on one side.
The two rotation curves are indistinguishable; the kinematics of the extraplanar feature are disk-like.

gmw_00006376.overview: 001424+004145 is a gravitationally lensed quiescent galaxy at a redshift of z=1.374 [arXiv:2212.04731]. It features red arcs, lacks emission lines, and is characterized by an old stellar population with low iron and magnesium abundances [arXiv:2212.04731].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 001424+004145 as a z=1.374 lensed quiescent galaxy with red arcs, no emission lines, old stellar age, and low Fe and Mg abundances.

quotes.quote: The two gravitationally lensed galaxies, 132304+034319 (hereafter ) at z=1.016 and 001424+004145 ( ) at z=1.374 , were identified as part of the \ survey Tran22 . The \ survey performed spectroscopic observations on strong gravitational lenses selected from the Dark Energy Survey and the Dark Energy Camera Legacy Survey to measure their redshifts. These lensed galaxy candidates were discovered using convolutional neural networks Jacobs19a, Jacobs19b and were notable for their red arcs. \ was also independently discovered by the COOL-LAMPS Surveys as COOL J1323+0343 Sukay22 in a visual search of the northern galactic cap portion of the southern DECaLS dataset. Neither galaxy displayed emission lines in the identification spectra from \ survey, indicating their quiescent nature.

We measured the age, [ Fe/H ] , and [ Mg/Fe ] of the two lensed galaxies using deep, rest-frame-optical spectra (S/N 40 ^ 1 ) obtained on the Keck I telescope. The ages of \ and \ are 5.6^ +0.8 _ 0.8 Gyr and 3.1^ +0.8 _ 0.3 Gyr, respectively, indicating that most of the stars in the galaxies were formed less than 2 Gyr after the Big Bang. Compared to nearby quiescent galaxies of similar masses, the lensed galaxies have lower [ Fe/H ] and [ Mg/H ] . Surprisingly, the two galaxies have comparable [ Mg/Fe ] to similar-mass galaxies at lower redshifts, despite their old ages.

gmw_00006377.name: SDSS J0831+3213
gmw_00006377.overview: SDSS J0831+3213 is a quasar located at a redshift of 1.797 [arXiv:2307.07554]. Although archival observations caught the active nucleus in an X-ray weak state, deeper monitoring revealed it recovered to nominal emission levels, indicating strong X-ray variability likely caused by temporary absorption [arXiv:2307.07554].

sources.summary: The paper presents deeper XMM-Newton observations of SDSS J0831+3213, finding it recovered from an archival X-ray weak state and likely was temporarily absorbed rather than intrinsically weak.

quotes.quote: In this study, we present additional deeper XMM-Newton observations of two examples of the unclassified type of X-ray weak quasars in 2020ApJ...900..141P , SDSS J083116.62+321329.6 and SDSS J142339.87+042041.1 (hereafter SDSS J0831+3213 and SDSS J1423+0420). At a redshift of 1.797, SDSS J0831+3213 was detected serendipitously by a Chandra observation on 2007 December 22. It has 17.4^ +5.9 _ 4.8 counts in the 0.5--7 keV band with an effective power-law photon index ( _ eff ) of 1.0^ +0.6 _ 0.5 2020ApJ...900..141P .

Using the _ OX - L_ 2500 relation in 2006AJ....131.2826S , these quasars have _ OX values of 0.34^ +0.05 _ 0.05 and 0.48^ +0.08 _ 0.10 , corresponding to f_ weak values of 7.7^ +2.9 _ 1.9 and 17.4^ +14.2 _ 6.8 , respectively. In the deeper XMM-Newton observations, both quasars recovered to nominal levels of X-ray emission, and thus they displayed strong X-ray variability compared to the archival Chandra data. Optical spectra simultaneous to the XMM-Newton observations are also available. The results support the notion that these rare X-ray weak quasars were not peculiar objects having weak/suppressed coronal X-ray emission; instead, they were simply caught in unusual X-ray absorbed states.

gmw_00006378.name: SDSS J1423+0420
gmw_00006378.overview: SDSS J1423+0420 is an active galactic nucleus, specifically classified as a quasar, located at a redshift of 1.702 [arXiv:2307.07554]. Archival observations caught the quasar in a temporary, heavily absorbed X-ray weak state, from which it has since recovered to nominal emission levels [arXiv:2307.07554].

sources.summary: The paper presents deeper XMM-Newton observations of SDSS J1423+0420, finding it recovered from an archival X-ray weak state and likely was temporarily absorbed rather than intrinsically weak.

quotes.quote: In this study, we present additional deeper XMM-Newton observations of two examples of the unclassified type of X-ray weak quasars in 2020ApJ...900..141P , SDSS J083116.62+321329.6 and SDSS J142339.87+042041.1 (hereafter SDSS J0831+3213 and SDSS J1423+0420). At a redshift of 1.797, SDSS J0831+3213 was detected serendipitously by a Chandra observation on 2007 December 22. It has 17.4^ +5.9 _ 4.8 counts in the 0.5--7 keV band with an effective power-law photon index ( _ eff ) of 1.0^ +0.6 _ 0.5 2020ApJ...900..141P . SDSS J1423+0420 is at z=1.702 . It was detected by a Chandra observation on 2012 December 15, with 6.0^ +3.9 _ 2.7 counts in the 0.5--7 keV band and _ eff <1.3 2020ApJ...900..141P .

Using the _ OX - L_ 2500 relation in 2006AJ....131.2826S , these quasars have _ OX values of 0.34^ +0.05 _ 0.05 and 0.48^ +0.08 _ 0.10 , corresponding to f_ weak values of 7.7^ +2.9 _ 1.9 and 17.4^ +14.2 _ 6.8 , respectively. In the deeper XMM-Newton observations, both quasars recovered to nominal levels of X-ray emission, and thus they displayed strong X-ray variability compared to the archival Chandra data. Optical spectra simultaneous to the XMM-Newton observations are also available. The results support the notion that these rare X-ray weak quasars were not peculiar objects having weak/suppressed coronal X-ray emission; instead, they were simply caught in unusual X-ray absorbed states.

gmw_00006379.name: SDSS J132421.40+041918.5
gmw_00006379.overview: SDSS J132421.40+041918.5 is the second-brightest galaxy in its cluster [arXiv:2104.04548]. It is a possible brightest cluster galaxy of an infalling subcluster that is linked to a trail of cool, low-entropy gas [arXiv:2104.04548].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J132421.40+041918.5 as the second-brightest cluster galaxy and possible infalling-subcluster BCG linked to a cool, stripped gas spur.

quotes.quote: This galaxy, SDSS J132421.40+041918.5, is located 28 /114 kpc to the east of the BCG, and has a redshift z =0.26058, 740 \ offset from the BCG. Its SDSS i band Petrosian magnitude is i =17.539, compared to i =16.676 for the BCG. Small offsets between BCG and X-ray centroids are quite common in galaxy clusters see, e.g., Sandersonetal09,Hudsonetal10,Pasinietal19,Pasinietal21 and can be caused by a number of factors, including dynamical disturbances and the disruption of the core by AGN jets.
The southern tail and eastern spur of cool, low entropy gas may be the remnant of the ICM of the infalling subcluster, with SDSS J132421.40+041918.5 perhaps being its BCG.

We find that \ has probably undergone a minor merger in the recent past, which has left a trail of cool, low entropy gas extending from the south, past the cool core and BCG, and pointing to the second brightest galaxy in the cluster, SDSS J132421.40+041918.5.

gmw_00006380.name: RCS2327 - 1412
gmw_00006380.overview: RCS2327 - 1412 is a high-redshift galaxy candidate located at a photometric redshift of z=6.8 [arXiv:1503.02670]. The galaxy is positioned inside the critical curve of its lensing cluster, though its predicted counter-images remain undetected in Hubble Space Telescope data [arXiv:1503.02670].

sources.summary: The paper identifies RCS2327 - 1412 as a z=6.8 dropout candidate inside the critical curve, with uncertain predicted counter-images and favored high-redshift solutions after IRAC limits.

quotes.quote: RCS2327 - 1412 is located inside the critical curve at its photometric redshift, z=6.8 . We check for an image on the opposite side of the critical curve at the expected location predicted by the model, but we do not detect it in the HST data. Because we lack strong lensing constraints near the location of RCS2327 - 1412, our model is more uncertain in this area. Therefore, the model may not be able to accurately predict counter-images at this position.
In particular for candidates RCS2327 - 1914 and RCS2327 - 1412, for which we obtained IRAC limits only, high redshift solutions are much more favored once the IRAC data are included.

gmw_00006381.name: RCS2327-1282
gmw_00006381.overview: RCS2327-1282 is a high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy candidate [arXiv:1503.02670]. It is the brightest candidate in its sample, though it is only ambiguously resolved and its colors leave open the possibility of T-dwarf contamination [arXiv:1503.02670].

sources.summary: The paper treats RCS2327-1282 as the brightest dropout candidate, resolved only ambiguously; its IRAC detections and colors leave a possible T-dwarf contamination unresolved.

quotes.quote: All of the candidates are clearly resolved except RCS2327-1282, the brightest object in our sample. We attempt to fit its photometry to M, L and T dwarf spectra using the SpeX prism libraries http://pono.ucsd.edu/ adam/browndwarfs/spexprism/ . We are unable to find a good fit to any of the dwarf SEDs.

The SpeX libraries only extend out to the near-IR, but RCS2327-1282 is detected at >10 in both IRAC [ 3.6 ] and [ 4.5 ] . At these wavelengths, we rely on a sample of M, L and T dwarfs studied by patten06 for the comparison. We compare the F125W [ 3.6 ] and [ 3.6 ] [ 4.5 ] colors of RCS2327-1282 with the colors of the dwarfs, finding consistency with their T dwarfs only. Therefore, we cannot rule out the possibility that RCS2327-1282 is a T dwarf contaminant.

gmw_00006382.overview: 0501 is a bright, doubly lensed quasar located at a redshift of z=2.47 [arXiv:2304.02499]. The system's two lensed images are separated by 2.7 arcseconds and exhibit microlensing variability, with archival optical light curves showing a variability time delay of approximately 100 days [arXiv:2304.02499].

sources.summary: The paper reports 0501 as a newly discovered bright doubly lensed quasar at z=2.47, confirmed spectroscopically, with resolved lensed images, microlensing variability, and time-delay cosmography potential.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery and spectroscopic identification of the bright doubly lensed quasar 0501 at redshift z=2.47 , selected from the first all-sky survey of the Spectrum Roentgen Gamma (SRG) eROSITA telescope and the Gaia EDR3 catalog. We systematically searched for extragalactic sources with eROSITA X-ray positions that have multiple Gaia counterparts, and we have started spectroscopic follow-up of the most promising candidates using long-slit spectroscopy with NTT/EFOSC2 to confirm the lens nature. The two images are separated by 2.7 , and their average Gaia g band magnitudes are 16.95 and 17.33. Legacy Survey DR10 imaging and image modeling reveal both the lensing galaxy and tentatively the lensed image of the quasar host galaxy. Archival optical light curves show evidence of a variability time delay, with the fainter component lagging the brighter by about 100 days.

0501 is highly affected by microlensing, with an overall variation of 1.25 magnitudes over 8 years of archival data. The reported time delay and the microlensing variability make 0501 a suitable candidate on which to perform time-delay cosmography and study a likely caustic-crossing event, respectively.

gmw_00006383.name: JWST-ER1g
gmw_00006383.overview: JWST-ER1g is a compact, massive early-type elliptical galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 2.02 [arXiv:2309.07969, arXiv:2309.15986]. It is a quiescent galaxy with a low star formation rate [arXiv:2309.07969, arXiv:2309.15986] and acts as a strong gravitational lens [arXiv:2402.15840, arXiv:2309.15986].

sources.summary: The paper models JWST-ER1g as a compact early-type lens galaxy with a dense dark halo, showing adiabatic contraction can explain its mass while constraining self-interacting dark matter.

quotes.quote: The object consists of a complete Einstein ring with a radius of 6.6 kpc and a compact early-type galaxy (JWST-ER1g) with an effective radius of 1.9 kpc . Within the ring, the total mass is 6.5 10^ 11 M_ , while the stellar mass mass is 1.1 10^ 11 M_ assuming a Chabrier initial mass function 2003PASP..115..763C . The mass gap is large, indicating that JWST-ER1g has a dense halo. However, for a typical Navarro-Frenk-White (NFW) halo Navarro_1997 , the dark matter density is not high enough to fully account for the mass gap, unless the halo mass is 10^ 14 M_ 2023NatAs.tmp....7V , which would be rare at z 2 , significantly higher than expected in the stellar mass-halo mass relation 2013ApJ...770...57B . also analyzed the JWST-ER1 system and found the total mass is 3.7 10^ 11 M_ within the Einstein ring, a factor of 2 smaller than that in , and a 10^ 13 M_ halo is favored for JWST-ER1g.

In this section, we study the interpretation of JWST-ER1 in the SIDM scenario Tulin_2018,Adhikari:2022sbh . Dark matter self-interactions could thermalize the inner halo and produce a shallow density core, which seemed contradictory to the observations of JWST-ER1. However, in the presence of baryons, SIDM thermalization actually leads to a small core and a high inner halo density PhysRevLett.113.021302,Kamada:2016euw,Creasey:2017qxc,Elbert:2016dbb,Robertson:2017mgj,Robertson:2018anx,Robertson:2020pxj,Sameie:2018chj,Despali:2018zpw,Santos-Santos:2019vrw,Feng:2020kxv,Sameie:2021ang,Zhong:2023yzk . To model the SIDM density profile for JWST-ER1g, we follow a semi-analytical method in , which has been tested extensively using hydrodynamical SIDM simulations, see, e.g., .

sources.summary: JWST-ER1g is a compact early-type lensing galaxy in JWST-ER1, with quiescent colors, an old low-SFR stellar population, high stellar mass, and small effective radius.

quotes.quote: The object, dubbed JWST-ER1, is shown in Fig. \ mainfig.fig a. It consists of a compact early-type galaxy (JWST-ER1g) and a complete Einstein ring (JWST-ER1r) with two conspicuous red concentrations.

The lensing galaxy appears to be a textbook example of the class of massive quiescent galaxies at z 2 . Its rest-frame colors, U-V 2.10 and V-J 1.3 , place it comfortably in the quiescent region of the z 2 UVJ diagram. The best fit stellar population parameters from the Prospector fit imply an age of 1.9^ +0.3 _ 0.6 Gyr and a low star formation rate of 4^ +19 _ 3 yr ^ 1 . The Prospector total stellar mass of JWST-ER1g is 1.3^ +0.3 _ 0.4 10^ 11 \ for a Chabrier IMF, and its observed effective radius corresponds to r_ e =1.9 0.2 kpc. This makes the galaxy quite compact, just like other quiescent galaxies at these redshifts, and it falls on of the canonical size-mass relation of quiescent galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper characterizes the lens as a compact, quiescent massive elliptical at z about 2 whose stellar and dark matter masses explain the Einstein-ring mass budget.

quotes.quote: We extract the visible and near-infrared photometry of the source and the lens from more than 25 bands. We combine these observations with far-infrared detections to study the dusty nature of the source and we derive the photometric redshifts and physical properties of both the lens and the source with three different SED fitting codes. Using / images, we also produce two lens models to (i) recover the total mass of the lens, (ii) derive the magnification of the system, (iii) reconstruct the morphology of the lensed source, and (iv) measure the slope of the total mass density profile of the lens.

We find the lens to be a very massive elliptical galaxy at z = 2.02 0.02 with a total mass within the Einstein radius of M_ tot (< _ Ein ) = 3.66(0.36)e11 and a total stellar mass of M_ = 1.37^ +0.14 _ 0.11 e11 . We also estimate it to be compact and quiescent with a sSFR e-13 e-15 . Compared to stellar-to-halo mass relations (SHMRs) from the literature, we find that the total mass of the lens within the Einstein radius is consistent with the presence of a dark matter (DM) halo of total mass M_ h = 1.09^ +1.46 _ 0.57 e13 .

gmw_00006384.name: NGC 3706
gmw_00006384.overview: NGC 3706 is an early-type elliptical galaxy [arXiv:2108.03247, arXiv:astro-ph/9601044] that hosts a central supermassive black hole [arXiv:2108.03247]. The galaxy features an edge-on stellar ring with a retrograde component [arXiv:2108.03247], and its kinematics indicate the presence of a dark matter halo [arXiv:astro-ph/9601044].

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 3706 as an early-type galaxy with a central surface-brightness minimum, black hole, and stellar ring whose kinematics may be consistent with an eccentric nuclear disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 3706 is an early-type galaxy with a central surface brightness minimum arising from an apparent edge-on stellar ring. Fitting imaging and spectroscopic data to axisymmetric orbit models, Gultekin2014 uncover a central black hole of mass M = (6.0 ^ +0.7 _ 0.9 ) 10^8 M_ . They find however that the stellar ring is inconsistent with a population of co-rotating stars on circular orbits which would produce a narrow line-of-sight velocity distribution (as in the solid black lines of Figure fig:onepix ). Instead the data indicate small line-of-sight values of |v|/ 0.1 0.4 . They conclude that the stellar ring contains a retrograde (counter-rotating) component and is not consistent with co-rotating circular orbits.

As a consistency check, we determine that the central minimum feature in NGC 3706 lies within the radius of influence of the central black hole. We use the mass of the supermassive black holes, estimated using the M- relation Fer00,Geb00 , to determine the radius of influence Pee72 . In NGC 3706, the central minimum feature is within the calculated radius of influence.

sources.summary: The paper applies the same dynamical models to NGC 3706, showing its kinematic data require a dark halo but do not uniquely require the halo to be flattened.

quotes.quote: The distribution functions are used to model the galaxies NGC 2434 (E1) and NGC 3706 (E4), for which stellar kinematical measurements out to two effective radii indicate the presence of dark halos (Carollo et al.). The velocity profile shapes of both galaxies can be well fit by radially anisotropic case II models with a spherical logarithmic potential. This contrasts with the f(E,L_z) models studied previously, which require flattened dark halos to fit the data. galaxies: elliptical and lenticular, cD -- galaxies: individual: NGC 2434 and NGC 3706 -- galaxies: kinematics and dynamics -- galaxies: structure -- line: profiles.

The families of DFs presented here can be used to further interpret the observations for NGC 2434 and NGC 3706. The model parameter is chosen to fit the observed surface brightness slope at large radii, and q_ p is chosen equal to the average apparent flattening of the isophotes outside half the effective radius. This yields = 2.94 and q_ p =0.92 for NGC 2434, and = 3.36 and q_ p =0.65 for NGC 3706. The potential is chosen to be logarithmic ( = 0 ), since it has already been demonstrated that both galaxies must be embedded in a dark halo.

gmw_00006385.name: ESO 55--2
gmw_00006385.overview: ESO 55--2 is a well-resolved Seyfert 2 active galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0510507]. It features an extended core and a broad emission line, which is likely redshifted [O II] [arXiv:astro-ph/0510507].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ESO 55--2 as a resolved Seyfert 2 galaxy in the sample, noting its extended core and a detected broad emission line likely to be redshifted [O II].

quotes.quote: ESO 55--2 may be a good candidate for follow-up projects to probe the extended absorption properties of the Bridge ISM given that its core extends to 1.8 arcseconds with a brightness of 1/10th of the peak.

ESO 55--2 is a Seyfert 2 galaxy catalogued by Arp Madore (1987). It is one of only two objects in the current sample that are well-resolved by the current observations. In addition, ESO 55--2 is the only object for which we detect a clear emission line shown in Fig. esoission . This is a broad feature and lies at a (uncorrected) wavelength of 3907.91 0.06 with a FWHM width of 5.65 0.14 (equivalent to 410 km s ^ 1 in the restframe), and with a line flux of 4.7 0.2 .

gmw_00006386.name: Holmberg II
gmw_00006386.overview: Holmberg II is an inclined dwarf galaxy with a unique interstellar medium morphology [arXiv:1910.04167]. Its extended disk contains spiral neutral hydrogen structures with large holes, numerous high surface brightness H-alpha bubbles, and diffuse H-alpha emission [arXiv:1910.04167].

sources.summary: The paper describes Holmberg II as an inclined dwarf with spiral H I structures, large holes, widespread H-alpha bubbles, diffuse emission, and gas energetic enough to vent from the disk.

quotes.quote: Holmberg II From Figure fig:hoii , the galaxy is inclined towards us with a unique ISM morphology including spiral structures with significant holes, visible in the extended \ disk (seen in blue). The holes in the \ are much larger than those found in more massive spiral galaxies, which has been attributed to the larger \ scale height of 625 pc, rather than the more usual 120 pc Puche1992 . The larger scale height and implied lower gas density allows greater expansion of the hot ISM with less mass loading. There are numerous, high surface brightness \ bubbles and structures throughout the galaxy with a large superbubble feature outlined in the southern half. Holmberg II also has significant diffuse \ emission throughout the disk that fills many of the \ holes.

gmw_00006387.name: J1118 + 7913
gmw_00006387.overview: J1118 + 7913 is a resolved dwarf galaxy located near UGC 6456 [arXiv:1910.04167]. It exhibits a disturbed morphology with multiple stellar shells outside its main disk, as well as emission lines that indicate ordered rotation [arXiv:1910.04167].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1118 + 7913 as a previously uncatalogued resolved galaxy near UGC 6456, with disturbed morphology, possible stellar shells, emission lines, and ordered rotation.

quotes.quote: We have identified a previously uncatalogued resolved galaxy, J1118 + 7913, located 30 \ from UGC 6456 in projection. The galaxy is identified in the GALEX all sky imaging survey, but not catalogued. In Figure fig:newgal1 we present a 3-color image (B-band in blue, R-band in green, and \ narrow band in red) and an \ continuum subtracted image of the galaxy. The newly identified galaxy has a disturbed morphology including what appears to be multiple stellar shells outside the main disk visible even in integrated light.
Emission lines from [ NII ] , and [ SII ] were detected in nine of the fibers with relative velocity shifts indicative of ordered rotation.

gmw_00006388.name: LBQS 2113-4538
gmw_00006388.overview: LBQS 2113-4538 is a class A quasar located at a redshift of z = 0.956 [arXiv:1308.3980]. Spectroscopic observations reveal that the amplitudes of its spectral components vary over time, though the overall line shapes remain stable [arXiv:1308.3980].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes SALT spectra of quasar LBQS 2113-4538, finding a Lorentzian Mg II profile, stable line shapes, variable component amplitudes, and class A quasar line width.

quotes.quote: We performed three spectroscopic observations of a quasar LBQS 2113-4538 (z = 0.956) with the SALT telescope separated in time by several months and we analyze in detail the mean spectrum and the variability in the spectral shape. We show that even in our good-quality spectra the Mg II doublet is well fit by a single Lorentzian shape. We tested several models of the Fe II pseudo-continuum and showed that one of them well represents all the data. The amplitudes of both components vary in time, but the shapes do not change significantly. The measured line width of LBQS 2113-4538 identifies this object as a class A quasar.

gmw_00006389.name: host galaxy of GRB 191004B
gmw_00006389.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 191004B is a faint star-forming galaxy at a redshift of z=3.5055 [arXiv:2006.09377]. It displays Lyman-alpha emission, negligible extinction, and a constrained star-formation rate between 1 and 4.7 solar masses per year [arXiv:2006.09377].

sources.summary: The host of GRB 191004B is a z=3.5055 LyC-leaking, Lyman-alpha-emitting faint star-forming galaxy with low neutral hydrogen, negligible extinction, and constrained star-formation rate.

quotes.quote: Here we present the observations of LyC emission in the afterglow spectra of GRB 191004B at z=3.5055 , together with those of the other two previously known LyC-leaking LGRB host galaxies ( GRB 050908 at z=3.3467 , and GRB 060607A at z=3.0749 ), to determine their LyC escape fraction and compare their properties. From the afterglow spectrum of GRB 191004B we determine a neutral hydrogen column density at the LGRB redshift of = 17.2 0.15 , and negligible extinction ( A_ V =0.03 0.02 mag). The only metal absorption lines detected are C iv and Si iv . In contrast to GRB 050908 and GRB 060607A, the host galaxy of GRB 191004B displays significant Lyman-alpha ( ) emission. From its emission and the non-detection of Balmer emission lines we constrain its star-formation rate (SFR) to 1 SFR 4.7 .

gmw_00006390.name: GRB 050908
gmw_00006390.overview: GRB 050908 is a faint host galaxy located at a redshift of z=3.3467 [arXiv:2006.09377]. Deep Hubble Space Telescope imaging detected the galaxy at a magnitude of 27.67, and it is identified as a Lyman continuum leaker [arXiv:2006.09377].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the GRB 050908 host as a faint z=3.3467 LyC-leaking galaxy detected in deep HST imaging, with afterglow spectra used to derive its escape fraction.

quotes.quote: GRB 050908 occurred at z=3.3467 . Its afterglow spectrum was observed with VLT/FORS1 Fynbo2009 , KeckII/Deimos and Gemini North/GMOS Chen2007 , and showed very strong high- and low-ionization absorption lines at the GRB redshift. In this paper we use the spectrum obtained by VLT/FORS.

In addition to the absorptions associated with the GRB host galaxy, two strong Mg II intervening absorbers are identified in the GRB afterglow spectrum at z=0.6915 and 1.4288. The emission counterparts of these absorbers have been identified by Schulze2012 a few tens of kpc away from the GRB host. Deep HST-F775W images of the field allowed the detection of the GRB host galaxy with m_ AB =27.67 mag Hjorth2012, Blanchard2016 .

The median escape fraction for the latter 92 is 0.10_ 0.02 ^ +0.08 , with an average value of f_ esc, abs = 0.15 . This estimation is fully consistent with the value of f_ esc =0.08^ +0.05 _ 0.04 derived by from the column density ( =17.6 ), following the method presented in Sect. DirectMeasurement .

gmw_00006391.name: NGC 7162
gmw_00006391.overview: NGC 7162 is a late-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:1810.12466]. It is rich in neutral hydrogen gas, and its inclination angle decreases from 70 degrees to 55 degrees toward the outer edge of its disk [arXiv:1810.12466].

sources.summary: The paper detects NGC 7162 in H I, finds it H I rich and dark-matter dominated, and reports faint extended H I emission toward NGC 7162A.

quotes.quote: NGC 7162 and NGC 7162A are late type spirals, detected and included in the H i Parkes All-Sky Survey catalogue HIPASS, sources HIPASS J2159--43 and HIPASS J2200--43, respectively, Meyer2004 . Note however, the HIPASS detection of NGC 7162 is confused with NGC 7162A due to the Parkes beam size ( 15.5 arcmin), so the HIPASS measured spectrum and total flux for both galaxies are not separated.

NGC 7162 is H i rich by 0.72 dex. Similarly to NGC 7162A, NGC 7162 follows the scaling relations in figure 5 from for (M_ HI /M_*) vs (M_*) and ( sSFR ) (Table  table:galaxy_params ). We find the inclination angle decreases towards the outer edge of the disk, starting at 70^ and decreasing to 55^ at the largest measured radius.

gmw_00006392.name: NGC 7162A
gmw_00006392.overview: NGC 7162A is an H I-rich late-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:1810.12466]. It exhibits a 40-degree offset between its optical major axis and its gas kinematics, which may indicate a past interaction [arXiv:1810.12466].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7162A as an H I-rich late-type spiral with kinematic-optical position-angle offsets, possible past interaction signatures, and a Type 2 XUV disk.

quotes.quote: I We start with the most promising candidate, NGC 7162A, which appears to have a small twist in position angle between velocity field contours at the galaxy's centre compared with the outer regions (Fig.  fig:all_maps4 row b, centre column). We also see this in the derived position angle from the tilted ring fitting to the the full disk. However there is large uncertainty when looking at either the approaching or receding sides separately and the position angle is approximately constant across all radii within the errors (Fig.  fig:both_galaxies_kin15 e).

The position angles from the 25 mag arcsec ^ 2 B- band image and the value derived from the H i gas kinematics from SoFiA are offset by 40^ , indicating the gas does not rotate in accordance with the galaxy's optical major axis. This, along with the possible small twist in the position angle with radius, could indicate that an interaction occurred in the group's past, either sufficiently long ago that there are no other obvious signs of the interaction or that we simply are unable to detect any morphological signs due to the imaging limitations of our observations. In this group, NGC 7162A is the most H i rich galaxy with a H i mass excess of 0.95 dex compared with isolated galaxies.

gmw_00006393.name: ESO 288-G025
gmw_00006393.overview: ESO 288-G025 is a late-type spiral galaxy that belongs to a galaxy group [arXiv:1810.12466]. It exhibits a normal neutral hydrogen gas content and shows excellent alignment between its optical and gas kinematics [arXiv:1810.12466].

sources.summary: The paper treats ESO 288-G025 as a late-type spiral group member, detected in H I, with normal H I content and aligned optical and gas kinematics.

quotes.quote: ESO 288-G025 and ESO 288-G033 are late type spirals, which are marginally detected in the HIPASS data, but not included in the HIPASS catalogue. AM 2159-434 and GALEXASC J220338.65-431128.7 are dwarf galaxies with no previous H i detection or distance measurement.

ESO 288-G025 has a H i excess of 0.39 dex, which we consider to be H i normal (see Section  sec:hi_gas ). This is expected as ESO 288-G025 has the largest separation from the group (e.g. projected distance of 305 kpc to NGC 7162). There is excellent agreement between the position angles from the optical B- band image with the derived value from the H i gas kinematics from SoFiA ( 53^ vs 52^ , respectively).

gmw_00006394.name: ESO 288-G033
gmw_00006394.overview: ESO 288-G033 is a newly detected galaxy group member that is rich in neutral hydrogen gas [arXiv:1810.12466]. Its neutral hydrogen emission appears asymmetric, extending further to the north, and exhibits a small position-angle offset from the optical B-band image [arXiv:1810.12466].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ESO 288-G033 as a newly detected H I-rich group member with modest position-angle offset and possibly asymmetric H I emission.

quotes.quote: ESO 288-G033, AM 2159-434 and J220338-431131 are newly detected group members. Additionally, these are the first H i detections and distance measurements of the dwarf galaxies AM 2159-434 and J220338-431131.

ESO 288-G033 is also H i rich (excess 0.66 dex). There is a small offset ( 10^ ) between the position angles from the optical B- band image with the derived value from the H i gas kinematics from SoFiA. The ATCA column density map of ESO 288-G033 appears asymmetric with the H i emission more extended to the north (Fig.  fig:all_maps4 row 4, left column, thick grey contour).

gmw_00006395.name: AM 2159-434
gmw_00006395.overview: AM 2159-434 is a dwarf galaxy that has been newly detected in neutral hydrogen [arXiv:1810.12466]. It has a measured velocity of 2558 kilometers per second and an estimated dynamical mass of approximately 9.1 billion solar masses [arXiv:1810.12466].

sources.summary: The paper reports AM 2159-434 as a dwarf galaxy newly detected in H I, newly measured in distance, and assigned an approximate dynamical mass.

quotes.quote: Two of the new detections are also the first H i detections of the dwarf galaxies, AM 2159-434 and GALEXASC J220338.65-431128.7, for which we have measured velocities of cz=2558 and cz=2727 km s ^ 1 , respectively. We confirm that there is extended H i emission around NGC 7162 possibly due to past interactions in the group as indicated by the 40^ offset between the kinematic and morphological major axes for NGC 7162A, and its H i richness.

Although we are unable to use envelope tracing or tilted ring modelling for either dwarf galaxy, we attempt to derive approximate dynamical mass estimates up to a factor of ^2(i) , leaving the galaxy inclination angle as a free parameter. For AM 2159-434, we derive a dynamical mass estimate of (M_ dyn ^2(i)/ M _ ) 9.1 0.4 (where we assume the rotational velocity to be half the velocity width, w_ 50 , of the integrated spectrum, 27 km s ^ 1 , and the radius to be the radius in the moment 0 map, 8.4 kpc ).

gmw_00006396.name: J220338-431131
gmw_00006396.overview: J220338-431131 is a dwarf galaxy with a measured velocity of 2727 km/s [arXiv:1810.12466]. It is a newly identified member of a galaxy group and has an estimated dynamical mass of approximately 8.9 billion solar masses [arXiv:1810.12466].

sources.summary: The paper treats J220338-431131 as a newly detected dwarf group member, gives its first H I detection and distance measurement, and estimates its dynamical mass.

quotes.quote: Two of the new detections are also the first H i detections of the dwarf galaxies, AM 2159-434 and GALEXASC J220338.65-431128.7, for which we have measured velocities of cz=2558 and cz=2727 km s ^ 1 , respectively. We confirm that there is extended H i emission around NGC 7162 possibly due to past interactions in the group as indicated by the 40^ offset between the kinematic and morphological major axes for NGC 7162A, and its H i richness.

Similarly we derive an approximate dynamical mass estimate for J220338-431131 of (M_ dyn ^2(i)/ M _ ) 8.9 0.4 (assumed rotational velocity 18 km s ^ 1 and radius 10.9 kpc ). As we mentioned, we are limited in our mass modelling to estimating the total dark matter content rather than differentiating among different dark matter density profiles.

gmw_00006397.name: SDSS0734
gmw_00006397.overview: SDSS0734 is an elliptical brightest cluster galaxy located within a fossil group [arXiv:1401.3176]. Spectroscopic data reveals that the galaxy has a younger central region and exhibits negative metallicity gradients across its entire probed radial range [arXiv:1401.3176].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS0734 as a fossil-group BCG with a strong metallicity gradient, a younger central region, and evidence for extended or centrally enhanced star formation.

quotes.quote: We report on Gemini GMOS spectroscopic data for the BCGs of the two fossil groups SDSS J073422.21+265144.9 and SDSS J075828.10+374711.8 (hereafter SDSS0734 and SDSS0758). The sample was selected from the list of fossil groups in diazgimenez08 , originally identified in the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) DR6 adelman08 . diazgimenez08 used a Friends-of-Friends analysis to find groups within the SDSS with an over-density contrast of 200, more than 10 spectroscopically confirmed members, masses greater than 5 10 ^ 13 h^ −1 M _ and redshifts lower than 0.1. Using these criteria, diazgimenez08 identified five groups with m _ 12 values larger than 2.0. We had originally intended to observe all five bona-fide fossil groups from diazgimenez08 , however, given the time allocation constraints, we obtained data for only two of them (galaxies II and IV in diazgimenez08 ).

Fig. agez shows that, in SDSS0734 there is clear evidence that the central region of the galaxy is younger than its outer regions. There is also a suggestion that the central region has lower [ E/Fe ] than the outer regions. Both [ Fe/H ] and [ Z/H ] show negative gradients over the whole radial range probed.

gmw_00006398.name: AS2UDS.0243.0
gmw_00006398.overview: AS2UDS.0243.0 is a high-redshift, luminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1805.05363]. It is identified as a line emitter whose photometric properties suggest the detected emission may be CO(8-7) at a redshift of z=1.63 [arXiv:1805.05363].

sources.summary: The paper identifies AS2UDS.0243.0 as a line emitter whose photometry may favor CO(8-7) at z=1.63 rather than a z≈4.5 interpretation.

quotes.quote: We next estimate the line luminosities of the two sources where the submillimeter emission is spatially coincident (within 0.5'' ) with a B band detection: AS2UDS.0243.0 and AS2UDS.0535.0, assuming these correspond to high- J ^ 12 CO lines. The photometric redshifts of AS2UDS.0243.0 and AS2UDS.0535.0 are reported in Table table:photometry . At these redshifts the emission lines would correspond to CO ( 8-7 ) at z=1.63 0.01 with L_ CO (8-7) = 1.5 10^8 \ for AS2UDS.0243.0 and CO ( 5-4 ) at z=0.70 0.01 with L_ CO (5-4) = 0.2 10^8 \ for AS2UDS.0535.0.

These luminosities are approximately an order of magnitude brighter than found in typical local ULIRGs e.g. \ Arp 220; Rangwala2011 or AGN-dominated sources e.g. \ Mrk 231; vanderWerf2010 . AS2UDS.0243.0 and AS2UDS.0535.0 have photometric properties which indicate the line we detect may instead be CO(8--7) or CO(5--4) respectively, corresponding to z_ CO < 2 . Further observations are needed to confirm the nature of these emission lines.

gmw_00006399.name: AS2UDS.0535.0
gmw_00006399.overview: AS2UDS.0535.0 is a line-emitting galaxy with photometry that suggests a possible CO(5-4) emission line at a redshift of z=0.70 [arXiv:1805.05363]. Alternatively, the galaxy's photometric redshift distribution exhibits a secondary peak at a high redshift of z=4.63 [arXiv:1805.05363].

sources.summary: The paper describes AS2UDS.0535.0 as a line emitter with photometry suggesting possible CO(5-4) at z=0.70 and a secondary high-redshift solution.

quotes.quote: Some or all of these sources may also be lensed (final column); see Section sec:COvCII . 3 AS2UDS.0535.0 has a secondary peak in its photometric redshift distribution at z = 4.63 (see Appendix app:notes ). We next estimate the line luminosities of the two sources where the submillimeter emission is spatially coincident (within 0.5'' ) with a B band detection: AS2UDS.0243.0 and AS2UDS.0535.0, assuming these correspond to high- J ^ 12 CO lines.

At these redshifts the emission lines would correspond to CO ( 8-7 ) at z=1.63 0.01 with L_ CO (8-7) = 1.5 10^8 \ for AS2UDS.0243.0 and CO ( 5-4 ) at z=0.70 0.01 with L_ CO (5-4) = 0.2 10^8 \ for AS2UDS.0535.0. AS2UDS.0243.0 and AS2UDS.0535.0 have photometric properties which indicate the line we detect may instead be CO(8--7) or CO(5--4) respectively, corresponding to z_ CO < 2 . Further observations are needed to confirm the nature of these emission lines.

gmw_00006400.name: AS2UDS.0109.0
gmw_00006400.overview: AS2UDS.0109.0 is a high-redshift galaxy candidate at z ≈ 4.5 [arXiv:1805.05363]. It has a possible companion, AS2UDS.0109.1, at the same redshift, though the field has limited photometric coverage [arXiv:1805.05363].

sources.summary: The paper reports AS2UDS.0109.0 as a z≈4.5 line-emitter candidate with a possible companion at the same redshift and limited photometric coverage.

quotes.quote: In one of these SMGs (AS2UDS.0109.1) we found a tentative S/N =5.3 emission line corresponding to the same redshift as the detected emission line source (AS2UDS.0109.0). AS2UDS.0109.1 has a tentative 5.3 detection of an emission line which would correspond to at z=4.451 . This would place it at the same redshift at AS2UDS.0109.0, however the line is very weak and the false positive rate at this significance and line width is 50 .

There is no optical or Spitzer coverage of this field so there is little photometric information available, however its K band photometry and 1.4 GHz limit is consistent with a z 4.5 emitter. AS2UDS.0109.0 is not covered by many optical or near-infrared images. It has a K and 4.5 \ detection, however it is undetected at 1.4 GHz and is therefore consistent with a z 4.5 emitter.

gmw_00006401.name: AS2UDS.0109.1
gmw_00006401.overview: AS2UDS.0109.1 is a high-redshift submillimeter galaxy [arXiv:1805.05363]. It is classified as a luminous infrared galaxy and has a tentative emission line detection that suggests a redshift of z=4.451 [arXiv:1805.05363].

sources.summary: The paper treats AS2UDS.0109.1 as a supplementary SMG with a tentative line at the same redshift as AS2UDS.0109.0, requiring confirmation.

quotes.quote: In one of these SMGs (AS2UDS.0109.1) we found a tentative S/N =5.3 emission line corresponding to the same redshift as the detected emission line source (AS2UDS.0109.0). We include this SMG as a ``supplementary'' source The false positive rate at this significance and line width is 50 and so this source requires further observations in order to confirm it. AS2UDS.0109.1 has a tentative 5.3 detection of an emission line which would correspond to at z=4.451 .

This would place it at the same redshift at AS2UDS.0109.0, however the line is very weak and the false positive rate at this significance and line width is 50 . Further observations are therefore required to confirm the line. The spectrum and photometric thumbnails for AS2UDS.0109.1 are shown in Figure fig:suppl .

gmw_00006402.name: HST 14113 + 5211
aliases_external: SDSS J141119.68+521129.9, lens galaxy of HST 14113+5211
gmw_00006402.overview: HST 14113 + 5211 is an early-type galaxy located at a redshift of 0.464 [arXiv:astro-ph/0507056]. It acts as a gravitational lens, and joint analyses of its lensing and stellar dynamics indicate a nearly isothermal total density profile [arXiv:astro-ph/0507056].

sources.summary: Subaru spectroscopy measured the lensing galaxy velocity dispersion and the paper models its lensing, stellar dynamics, mass-to-light ratio, dark matter fraction, and nearly isothermal total density profile.

quotes.quote: We select two lens systems, HST14113 + 5211 ( z_L=0.464 ; Fischer, Schade Barrientos 1998; Lubin et al. 2000) and B 2045 + 265 ( z_L=0.868 ; Fassnacht et al. 1999), where the lensing galaxies are an early-type galaxy. We conducted the direct measurement of velocity dispersion of the lensing galaxies of those systems using the Faint Object Camera and Spectrograph (FOCAS, Kashikawa et al. 2002) mounted on the Subaru Telescope. We perform a joint gravitational lensing and stellar dynamics analysis to explore the luminous and dark matter distribution of lensing galaxies focusing on the slope of (dark and total matter) density profile and mass-to-light ratio.

From the results based on the current joint analysis (dashed lines), the likely mass-to-light ratio appears to be in the range of 4 M_ /L_B 6 M_ /L_ B, : after marginalizing over , we obtain the most likely values of M_ /L_B ( M_ /L_ B, ), yielding 4.4^ +2.0 _ 2.1 for Model A and 5.6^ +2.2 _ 2.2 for Model B. As is evident, these values of M_ /L_B are virtually consistent with those obtained from the FP in 3 ( 4.9 1.4 M_ /L_ B, ). We also calculate the most likely fraction of the dark matter projected inside R_E ( 1.4 R_e ), which is denoted as f_ DM .

gmw_00006403.name: IC 3973
gmw_00006403.overview: IC 3973 is a galaxy in the Coma Cluster identified as a post-merger candidate [arXiv:1601.06638]. Its outer halo appears rotated and shifted relative to its bright core, displaying a curved residual extension [arXiv:1601.06638].

sources.summary: The Coma red-sequence study identifies IC 3973 as its only post-merger candidate, with a shifted outer halo and curved residual extension but low measured asymmetry.

quotes.quote: The only remnant candidate found is IC 3973, which is previously identified as a member of one of the five pairs and the triple system in Sect. visual . This galaxy is not identified as a member of an on-going merger since the observed feature does not appear to be related to the presence of the projected partner, and the partner itself does not show any evidence of perturbation. The asymmetry parameter determined for this galaxy is 0.0419, a value still small for a perturbed galaxy; we believe that this parameter does not actually reflect the merger remnant character detected visually due to the low surface brightness of its external halo compared with the galaxy nucleus. In the direct F814W image of this galaxy (Fig. 4, upper panel), the outer halo of the galaxy appears to be rotated and shifted with respect to the inner bright core, while in the residual image (lower panel), a curved extension is apparent coming out clockwise from the low right corner of the galaxy halo.

gmw_00006404.name: QSO J2345 1555
gmw_00006404.overview: QSO J2345 1555 is an active galactic nucleus classified as a quasar [arXiv:2402.04352]. Observations of the source have revealed a tentative quasi-periodic oscillation with a period of 3.5 days [arXiv:2402.04352].

sources.summary: The paper finds a tentative 3.5-day QPO in QSO J2345 1555, with GLSP and time-averaged WWZ support despite entanglement with weaker peaks.

quotes.quote: Fig. 1(b) shows the results obtained for the 25-day sector 29 (cycle 3) observation of QSO J2345 1555. In the GLSP result, a strong peak having significance at least 3 is detected at 3.5 days assuming a either simple or a bending power law as the underlying model. The wavelet analysis shows this peak is entangled with other lower significance peaks and together, they are much stronger in the first half of the observation. In the time-averaged WWZ plot, this peak at 3.5 days has a significance of 3 , so we accept it as meeting our full set of criteria or a tentative claim of a QPO.

gmw_00006405.name: QSO B0422+004
gmw_00006405.overview: QSO B0422+004 is a blazar and active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2402.04352] that exhibits high and variable optical and infrared polarization [arXiv:astro-ph/9910269]. Timing analysis of its light curves reveals a quasi-periodic oscillation of approximately 6 days, with additional power detected around 3 days [arXiv:2402.04352].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes two TESS sectors for QSO B0422+004 and favors a roughly 6-day QPO, with additional 3-day power possibly harmonically related.

quotes.quote: The light curves and the timing analysis results of QSO B0422+004 are plotted in Fig. 2. This source was observed during two different TESS cycles. The results for the 25-day sector 5 (cycle 1) observation are shown in Fig. 2(a).

The analysis of the other 25-day sector 32 (cycle 3) data for this source (Fig. 2(b)), shows a strong (3 ) signal at 6.0 days in the GLSP with both red noise models. This is also present in time-averaged WWZ analysis with 3 significance, respectively. With a simple power law, a peak around 3 days is also found to be more than 3 significant.

sources.summary: The paper reports optical and infrared polarization changes in 0422+004, similar infrared and optical variations, and significant infrared color correlations.

quotes.quote: 0422+004, z=? An optical variation of m=2.2 (Branly et al. 1996) and high and variable polarizations are known for this object. The infrared polarization increases from 13.6 to 19.8 when the source dimmed by 0.3 mag in K (Holmes et al. 1984) and in other case it decreases from 20.27 to 12.25 in two days when the source does not show significant variation, during which period the optical polarization also decreases from 22.14 to 13.69 (Mead et al; 1990). The infrared data show that the infrared variations are similar to the optical ones. A correlation between (H-K) and J: (H-K) = -0.51 + 0.1J with = 0.50 and p = 4.4 10^ 3 .

gmw_00006406.name: 1RXS J002159.2 514028
gmw_00006406.overview: 1RXS J002159.2 514028 is a blazar and an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2402.04352]. Timing analyses of the source reveal a tentative 4.5-day quasi-periodic oscillation that is stronger during the first half of the observation period [arXiv:2402.04352].

sources.summary: The paper reports a tentative 4.5-day QPO in 1RXS J002159.2 514028, significant in GLSP and WWZ but weaker because power is concentrated early.

quotes.quote: In Fig. 3(a), our timing analyses for the 27-day sector 2 observation of 1RXS J002159.2 514028 are shown. A peak of around 4.5 days is found which is at least 3 in both the GLSP and time-averaged WWZ results using both simple and bending power laws as underlying red noise models. This 4.5-day signal is found to be stronger in the first half of the observation, as displayed in the WWZ colour diagram so the claim of a QPO for this source is less strong.

gmw_00006407.name: BX1
gmw_00006407.overview: BX1 is a high-redshift, UV-bright Lyman-alpha galaxy [arXiv:1301.5600]. It has an estimated stellar mass of approximately 1.8 billion solar masses and a dust-corrected H-alpha star formation rate of 28.5 solar masses per year [arXiv:1301.5600].

sources.summary: The paper derives BX1's reddening, stellar mass, corrected H-alpha star-formation rate, and UV-based star-formation rate from its spectrum and LAE subsample membership.

quotes.quote: As an example of the quantities introduced above, we present here the calculation relative to BX1. It was characterized by a quality A spectrum in GOODS survey. Fitting the spectroscopic rest-frame UV continuum with a power law, f _ ^ , we obtained _ best fit =-1.6 0.11 and so E(B-V) _ =0.12 0.02 , Eg(B-V) _ =0.27 0.04 . The Balmer decrement is measured as F(H )/F(H ) = 2.6 2.5 i.e. F(H )/F(H ) 5.1 , which implies E _ 0.63. Its stellar reddening is then E(B-V) _ 0.54 , roughly consistent with the SED estimation, but not very conclusive.
