BX1 was originally selected to have R=24.65 and it belonged to the UV-bright LAE sub-sample (the R band magnitude of the stacked UV-bright sub-sample SED is 25.0). We, therefore, assumed the stacked SED shape was representative of this object, but we rescaled by a factor 1.4 the stellar mass to account for the individual object having a brighter R mag, yielding log(M/M _ )=9.25 [ 9.05-9.55 ] . Assuming E(B-V) _ = 0.12 0.02 and a Calzetti law for dust attenuation, we calculated F _ corr (H ) = (9.4 2.1)E-17 erg sec ^ 1 cm ^2 and SFR _ corr (H ) = 28.5 8.2 M _ yr ^ 1 .

gmw_00006408.name: BX10
gmw_00006408.overview: BX10 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy [arXiv:1301.5600]. Spectroscopic observations of the galaxy show an upper-limit hydrogen-alpha star-formation rate and a potentially large velocity offset [arXiv:1301.5600].

sources.summary: The paper reports BX10 as a Ly-alpha emitter with an upper-limit H-alpha star-formation rate and a large, uncertain velocity offset affected by sky-line residuals.

quotes.quote: Our spectroscopy revealed the presence of Ly emission with equivalent widths larger than 20 for BX1 and BX10, confirming them as LAEs. On the other hand H flux was an upper limit for the BX10 spectrum and it translated into an upper limit of SFR _ corr (H ). BX10 is characterized by 749.9 188.3 km sec ^ 1 and the highest uncertainty in [ OIII ] central wavelength ( 18095.5 11.6 ). In fact this emission line is on top of sky line residuals (Fig. ELLAE ), which affect the line integrated flux and central wavelength. However, we can not exclude it being a high-velocity-offset source.

gmw_00006409.name: z2LAE3
gmw_00006409.overview: The galaxy z2LAE3 was initially selected as a Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy through a narrow-band technique [arXiv:1301.5600]. However, line ratios indicate that it is likely an active galactic nucleus and the brightest Lyman-alpha source in its sample [arXiv:1301.5600].

sources.summary: The paper finds z2LAE3, initially selected as a narrow-band LAE, is likely an AGN and the brightest Ly-alpha source in the sample.

quotes.quote: The red star corresponds to z2LAE3 . Even if it was initially selected as LAE through narrow-band technique in EHDF-S, it appeared to be an AGN based on Fig. agn line ratios. It is the brightest source of the sample, with L(Ly ) was equal to 10 ^ 43.5 erg sec ^ 1 (F(Ly ) 1.0E-15 erg sec ^ 1 cm ^ 2 and EW (Ly )_ rest-frame = 74 ) and it was the brightest source of the LAE sample in EHDF-S. For comparison, we show BX16 and DB12, which fall in the Starburst region of the diagram, as well as LAEs from Fin2011 . However, we note that the most massive and most Ly luminous galaxies are generally found near the border of AGN and Starburst regions.

gmw_00006410.name: DB12
gmw_00006410.overview: DB12 is classified as a starburst galaxy [arXiv:1301.5600]. Its metallicity has been constrained from its emission line ratios, and its H-alpha star-formation rate is treated as a lower limit [arXiv:1301.5600].

sources.summary: The paper places DB12 in the starburst region, treats its H-alpha star-formation rate as a lower limit, and derives its metallicity from line ratios.

quotes.quote: For comparison, we show BX16 and DB12, which fall in the Starburst region of the diagram, as well as LAEs from Fin2011 . In the case of BX4, BX7, BX14, and DB12, the SFR(H ) was assumed to be a lower limit of the SFR _ corr (H ) given the uncertain correction factor. Due to low signal-to-noise from most [ NII ] and H emission lines, we were only able to constrain 12+log(O/H) only for two sources (BX16 and DB12) and to set lower (upper) limits for two (two) others. As an example, [ NII ] /H =0.2 for DB12. This ratio corresponds to 12+log(O/H)=8.7 or 8.5 following Nagao2006 or PP2004 respectively.

gmw_00006411.name: LBG11
gmw_00006411.overview: LBG11 is a high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy [arXiv:1301.5600]. It exhibits a Lyman-alpha rest-frame equivalent width of 8 and displays one of the highest expansion velocities in its sample [arXiv:1301.5600].

sources.summary: The paper measures LBG11's Ly-alpha equivalent width, finds its metallicity estimate uninformative, and identifies one of the sample's highest expansion velocities.

quotes.quote: Thanks to MUSYC VIMOS and IMACS rest-frame UV spectra, LAE27 and z3LAE2 were confirmed to be LAEs, while LBG11 showed EW _ rest-frame = 8 (Fig. ELLAE ). In the case of LBG11, [ OIII ] 5007/H = 2.8 2.4 gave 12+log(O/H) 9.0 , which is not informative. For LBG11, we estimated v_ Ly [ OIII ] 5007 = 344.5 107.0 km sec ^ 1 , which is consistent with the value estimated by McLin2011 for their strongest [ OIII ] 5007 emission line LAE (F( [ OIII ] 5007)=( 35.48 1.15 )E-17 erg sec ^ 1 cm ^ 2 ) at similar redshift. LBG11, for example, shows one of the highest Vexp of our sample.

gmw_00006412.name: BL Lac J1043+2408
gmw_00006412.overview: BL Lac J1043+2408 is a blazar located at a redshift of approximately 0.563 [arXiv:1811.12689]. The source exhibits long-term radio variability at 15 GHz, characterized by a periodic flux modulation of roughly 563 days [arXiv:1811.12689].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes decade-long 15 GHz monitoring of BL Lac J1043+2408 and reports long-term variability plus a roughly 563-day periodic radio modulation.

quotes.quote: BL Lac J1043+2408 (RA=10h 43m 09.0s, Dec= +24d 08m 35s, and z = 0.563446; ) has been detected by most of the currently available instruments operating within a wide range of electromagnetic frequencies. The source is cataloged by Fermi/LAT as 3FGL J1043.1+2407 , and in the soft X-ray (0.1-2.4 keV) it was frequently observed by ROSAT . During The Micro-Arcsecond Scintillation-Induced Variability Survey III, its optical (R-band) brightness was recorded to be 16.84 magnitudes . The blazar is being regularly monitored in the 15-GHz radio band by the 40-m telescope of the Owens Valley Radio Observatory (OVRO) since 2008. In this paper, we analyze the long term ( 10.5 years) radio observations of the blazar J1043+2408 and report detection of 563 d periodicity in the light curve.

The 15 GHz band light curve of the blazar J1043+2408 from the last decade is presented in Figure LCs . In the figure, it can be clearly seen that not only the source displays long-term variability but it also shows a periodic flux modulation such that in each cycle the flux nearly doubles between minimum and maximum. To quantify the observed long-term variability, we estimated a fractional variability of 20.29 0.63 , indicating a moderate variability over the period.

gmw_00006413.name: J2219+0102
gmw_00006413.overview: J2219+0102 is a faint quasar discovered at a redshift of z=6.16 [arXiv:1710.02212]. Its host galaxy exhibits a starburst with resolved kiloparsec-scale continuum and line emission [arXiv:1710.02212], as well as a high line-to-infrared luminosity ratio [arXiv:2203.03658].

sources.summary: VIMOS2911 is a faint z=6.16 quasar whose ALMA host detections imply a powerful starburst with resolved kiloparsec-scale continuum and line emission.

quotes.quote: VIMOS2911 VIMOS2911 is a faint quasar discovered at z=6.16 in the Subaru High-z Quasar Survey of . With absolute magnitude M_ 1450 =-23.1 it was the second z 6 quasar, after CFHQS J0216 0455, to be found in the rest-frame UV luminosity range overlapping that of star-forming galaxies. The black hole mass has not yet been measured using broad emission line widths, but assuming that it accretes at the Eddington rate (see Section 5 for rationale of this assumption), find it to have M_ BH = 5 10^7 M_ .

Considering the low luminosity and black hole mass of VIMOS2911, the ALMA observations show a surprisingly powerful starburst in the host galaxy. There are very strong \ line and continuum detections, only a little less luminous than for PSO J167 13. In the continuum the source has a deconvolved size of 0.29 0.07 0.08 0.14 arcsec at position angle of 142 ^ , with the major axis size equivalent to 1.7 kpc. The \ moment zero source size is somewhat larger at 0.52 0.05 0.38 0.06 arcsec at position angle of 20 ^ , equivalent to 3.0 2.2 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper observes J2219+0102 in two programs covering multiple CO lines, merges the datasets, and identifies it as the sample source with the highest quoted line-to-infrared ratio.

quotes.quote: We observed the quasar J2219+0102 as part of two programs, one covering the CO(5-4) and CO(6--5) lines (August--September 2018, program ID: S18DM), and one covering the CO(6--5), CO(7--6) and lines (June 2019, program ID: S19DM). The bright sources 3C454.3 and MWC349 acted as bandpass and flux calibrators, while the quasars 2216-038 and 2223-052 were observed as amplitude and phase calibrators. We reduced the two settings independently, and then merged the visibilities in the overlapping frequency range. The 2018 and 2019 observations comprise 13380 and 8640 visibilities, respectively, corresponding to 5.97 and 3.86 h on source (8-antenna equivalent).

Finally, the /IR luminosity ratios in our sample span a factor 6 , from 1.4 10^ 3 (J2219+0102) to 2.4 10^ 4 (J2054--0005), with lower /IR ratios typically observed for quasars with L_ IR >3 10^ 12 see, e.g., stacey10,gullberg15 .

gmw_00006414.name: J1048-0109
gmw_00006414.overview: J1048-0109 is a high-redshift quasar host galaxy [arXiv:2203.03658]. The galaxy exhibits extremely high star formation rate densities, which lead to elevated dust temperatures ranging from 40 to 100 Kelvin [arXiv:2203.03658].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1048-0109 observations and calibration, and includes the source among high-resolution quasar hosts whose star-formation-density method is compared against gas-mass estimates.

quotes.quote: Observations of the quasar J1048-0109 were performed using 3C84, 3C273, and LKHA101 as bandpass and flux calibrators, and 1055+018 as phase and amplitude calibrator. The final cube consists of 10170 visibilities (3.53 h on source, 9-antenna equivalent).

Along the same lines, one could adopt the method proposed by sommovigo21 to infer _ CII and T_ d based on the surface brightness of for those quasars in our sample that have already been observed at high angular resolution in PJ036+03, J1048--0109, J1319+0950, J2054--0005, and PJ359--06; see venemans20 . The extremely high SFR densities reached in these sources lead to very low _ CII 1 values and correspondingly to slightly higher dust temperatures, T_ d =40--100 K.

gmw_00006415.name: PJ359-06
gmw_00006415.overview: PJ359-06 is a high-redshift quasar host galaxy [arXiv:2203.03658]. High-resolution observations of the source reveal extremely high star formation rate densities and dust temperatures ranging from 40 to 100 K [arXiv:2203.03658].

sources.summary: The paper reports PJ359-06 calibration and final-cube exposure, then uses high-resolution information for this quasar host when discussing star-formation-density and gas-mass estimates.

quotes.quote: Finally, the observations of PJ359-06 capitalized on 3C454.3, MWC349, and 0003-066 for bandpass, flux, and amplitude/phase calibration. The final cube comprises 3.46 h on source (9-antenna equivalent).

Along the same lines, one could adopt the method proposed by sommovigo21 to infer _ CII and T_ d based on the surface brightness of for those quasars in our sample that have already been observed at high angular resolution in PJ036+03, J1048--0109, J1319+0950, J2054--0005, and PJ359--06; see venemans20 . The extremely high SFR densities reached in these sources lead to very low _ CII 1 values and correspondingly to slightly higher dust temperatures, T_ d =40--100 K.

gmw_00006416.name: ESO 184-G042
gmw_00006416.overview: ESO 184-G042 is a massive early-type galaxy located at a distance of 75 Mpc [arXiv:2404.09522]. It forms an interacting pair with the galaxy LEDA 418116, surrounded by diffuse light and outer radio shells that are centered on ESO 184-G042 [arXiv:2404.09522].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ESO 184-G042 as the primary S0 host and central galaxy of the Physalis interacting pair, with radio shells centered on it and diffuse intragroup light indicating tidal interaction.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy pair, which consists of the early-type galaxies ESO 184-G042 and LEDA 418116, is part of a loose group at a distance of only 75 Mpc (redshift z = 0.017 ). The observed outer radio shells (diameter 145 kpc) and ridge-like central emission of the system, ASKAP J1914--5433 (Physalis), are likely associated with merger shocks during the formation of the central galaxy (ESO 184-G042) and resemble the new class of odd radio circles (ORCs). This is supported by the brightest X-ray emission found offset from the centre of the Physalis system, instead centered at the less massive galaxy, LEDA 418116. The host galaxy pair is embedded in an irregular envelope of diffuse light, highlighting on-going interactions.

Both, shells and ridge, are centred on the massive early-type galaxy ESO 184-G042 (see Fig. fig:physalis-combo ). Some diffuse radio emission is also detected between the shells and the ridge. The overall size of the Physalis system is 400 \ 320 \ ( PA 156 ) or 145 kpc 116 kpc. That is less than half the diameter of the more distant single ORCs Norris2021a,Koribalski2021 . The velocity dispersion of ESO 184-G042 176 , Wegner2003 is close to that of the ORC host galaxies Rupke2023,Coil2024 .

gmw_00006417.name: LEDA 418116
gmw_00006417.overview: LEDA 418116 is an early-type galaxy located in a loose galaxy group [arXiv:2404.09522]. It is the less massive companion to ESO 184-G042, with which it is embedded in an irregular envelope of diffuse stellar light that indicates ongoing tidal interactions [arXiv:2404.09522].

sources.summary: The paper describes LEDA 418116 as ESO 184-G042's less massive companion, offset from the Physalis center, embedded with it in diffuse light and likely participating in an ongoing merger.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy pair, which consists of the early-type galaxies ESO 184-G042 and LEDA 418116, is part of a loose group at a distance of only 75 Mpc (redshift z = 0.017 ). The observed outer radio shells (diameter 145 kpc) and ridge-like central emission of the system, ASKAP J1914--5433 (Physalis), are likely associated with merger shocks during the formation of the central galaxy (ESO 184-G042) and resemble the new class of odd radio circles (ORCs). This is supported by the brightest X-ray emission found offset from the centre of the Physalis system, instead centered at the less massive galaxy, LEDA 418116. The host galaxy pair is embedded in an irregular envelope of diffuse light, highlighting on-going interactions.

The host galaxy system ESO 184-G042 is the brightest member of a loose galaxy group Tempel2018,Diaz2012 , which consists of three early-type galaxies and a small spiral galaxy that are linearly aligned (see Fig. fig:physalis-overview and Table tab:physalis-prop ). The closest neighbour of ESO 184-G042 is the galaxy LEDA 418116, located 1.4 arcmin or 30 kpc to the west. The extended envelope of diffuse stellar light around these two brightest group galaxies is remarkable, spanning at least 3.5 arcmin ( 76 kpc, see Fig. fig:physalis-combo ). The large amount of intra-group light is indicative of strong tidal interactions e.g., Spavone2018,Montes2022 .

gmw_00006418.name: Source ID 1
gmw_00006418.overview: Source ID 1 is a faint, diffuse galaxy with a face-on disk and clumpy star formation in its arms [arXiv:2107.01237]. It features a clumpy, extended northern tail and a distorted gas distribution that suggests interactions with an intra-group medium [arXiv:2107.01237].

sources.summary: The paper describes Source ID 1 as a faint, diffuse group galaxy with clumpy star formation, high H I column density, distorted gas, and a face-on disk with an extended tail.

quotes.quote: Source ID 1 is faint and diffuse in the optical, with clumpy star-formation in the arms, but with high column density ( N_ HI 2.5 10^ 21 cm ^ 2 ).
Sources ID 1 and ID 5 also have somewhat distorted distributions, suggestive of interactions with a putative intra-group medium.
The moment-0 map for source ID 1 shows a face-on disk and a clumpy, extended tail on the northern end.

gmw_00006419.name: source ID 5
gmw_00006419.overview: Source ID 5 is an edge-on spiral galaxy featuring an off-centre disk that extends further on its eastern side [arXiv:2107.01237]. The galaxy exhibits a distorted H I distribution and a large northern tidal tail, which are suggestive of environmental interactions [arXiv:2107.01237].

sources.summary: The paper highlights source ID 5 as an edge-on spiral with distorted H I, an off-centre disk, and a large tidal tail likely tied to environmental interaction.

quotes.quote: Sources ID 1 and ID 5 also have somewhat distorted distributions, suggestive of interactions with a putative intra-group medium. Source ID 5 is an edge-on spiral, with an off-centre disk. The disk extends further on the eastern side, with a large tidal tail on the northern end of the galaxy, in the opposite direction of the group's centre.

gmw_00006420.name: source ID 6
gmw_00006420.overview: Source ID 6 is classified as an irregular group galaxy that lacks any visible spiral structure in optical images [arXiv:2107.01237]. It possesses a high neutral hydrogen mass and exhibits the highest neutral hydrogen-to-stellar mass ratio within its group [arXiv:2107.01237].

sources.summary: The paper treats source ID 6 as an irregular group galaxy lacking spiral structure, with unusually extended H I, high H I mass, and the group's highest H I-to-stellar mass ratio.

quotes.quote: The majority of the identified sources in Fig.  fig:3-mommap1 can be classified as spiral or disk-like galaxies, based on the optical images, with the exception of sources ID 0 and ID 6, which appear to be irregular galaxies. Unlike the other sources, sources ID 0 and ID 6 do not have a sign of spiral structure in the optical images.

The higher-mass outlier, source ID 6, has an mass of (M_ HI / M _ ) = 10.66 , which is on the high-mass end of the mass function (M_ HI ^*/ M _ ) > 9.94 0.02 0.05 h_ 70 ^ 2 Jones_2018 . This source also has the highest to-stellar mass ratio of the group of f_ HI ^* = 1.55 , which is interesting, as the most massive objects tend to be stellar-dominated Maddox_2014 .

gmw_00006421.name: source ID 11
gmw_00006421.overview: Source ID 11 is an edge-on spiral galaxy that belongs to a group [arXiv:2107.01237]. It exhibits a slightly disturbed morphology, a central H I deficiency, and gas flaring on the southern side of its disk toward the edges [arXiv:2107.01237].

sources.summary: The paper notes Source ID 11 as an edge-on group galaxy with central H I deficiency, slight disturbance, and southern disk gas flaring toward the disk edges.

quotes.quote: A trend observed in most of the edge-on detected sources is an deficiency in the centre of galaxies, as is frequently seen in spirals with large central bulges or high SFR Bigiel_2008 . This can be clearly seen in sources ID 3, ID 4, and ID 11 (Fig.  fig:3-mommap1 ). There is a galaxy asymmetry or lopsidedness throughout the sample, particularly in the edge-on spiral galaxies. An example of this is the slightly disturbed morphology in source ID 11. On the southern side of the disk, the distribution appears to be flaring out towards the edges of the disk.

gmw_00006422.name: SDSS J2345+1104
gmw_00006422.overview: SDSS J2345+1104 is a prototype quasar located at a redshift of z=2 [arXiv:2010.15096]. Simulations of its assembly history indicate that both heavy and light black hole seeds formed during its early cosmic evolution [arXiv:2010.15096].

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSS J2345+1104 as the z=2 prototype quasar, modeling its host properties and predicting heavy and light seed formation.

quotes.quote: We choose as proto-typical objects for the three redshifts the quasars J1148 at z_ QSO =6.4 Fan01 , SDSS J2345+1104 at z=2 hereafter J2345 Shen11, Schulze19 , and PDS 456 at z=0.2 PDS456 hereafter Nardini15, Bischetti19 . We model the evolution of J1148, J2345 and PDS456 performing, for each of them, ten independent simulations adopting the set of model parameters described in Table  tab:parameters . These are tuned to reproduced the observed SMBH mass and host galaxy physical properties see for more details Valiante11,Valiante14,Valiante16 .

A total of 39 (31 and 40) heavy and 4228 (5327 and 5319) light seeds are formed along the assembly history of J1148 (J2345 and PDS456, respectively). In all cases, light seeds form in larger numbers at very high redshift ( 12<z<30 ) and over a longer period of cosmic evolution than heavy seeds, which are rarer (with relative fraction 1 ) and form for a shorter period of time at slightly lower redshift ( z 12 - 17 , depending on the considered system). In-situ and/or external pollution determines the end of the seed (and Pop III stars) formation era: as soon as all the galaxies have been enriched above the critical metallicity threshold ( Z_ cr =10^ 3.8 Z_ ), the transition to the Pop II star formation regime is completed. This critical level is reached, on average, at z 16 , 13 and 12 for quasar J1148, J2345 and PDS456, respectively.

gmw_00006423.name: NGC 5331
gmw_00006423.overview: NGC 5331 is a poorly studied, two-component starburst system [arXiv:0806.3002]. Both components are detected in the radio band, with the southern component being approximately five times brighter than the northern component [arXiv:0806.3002].

sources.summary: The survey describes NGC 5331 as a poorly studied two-component starburst system detected in radio, with an upper limit on soft X-ray luminosity.

quotes.quote: NGC 5331 (UGC 8774, VV 253) This galaxy has been relatively poorly studied. a95 classify both components as starbursts based on optical spectroscopy and rush96 give an upper limit on the soft X-ray luminosity. c90 detect both components at 1.49 GHz, with the southern component being about five times brighter than the northern component.

gmw_00006424.name: NGC 4848
gmw_00006424.overview: NGC 4848 is a blue, gas-deficient spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0109316, arXiv:1006.4867] that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2112.12244, arXiv:2207.06624] and exhibits starburst-level star formation [arXiv:2112.12244]. It features a disturbed inner morphology [arXiv:1006.4867] with asymmetric, one-sided gas tails extending to the northwest [arXiv:1006.4867, arXiv:2207.06624] due to ram-pressure stripping [arXiv:astro-ph/0107049, arXiv:1207.4806].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4848 as a blue, gas-deficient disk with displaced H I; simulations favor post-core-passage stripping and possible gas re-accretion over a merger.

quotes.quote: NGC 4848 is a blue disk galaxy showing a very intriguing distribution (see Fig. 6), as we only detect gas in the northern edge of the galaxy, 19 (6.5 kpc) NW from the optical center. We are probably missing some flux previously detected by single dish observations in the center of the galaxy (Gavazzi, 1989). This object was also observed in by Amram (1992) who reported a double peak in the profile, with the northern peak at the same position than the . Interestingly, the brightest HII regions are in the south, where the gas has been totally depleted.

Mergers do not account for the morphology or the star formation activity in NGC 4848, because no obvious companion is seen in the DSS optical image (Fig. 6). However, the more detailed B-band CCD imaging by Gavazzi (1990) shows a ring-like structure and blue bright zones in the NW, where the and the secondary peaks of CO, and 20 cm radio continuum are found. The hypothesis of a dwarf system crossing the NGC 4848 disk is explored by Vollmer (2001a) but further observations, both optical and higher resolution imaging, are needed to confirm it. N--body simulations by the same authors suggest that NGC 4848 has already gone through the cluster core, 4 10 ^8 yr ago, and is now moving away from the cluster. They conclude that re-accretion of some of the stripped gas could explain the star formation burst.

sources.summary: GMP 4471/NGC 4848 is the sample's brightest galaxy, a disturbed spiral with northwest UV and H trails, asymmetric core emission, unusual HI distribution, and HI deficiency.

quotes.quote: GMP 4471 = NGC 4848 This is the brightest galaxy in the sample, a spiral with a disturbed inner morphology and a faint extension to the north-west visible in the MegaCam imaging. In the GALEX data, we observe trails pointing north-west away from cluster centre, certainly to 1.4 arcmin (40 kpc), and tentatively to twice this distance. (The uncertainty arises because this galaxy is close to the edge of the GALEX field.) The INT H images show an asymmetric distribution of emission in the core, and a stream of emitting knots to the north-west of the galaxy, some of which are coincident with compact blue sources in the MegaCam imaging.

GMP 4471 has been the subject of previous studies: Gavazzi et al. (1998) showed the H morphology of the inner region, but no extended structure. Bravo-Alfaro et al. (2001) noted an unusual HI distribution peaking 0.3 arcmin (10 kpc) north-west of the core. It is also deficient in total HI (Def _ HI >0.49 , Gavazzi et al. 2006).

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4848 as an AGN-bearing Coma source with starburst-level sSFR, disturbed H I, Seyfert-like MIR properties, and H I fluxes consistent with prior measurements.

quotes.quote: chen20 found 0.3 dex higher H i fluxes for their three H i detections (NGC 4848, NGC 4911, and IC 4040) than that of bravo00 . A comparison of H i fluxes in chen20 to ours reveals a median flux ratio of 1. chen20 proposed that bravo00 may have missed the diffuse H i component due signal-to-noise constraints.

In Fig. fig::sfms we marked 4 sources, NGC 4921, NGC 4907, NGC 4911, and NGC 4848, with red circles, indicating they have been found to harbour an AGN in previous studies mahajan10,gavazzi11,toba14 . The two most massive ones ( (M_ / M_ ) 11.0 -- 11.5 ), NGC 4921 and NGC 4907, are quenched late-type galaxies. NGC 4911 is of similar M_ , but resides on the SFMS, while NGC 4848 ( (M_ / M_ ) 9 ) has a sSFR consistent with that of a starburst galaxy. NGC 4921, NGC 4907, and NGC 4848 have disturbed H i components.
They are most likely Seyferts, whose star formation can be comparable with their AGN activity e.g. in Circinus for12 , and as a result can have MIR colours similar to regular SF galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4848 as an H I-deficient Coma spiral whose molecular gas remains abundant but lopsided, with star formation and reaccreting stripped gas linked to earlier ram-pressure stripping.

quotes.quote: We study the molecular gas content and distribution in the Coma cluster spiral galaxy NGC 4848 which is H i deficient and where the remaining atomic gas is on one side of the galaxy, presumably because of the strong ram-pressure in the rich Coma cluster. Plateau de Bure interferometric CO(1--0) observations reveal a lopsided H _2 distribution with an off-center secondary maximum coincident with the inner part of the H i . NGC 4848 is not at all deficient in molecular gas as it contains M _ H_ 2 4 10^ 9 M _ in the central and inner disk regions.

We suggest that NGC 4848 already passed through the center of the cluster about 4 10 ^ 8 years ago. At the observed stage ram pressure has no more direct dynamical influence on the galaxy's ISM. We observe the galaxy when a fraction of the stripped gas is falling back onto the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4848 as a Coma SBab galaxy with a 65 kpc ionized-gas tail, vigorous ring star formation, and gas stripping from ram pressure during first cluster passage.

quotes.quote: In a five hour H exposure of the northwest region of the Coma cluster with the 2.1m telescope at San Pedro Martir (Mx), we discovered a 65 kpc cometary emission of ionized gas trailing behind the SBab galaxy NGC 4848. The tail points in the opposite direction of the cluster center, in the same direction where stripped HI had been detected in previous observations. The galaxy shows bright HII regions in an inner ring-like pattern, where the star formation takes place at the prodigious rate of 8.9 M_ yr^ 1 . From the morphologies of the galaxy and the trailing material, we infer that the galaxy is suffering from ram pressure due to its high velocity motion through the intergalactic medium. We estimate that 4 10^9 M_ of gas is swept out from the galaxy forming the tail.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4848 as a perturbed blue Scd disk with high H I deficiency, one-sided northwest radio tails, and RPS/AGN classifications.

quotes.quote: 8 (d): NGC 4848 The optical host is a blue disk Scd that shows perturbed gas distributions with most of the neutral hydrogen in the north and a high H I gas deficiency Gavazzi1989 . The radio emission at both bands, 250--500 MHz and 550--850 MHz, is one-sided with asymmetric tails toward the northwest, extending beyond the optical host galaxy. The 550--850 MHz image shows smaller extent than the 250--500 MHz image. The source is classified as an AGN KentandGunn , and is listed as a RPS galaxy by see Sec. tab:tab3-rps Chenetal . The largest projected linear extent of the source at 250--500 MHz band is 1 23 ( 34.5 kpc).

gmw_00006425.name: 2MASX J10235019-2715232
gmw_00006425.overview: 2MASX J10235019-2715232 is a brightest cluster galaxy embedded within the galaxy cluster ACO 3444 [arXiv:2303.08427]. It is associated with a radio mini-halo and contains a central radio source with a flat spectral index [arXiv:2303.08427].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2MASX J10235019-2715232 as the embedded BCG in ACO 3444, visible with the mini-halo and measured with a flatter spectral index than the diffuse emission.

quotes.quote: Figures fig:A3444_radio_mini_halo a, b show the comparison between the 3GC vs 3GC SRC-SUB images. The embedded BCG (marked S1, optical counterpart 2MASX J10235019-2715232) and mini-halo are clearly visible. Also visible are five compact sources on the eastern edge of the mini-halo that obscure its boundary, identified from NED to be individual infrared sources and indicated in Figure fig:A3444_radio_mini_halo a by small blue crosses.

However, the BCG flux density is significantly higher than that reported in 2019ApJ...880...70G . The BCG has ^ 1.5 GHz _ 1 GHz = 0.61 0.27 . No preferential gradient is evident as much of the region outside the BCG has an index consistent with the integrated spectrum.

gmw_00006426.name: SDSS J111551.90+012955.0
gmw_00006426.overview: SDSS J111551.90+012955.0 is a brightest cluster galaxy [arXiv:2303.08427]. It is a radio galaxy featuring radio lobes oriented in a northwest-southeast direction [arXiv:2303.08427].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J111551.90+012955.0 as the MACS J1115.8+0129 BCG, describes its radio lobes, and measures BCG flux and spectral index alongside the mini-halo.

quotes.quote: The BCG (optical counterpart SDSS J111551.90+012955.0) is shown to have radio lobes orientated in the north-west/south-east direction, which are removed in the SRC-SUB image showing the residual diffuse emission. After such subtraction, the mini-halo is shown to have a similar orientation. We are limited by sensitivity to confirm the full extent of its morphology; however, we note that there appears to be a hint of a radio arm extending from the south, seemingly misaligned with the rest of the mini-halo.

The flux density of the mini-halo is consistent with the missing flux cited by 2020A&A...640A.108G , and the BCG flux density is consistent with their FIRST measurement. The integrated spectra gives ^ 1.5 GHz _ 1 GHz = 1.0 1.1 for the mini-halo and ^ 1.5 GHz _ 1 GHz = 0.78 0.26 for the BCG.

gmw_00006427.name: 2MASX J21401517-2339398
aliases_local: brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) in the cluster MS2137-23
gmw_00006427.overview: 2MASX J21401517-2339398 is the brightest cluster galaxy of the galaxy cluster MS2137-23 [arXiv:2303.08427, arXiv:astro-ph/0207048]. The galaxy is surrounded by a diffuse radio mini-halo that is smoothly oriented in the east-west direction [arXiv:2303.08427].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2MASX J21401517-2339398 as the MACS J2140.2-2339 BCG, subtracts it to reveal a new mini-halo, and reports matching flux and spectral-index measurements.

quotes.quote: Diffuse emission is clearly evident around the BCG (optical counterpart 2MASX J21401517-2339398), and satisfies all the criteria defining a radio mini-halo set out in 2017ApJ...841...71G . Thus, we classify this source as a new mini-halo detection. The BCG and high-redshift galaxy on the western edge CLASH MS2137-2353 830, z = 5.9, 2014ApJ...792...76B are removed after subtraction, providing an uncontaminated view of the mini-halo, which seems to be smoothly orientated in the east-west direction with no AGN jets influencing its morphology.

The measured BCG flux density is consistent with that given in 2018ApJ...853..100Y . The integrated spectra gives ^ 1.5 GHz _ 1 GHz = 1.21 0.36 for the mini-halo, and ^ 1.5 GHz _ 1 GHz = 0.72 0.31 for the BCG. The BCG and western infrared source obscure much of the underlying mini-halo spectral distribution.

sources.original_name: brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) in the cluster MS2137-23
sources.summary: The paper uses spectroscopy, stellar kinematics, and HST photometry of the isolated BCG in MS2137-23 to separate its stellar mass from the cluster dark matter halo.

quotes.quote: We present new spectroscopic observations of the gravitational arcs and the brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) in the cluster MS2137-23 ( z=0.313 ) obtained with the Echelle Spectrograph and Imager on the Keck II telescope. We find that the tangential and radial arcs arise from sources at almost identical redshifts ( z=1.501,1.502 ). We combine the measured stellar velocity dispersion profile of the BCG with a lensing analysis to constrain the distribution of dark and stellar matter in the central 100 kpc of the cluster. Our data indicate a remarkably flat inner slope for the dark matter profile, _d ^ , with <0.9 at 99 CL. Steep inner slopes obtained in cold dark matter cosmological simulations -- such as Navarro Frenk White ( =1 ) or Moore (1.5) universal dark matter profiles -- are ruled out at better than 99 CL. As baryon collapse is likely to have steepened the dark matter profile from its original form, our data provides a powerful test of the cold dark matter paradigm at the cluster mass scale.

HST WFPC2 images of MS2137-23 (GO 5402, PI: Gioia), comprising 10 F702W exposures with a total integration time of 22.2ks, were used to measure the surface photometry of the BCG and to locate arc positions. The exposures were reduced in a standard way, using the iraf package drizzle (Fruchter Hook 2002). The circularized surface brightness profile (in agreement with H97) was obtained using the iraf task ellipse and a fit performed as described in Treu et al. (1998, 2001a) taking into account the HST point-spread function. The best fitting R^ 1/4 parameters are summarized in Table 1.

gmw_00006428.name: Arp 158
gmw_00006428.overview: Arp 158 is an interacting galaxy system that contains abundant molecular gas throughout its structure [arXiv:1107.0969]. The system features tidal tails populated by many young stars, as well as one nucleus dominated by a starburst and another hosting an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1107.0969].

sources.summary: The paper studies star formation across Arp 158 and its tidal debris, finding abundant molecular gas, young tidal-tail stars, and region-dependent Schmidt-Kennicutt behavior.

quotes.quote: To do so we examine the process of star formation in the interacting system Arp 158 and its tidal debris. We perform an analysis of the properties of specific regions of interest in Arp 158 using observations tracing the atomic and the molecular gas, star formation, the stellar populations as well as optical spectroscopy to determine their exact nature and their metallicity. Abundant molecular gas is found throughout the system and the tidal tails appear to have many young stars compared to their old stellar content. One of the nuclei is dominated by a starburst whereas the other is an active nucleus.
Observations of the interacting system Arp 158 provide the first evidence in a resolved fashion that different star--forming regions in a merger may be following different Schmidt--Kennicutt laws.

gmw_00006429.name: J0054 + 7305
gmw_00006429.overview: J0054 + 7305 is a luminous infrared galaxy and a major merger consisting of two interacting galaxies separated by 5.3 kpc [arXiv:1807.04298]. Optical integral field spectroscopy classifies the central region of this system as a composite galaxy [arXiv:1807.04298].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0054 + 7305 as a LIRG major merger with HST-resolved interacting galaxies, GMRT radio continuum, broad absorption, and detected neutral-gas emission.

quotes.quote: J0054 + 7305 This system consists of the luminous infrared galaxy LIRG; sanders1996 , MCG + 12 02 001 sanders2003 . Optical integral field spectroscopy of the central region of this LIRG classifies it as a Composite system alonso2009 . Hubble Space Telescope / Advanced Camera for Surveys (HST/ACS) imaging of this system reveals it to be a major merger, with separation between the two interacting galaxies to be 5.3 kpc kim2013 . We detect resolved radio emission from one of the galaxies in our GMRT 1.4 GHz continuum map. We detect broad and shallow absorption towards the peak of the continuum emission.
